RULE-ALERTAS-001.source_quote: Counts how many criteria in a trilha have esta_alerta == 1
RULE-ALERTAS-001.justification: This rule is a foundational counting primitive with no clinical guideline backing (UNVERIFIABLE verdict); mandatory ratification is required before deciding whether v2 keeps a raw truthy-count primitive or replaces it with the correlation engine's structured criteria model.

RULE-ALERTAS-002.source_quote: Counts beds/movimentacoes by worst manual-pathway alert among
RULE-ALERTAS-002.justification: Aggregate bed/movimentacao alert counting has no clinical citation (UNVERIFIABLE) and only covers 4 of the manual pathways, so whether v2's alert engine keeps this exact VERMELHO-precedence tally or redesigns it around the new correlation engine must be ratified, not assumed.

RULE-ALERTAS-003.source_quote: a value above the yellow threshold but below red is downgraded to neutral
RULE-ALERTAS-003.justification: Status DISCREPANCY forbids plain ADOPT; the count->color mapping's clinical intent (red>=threshold, yellow band otherwise) is sound but its dead-zone bug must be corrected to close the gap between amarelo and vermelho before v2 ports the per-trilha threshold table.

RULE-ALERTAS-004.source_quote: A criterion is 'in alert' iff its value equals exactly 1.
RULE-ALERTAS-004.justification: Verified strict-equality esta_alerta==1 predicate is a simple, unambiguous boolean primitive underlying the count-based alert engine, with no divergence between its two legacy implementations, so it carries forward unchanged into the v2 alert-color mapping.

RULE-ALERTAS-005.source_quote: DEAD CODE: only referenced in commented-out view code
RULE-ALERTAS-005.justification: This bed-level rollup util is confirmed dead code (only reachable from commented-out view code) and functionally duplicated by the active RULE-ALERTAS-006 rollup, so it is retired rather than carried into the v2 alert engine.

RULE-ALERTAS-006.source_quote: an interactive sepse alert of LARANJA (automatic beds) takes top priority
RULE-ALERTAS-006.justification: The clinical severity-priority intent (LARANJA sepsis override beats VERMELHO/AMARELO/NEUTRO among unattended pathways) is sound and must survive into v2, but the legacy bed-type branching (homecare vs automatica ValidationError) is legacy plumbing to be replaced by a unified alert-engine rollup.

RULE-ALERTAS-007.source_quote: Highest-severity alert among an automatic bed's pathways, disregarding whether they were attended.
RULE-ALERTAS-007.justification: Surfacing the worst-severity color regardless of attendance is a useful unattended-escalation signal worth keeping, but its legacy trigger (Leito.save side effect tied to tipo=="automatica") must be replaced by an explicit alert-engine computation independent of bed-type persistence quirks.

RULE-ALERTAS-008.source_quote: Highest-severity alert among a homecare bed's PioraClinica and Sepse pathways, disregarding attendance.
RULE-ALERTAS-008.justification: Mirrors RULE-ALERTAS-007's unattended-escalation intent for homecare beds but hardcodes only two pathway types (PioraClinica, Sepse) with ad hoc NEUTRO stubs for missing records; v2 must generalize this to all monitored pathways in the unified alert engine.

RULE-ALERTAS-009.source_quote: a bed with only NEUTRO pathways is treated as not attended.
RULE-ALERTAS-009.justification: The rule that an all-NEUTRO bed can never be marked attended is a defensible acknowledgement-workflow safeguard, but the automatica/homecare branch duplication and inconsistent list-based re-derivation (vs RULE-ALERTAS-010) should be unified into one acknowledgement predicate in v2.

RULE-ALERTAS-010.source_quote: alertas.sort() then [-1] yields VERMELHO before AMARELO (alphabetical V>A).
RULE-ALERTAS-010.justification: Selecting worst severity via alphabetical sort (VERMELHO>AMARELO) is a fragile mechanism that happens to work only because of Portuguese-language ordering; v2 must adopt an explicit severity-rank comparison while preserving the same escalation semantics.

RULE-ALERTAS-011.source_quote: the system always prefers the 'being assisted' state over the raw alert-level field.
RULE-ALERTAS-011.justification: Prioritizing the assisted/acknowledged state visually over the raw severity color is a good UX pattern worth preserving in v2 screens, but the silent undefined-color fallback when statusKey is empty must be corrected rather than ported as-is.

RULE-ALERTAS-012.source_quote: collects all criterio alert messages from its get_payload()
RULE-ALERTAS-012.justification: This ad hoc string-content builder for RED-alert messaging is superseded by the v2 alert engine's structured alert schema (trigger logic + inputs + evidence per ALERT-<DOMAIN> record) rather than assembled free-text criterio message lists.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: v2 structured alert schema (_work/alerts/<domain>.yaml ALERT-* records)
supersedes_note.lost: ad hoc per-pathway VERMELHO-only free-text criterio message list

RULE-ALERTAS-013.source_quote: every criterio message of a RED pathway is included.
RULE-ALERTAS-013.justification: Like RULE-ALERTAS-012, this automatic-pathway RED-content extractor is superseded by the v2 structured alert schema; unlike the manual variant it applies no whitelist, a divergence in behavior that the new schema resolves by defining explicit alert fields instead of ad hoc lists.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: v2 structured alert schema (_work/alerts/<domain>.yaml ALERT-* records)
supersedes_note.lost: unconditional inclusion of all criterio messages for automatic RED pathways

RULE-ALERTAS-014.source_quote: Risco para SEPSE: realizar abertura do Protocolo Institucional
RULE-ALERTAS-014.justification: A disabled sepsis-protocol prompt message and an automatica-only whitelist filter that can silently drop RED-alert content are clinically consequential and explicitly flagged by the audit as requiring product-owner ratification, not agent-decided disposition.

RULE-ALERTAS-015.justification: This homecare RED-content extractor duplicates RULE-ALERTAS-013's structure with no whitelist filter and is superseded together with its siblings by the v2 structured alert schema, eliminating the need for a homecare-specific content-building variant.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: v2 structured alert schema (_work/alerts/<domain>.yaml ALERT-* records)
supersedes_note.lost: homecare-specific unconditional RED criterio message list

RULE-ALERTAS-016.justification: The intent of suppressing duplicate red-alert notifications while a bed stays critical is exactly the dedup/cooldown behavior the v2 alert schema formalizes via suppression.dedup_key and cooldown, but this legacy implementation is dead code only reachable from a commented-out call site.

RULE-ALERTAS-017.justification: This dual Movimentacao/Leito URL-kwarg-overload resolution strategy is legacy REST plumbing tightly coupled to the old bed-type/trilha model routing table (RULE-ALERTAS-021), which is retired wholesale under the AMH Gold-consumer, new-stack v2 API design.

RULE-ALERTAS-018.source_quote: auto-created with a hardcoded email and password if it does not already exist.
RULE-ALERTAS-018.justification: The always-VERMELHO system observation dispatch and its plaintext-password singleton-user auto-provisioning are legacy notification-transport plumbing that MUST NOT be ported into v2 auth per the audit's explicit security constraint; the v2 alert engine replaces this with proper severity-driven alerts.

RULE-ALERTAS-019.source_quote: using the same system-user provisioning as RULE-ALERTAS-018.
RULE-ALERTAS-019.justification: This bed-level variant shares RULE-ALERTAS-018's hardcoded-VERMELHO and plaintext-password system-user anti-pattern plus tipo-dependent FK branching tied to the legacy bed-type model, so it is retired alongside it rather than adapted.

RULE-ALERTAS-020.source_quote: clearing any prior clinician acknowledgement when the patient no longer meets any criterion.
RULE-ALERTAS-020.justification: Auto-clearing the assistido acknowledgement whenever the recomputed alert returns to NEUTRO is sound clinical workflow, but it is applied inconsistently across legacy trilha models (present in 5 of 9 plus v3, absent in v1 trilha1/2/4/8), so v2 must apply it uniformly across all pathways.

RULE-ALERTAS-021.source_quote: the legacy TrilhaSEPSEModel and TrilhaProfilaxiasModel are commented out (not used) for the same keys.
RULE-ALERTAS-021.justification: This tipo-keyed Django model-routing table is legacy bed-type plumbing mid-migration (v1 models already dead/commented) that has no place in the v2 architecture, which reads unified clinical entities from the AMH Gold layer instead of routing by legacy bed type.

RULE-ALERTAS-022.source_quote: the audit trail of 'who attended' is erased on un-assist, not preserved.
RULE-ALERTAS-022.justification: Recording who acknowledged an alert and when is a sound audit pattern to keep, but clearing assistido_por back to null on un-assist destroys history and conflicts with the platform's immutable audit-trail invariant, so v2 must append audit rows rather than overwrite the mutable field.

RULE-ALERTAS-023.source_quote: explicitly un-marking a trilha (assistido=False) does not create any audit record.
RULE-ALERTAS-023.justification: Snapshotting the full trilha state into an AssistidoPor audit record on acknowledgement is a good audit-trail lesson to keep, but only auditing the True case and skipping un-marking violates the immutable-audit-trail invariant v2 requires before real patient data.

RULE-ALERTAS-024.justification: The five-state PT-BR severity vocabulary (NEUTRO/VERMELHO/AMARELO/LARANJA/ASSISTIDO) and its red>orange>yellow>green triage ordering is a controlled clinical-UI vocabulary worth preserving verbatim, but the internally inconsistent LARANJA hex values must be corrected in the v2 design tokens.

RULE-ALERTAS-025.source_quote: Not a clinical divergence - two independent presentation layers.
RULE-ALERTAS-025.justification: Though flagged AMBIGUOUS, this antd theme semantic-token mapping is explicitly a generic presentation-layer convention with no clinical wiring shown in this partition, so per policy it is retired rather than ratified, to be replaced by the v2 design system's own token set.

RULE-ALERTAS-026.source_quote: the clinical-record lifecycle: draft-saved vs released/signed vs deactivated
RULE-ALERTAS-026.justification: The salvo/liberado/inativo PT-BR record-lifecycle vocabulary is a small controlled status enum with no bug or ambiguity (status OK, verdict NOT_APPLICABLE), so it is carried into v2 clinical-form screens verbatim alongside its icon/color mapping.

RULE-ALERTAS-027.source_quote: classifies the bed VERMELHO if ANY track is VERMELHO, else AMARELO if ANY is AMARELO, else NEUTRO
RULE-ALERTAS-027.justification: The sector-level VERMELHO>AMARELO>NEUTRO bed classification and gender tally is a useful dashboard KPI worth keeping, but its last-truthy-track gender source and silent-drop-on-unknown-key guard are fragile mechanisms that v2's alert-engine dashboard aggregation must replace with explicit, typed rollups.

RULE-ALERTAS-028.source_quote: counts occupied beds bucketed by leito.alerta_nao_assistido
RULE-ALERTAS-028.justification: Counting occupied beds by their attendance-ignoring alert color is a valid unattended-severity sector KPI to preserve, but its unguarded alertas.get(k,0)+1 pattern that silently creates new buckets for unexpected colors (e.g. LARANJA) needs a corrected, exhaustive-enum implementation in v2.

RULE-ALERTAS-029.justification: Counting assisted beds via get_assistido (automatica) or an all-non-False homecare track guard is a reasonable sector 'total_assistidos' KPI intent, but the homecare predicate's silent None-treated-as-not-False leniency is an inconsistency that v2's unified acknowledgement model should close.

RULE-ANTIMICROBIANO-001.justification: The active calcular_alerta_v2 VERMELHO/AMARELO/NEUTRO mapping (vermelho on criterio_4/8, amarelo on criterio_3/5/6) is legacy trilha-model alerting plumbing scoped to only 5 of the 12 stewardship criteria; v2's unified Alert Engine severity scale (normal/watch/urgent/critical) replaces this Django-model-specific enum, and criteria scope must be reconciled against the full stewardship catalog (RULE-ANTIMICROBIANO-003) rather than silently inheriting the legacy 3,4,5,6,8 subset.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: v2 Alert Engine severity scale (normal|watch|urgent|critical) driven by the full 12-criterion stewardship catalog
supersedes_note.lost: legacy VERMELHO/AMARELO/NEUTRO enum restricted to criteria 3,4,5,6,8 and the save()-side assistido reset on NEUTRO

RULE-ANTIMICROBIANO-002.source_quote: criterios == 3 exactly falls through BOTH branches to NEUTRO
RULE-ANTIMICROBIANO-002.justification: Legacy calcular_alerta (criterios 1-14 weighted count) is dead code never called by save() -- TrilhaAntimicrobianoModel uses calcular_alerta_v2 exclusively -- and carries a latent defect where criterios==3 falls through both the <=2 AMARELO and >3 VERMELHO branches to NEUTRO; retiring rather than reviving avoids reintroducing an unreachable, defective severity path into the v2 Alert Engine.

RULE-ANTIMICROBIANO-003.source_quote: Dose-adjustment tables for criterio_3 (weight) and criterio_4 (renal) are external S3 PNG images, not inline values.
RULE-ANTIMICROBIANO-003.justification: The 12-criterion stewardship catalog (antibiotic duration >7d, short-spectrum+internacao>48h, weight/renal dosing, CVC>7d+fever 38.3C, empiric echinocandin for candidemia, culture solicitation, PCR<100 CAP de-escalation) is VERIFIED against IDSA/SSC-2021, but the weight/renal dose tables are external S3 PNG images, not version-controlled, and get_detalhe() surfaces only 5 of 12 criteria -- v2 must migrate dose tables to versioned inline lookups and define authoritative rendering scope while preserving all 12 criteria's clinical content verbatim.
evidence: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline, Pappas et al., Clin Infect Dis 2016;62:e1-e50
IDSA 2009 Intravascular Catheter-Related Infection Guideline, Mermel et al., Clin Infect Dis 2009;49:1-45
Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021, Evans et al., Intensive Care Med 2021;47:1181-1247

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-001.justification: Internal date-range boundary convention has no clinical or authoritative reference to verify against, and the escalation band is UNVERIFIABLE, which mandates RATIFY regardless of how technically sound the logic itself appears.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-002.source_quote: request_body capture (GET vs write methods, JSON fallback)
RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-002.justification: Bespoke Django request-body capture fallback (GET/POST querydict then JSON) is legacy Tasy-app plumbing superseded by the AMH Data Platform's own ingestion and audit capture, which IntensiCare v2 inherits rather than reimplements per ADR-001.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-003.justification: Custom JSON-then-raw-text fallback for capturing HTTP response bodies is Django/Celery-specific legacy plumbing tied to the retired log app, not a clinical rule, and is superseded by AMH platform-native audit ingestion under the new stack.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-004.justification: User-agent-derived device classification feeds only the legacy audit dashboard's device analytics chart, a Django-era admin UI mechanic with no clinical bearing that is retired along with the rest of the legacy log app under the platform change.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-005.source_quote: Log retention / purge threshold (2 weeks, hard delete)
RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-005.justification: The legacy 14-day hard-delete purge directly conflicts with the vision-mandated 7-year TimescaleDB audit retention for LGPD/CFM compliance, so this short-retention mechanism is superseded by the new platform's retention policy rather than carried forward as-is.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: AMH/TimescaleDB 7-year LGPD/CFM audit retention policy (vision fact VIS-7.2-04)
supersedes_note.lost: the storage-cost savings and simplicity of the 14-day short-retention hard-delete window

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-006.source_quote: Default and selectable page size for log list
RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-006.justification: Selectable page-size UI convention (10/20/50/100/200/500, default 50) for the legacy audit-log list is presentation-layer plumbing of the retired Django dashboard with no clinical or architectural carry-forward value.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-007.justification: The 'top 4 most-accessed routes' aggregate is a legacy operational-dashboard chart with an arbitrary display cap, unrelated to clinical domains and retired along with the rest of the audit-log UI under the platform change.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-008.source_quote: 'Problematic routes' selection: one path per status code, top 4
RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-008.justification: The one-path-per-status-code top-4 selection algorithm for the 'problematic routes' chart is legacy dashboard mechanics with a known silent-drop edge case, offering no clinical value and retired with the rest of the log app.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-009.source_quote: 'Undesirable status codes' chart exclude is a no-op (AND instead of OR)
RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-009.justification: The exclude(A,B) AND-not-OR bug makes the 'undesirable status codes' chart filter remove zero rows; since status is DISCREPANCY plain ADOPT is prohibited, and the underlying legacy dashboard chart itself is retired under the platform change rather than fixed.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-010.source_quote: Status-code badge color mapping (get_status_style) — 399 boundary bug
RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-010.justification: The range(100,399) off-by-one causes status 399 to render as 'warning' instead of 'success' in a legacy UI badge helper; being DISCREPANCY it cannot be plain-ADOPTed, and the entire legacy dashboard is retired rather than corrected.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-011.justification: GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/other badge-color mapping is legacy dashboard presentation logic for the retired audit-log UI, with no clinical relevance to carry into the v2 domains or architecture.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-012.source_quote: Device icon mapping (get_icon) missing branches for tablet/email/touch_capable
RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-012.justification: get_icon()'s missing tablet/email/touch_capable branches (producing the literal string 'None' in a class attribute) is a legacy UI completeness bug; DISCREPANCY forbids plain ADOPT, and the whole legacy dashboard icon mechanic is retired, not patched.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-013.source_quote: is_status_code validity check — 599 boundary, unused (dead code)
RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-013.justification: is_status_code() has the same range() off-by-one bug as RULE-010 but is confirmed dead code with zero call sites, so it carries neither clinical value nor architectural lessons and is retired with the legacy app rather than fixed.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-014.source_quote: pretty_json double-encodes string-typed JSON values instead of formatting them
RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-014.justification: pretty_json()'s failure to json.loads() string-typed JSON before re-dumping it garbles log-detail display for that data shape; being DISCREPANCY it cannot be plain-ADOPTed, and the display mechanism itself is retired with the legacy dashboard.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-015.source_quote: Log-detail field rendering rules (skip falsy, JSON/HTML/plain branching)
RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-015.justification: The skip-falsy-fields plus JSON/HTML/plain-text branching used to render the legacy log-detail page is bespoke Django template presentation logic retired wholesale with the legacy audit dashboard under the platform change.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-016.justification: The comprehensive request/response audit payload (user, path, method, status, IP, headers, bodies, device, geo) captures the right audit-trail intent for LGPD's mandatory RIPD and 100%-auditable requirements, but its Django/Celery mechanism is replaced by AMH platform-native audit capture.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-017.justification: Celery apply_async dispatch on a 'trilhas' RabbitMQ exchange is legacy notification/task-transport plumbing explicitly out of scope for IntensiCare's own ingestion stack per ADR-001, which excludes bespoke messaging infra in favor of the AMH platform's mechanisms.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-018.justification: Logging every HTTP response with no exclusion predicate is a sound deny-by-default audit posture worth preserving, but the mechanism (a Django MiddlewareMixin calling a bespoke log_handler) is replaced by the AMH platform's native, unconditional audit capture.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-019.justification: Capturing client IP plus a public/routable flag on every log entry is a useful audit-trail enrichment worth keeping, but the django-ipware-specific mechanism is replaced by whatever IP/network capture the AMH Data Platform provides natively.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-020.justification: GeoIP2 city lookups performed unguarded even on private/non-routable IPs are legacy library-specific plumbing for a dashboard map feature that is retired along with the rest of the legacy audit UI under the platform change.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-021.source_quote: Soft-delete mixin present but not exercised for logs
RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-021.justification: The inherited-but-unexercised soft-delete mixin is dead-weight code specific to the legacy Django model hierarchy; it is not clinically material, and the storage/retention mechanism it decorates is superseded by RULE-005, so the mixin itself is simply dropped.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-022.source_quote: Log dashboard access control (authenticated-only, no staff/ownership check)
RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-022.justification: Any authenticated user, not just staff, can browse every other user's IP, geolocation, and request/response bodies via a /admin/-styled URL that only checks authentication — a genuine deny-by-default access-control question for the new platform that cannot be resolved unilaterally.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-023.source_quote: Default log list ordering (most recent first)
RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-023.justification: Ordering the legacy log list by -criado_em before pagination is a trivial UI convention of the retired audit dashboard, carrying no clinical or architectural significance for the v2 platform.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-024.justification: A copy-paste-and-forget nested EstadoLogView class is confirmed dead code with zero runtime effect; being DISCREPANCY it cannot be plain-ADOPTed, and the entire legacy views module is retired rather than cleaned up.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-025.source_quote: UUID-in-path anonymization is display-only, not applied to route aggregation stats
RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-025.justification: Whether UUID-path anonymization should also apply to route-aggregation stats is an open question confined entirely to the legacy dashboard's chart accuracy, with no clinical stakes, so it is retired along with the dashboard rather than escalated further.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-026.source_quote: LogModel *_tag() HTML-table helper methods are dead code
RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-026.justification: The four *_tag() HTML-table helper methods have zero call sites (no admin.py wires them up) and are confirmed dead code; being DISCREPANCY they cannot be plain-ADOPTed, and they are retired with the rest of the unused legacy model code.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-027.justification: Dropping WSGI-internal keys and allow-listing only serializable value types before persisting request.META is a sound data-hygiene pattern for audit logs worth preserving, even though the specific Django/WSGI key list is replaced under the new platform's own request-capture mechanism.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-028.justification: The '' (empty string) versus NULL sentinel ambiguity for anonymous-request user attribution is a DRF/serializer-version-dependent implementation detail of the legacy log app with no clinical stakes, retired along with the rest of the bespoke logging pipeline.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-029.justification: Fail-fast LogSerializer validation before save (raise_exception=True, no partial/dead-letter handling) is an implementation detail of the legacy DRF/Celery persistence pipeline being retired wholesale under the platform change, not a distinct architectural lesson.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-030.justification: Dead 'origem'/'cpf' whitelist entries that would raise a Django FieldError if ever supplied directly are legacy-app query-parameter debris; being DISCREPANCY they cannot be plain-ADOPTed, and the whole filter mechanism is retired with the legacy dashboard.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-031.source_quote: Geo cascade endpoints use a narrower filter whitelist than the main list
RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-031.justification: The inconsistent omission of 'path' from EstadoLogView/CityLogView's filter whitelist versus the main list view is a legacy dashboard filter-propagation bug; being DISCREPANCY it cannot be plain-ADOPTed, and the whole cascading-filter feature is retired.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-032.justification: The broad '<' plus '>' substring heuristic for HTML detection risks false positives on plain text but only affects how the legacy log-detail page renders a field; it is not clinically material and is retired with the rest of the legacy detail view.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-033.justification: Splitting log exposure into a minimal LogSimpleSerializer (no bodies/headers) versus a full LogSerializer gated behind the detail page is a sound need-to-know data-minimization pattern worth preserving, even as the concrete DRF serializer mechanism is replaced under the new platform's audit-access design.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-034.source_quote: Country -> region -> city cascading filter (city not re-scoped by country)
RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-034.justification: The city-not-rescoped-by-country geolocation cascade assumes globally unique region names, a legacy dashboard dropdown quirk with no clinical bearing that is retired along with GeoIP2 enrichment (RULE-020) and the rest of the legacy dashboard.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-035.source_quote: geolocalizacao field defaults to empty dict, never null in practice
RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-035.justification: The JSONField(default=dict) convention that lets LogDetailView call .get() without a null-check is a narrow Django-ORM implementation detail of the retired GeoIP2 enrichment feature, with no independent architectural value.

RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-036.justification: Skipping django-simple-history snapshots when only a whitelisted set of non-substantive Tasy fields changed is Tasy-ETL-internal wiring (trilha_tasy_header.py) explicitly out of scope for the new AMH-Gold-consuming platform per ADR-001, and is retired with the rest of the Tasy-direct integration.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-001.source_quote: defaulting every entry to False, then flips to True
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-001.justification: UNVERIFIABLE band is mandatory-RATIFY per policy; RBAC catalog-to-payload construction is proprietary internal logic with no published reference, needs product sign-off before it becomes the v2 permission-catalog mechanism.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-002.justification: UNVERIFIABLE band is mandatory-RATIFY; the None-vs-empty-list distinction for empresa-scoped cargo lookup is a proprietary API contract nuance that downstream v2 consumers must explicitly agree to preserve or drop.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-003.source_quote: Grants access only to users flagged as chatbot superusers
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-003.justification: Sound intent (privileged super-admin gate for a bot integration) but the bespoke is_chatbot_superuser flag is legacy Django plumbing; v2 must express the same elevated-access concept through the new IAM-backed role model.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-004.source_quote: Grants access only when the request is associated with a (non-null) partner
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-004.justification: Legacy partner/API-key middleware wiring specific to the old Django partner-auth mechanism; ADR-001 replaces bespoke partner auth with the AMH Data Platform's own IAM/SSO and tenant-scoping model, so this predicate has no v2 home.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-005.source_quote: Object-level access granted only when the requesting user's id equals the object's owner id
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-005.justification: Owner-vs-self object-level authorization is a durable security lesson worth keeping, but the fallback-to-obj.id-when-no-owner mechanism is a legacy shortcut that must be replaced by an explicit ownership field check in v2.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-006.source_quote: Grants access to any user that exists and is not anonymous
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-006.justification: This is a hand-rolled reimplementation of DRF's IsAuthenticated; v2's FastAPI stack has a native authenticated-dependency mechanism, so the bespoke predicate is replaced wholesale rather than adapted.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: FastAPI OAuth2/IAM-backed authenticated-request dependency
supersedes_note.lost: none — semantics (reject null/anonymous user) are fully preserved by the framework mechanism

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-007.source_quote: class names imply differentiated read vs write authorization
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-007.justification: DISCREPANCY forbids plain ADOPT; legacy's LeituraEmpresa/ReadAndWriteEmpresa are byte-identical (both just check authentication), so the reference-correct v2 form must implement genuinely distinct read and write checks per CON-0038 deny-by-default policy.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-008.source_quote: effective permission codenames at a level are the union of permissions granted by access-groups
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-008.justification: The Empresa>Estabelecimento>Setor downward-cascade permission model is the load-bearing multi-tenant RBAC design worth carrying forward, but the Django ORM union-of-querysets mechanism must be re-expressed against the new data platform's identity/authz service.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-009.source_quote: Enforces per-view permission requirements resolved against a configurable scope
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-009.justification: Scope-dispatch RBAC enforcement (empresa/estabelecimento/setor with per-method overrides) is the live authorization mechanism worth preserving conceptually, but the Django-mixin dispatch() pattern must be redesigned as explicit per-route deny-by-default guards per CON-0038.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-010.source_quote: setor (if setor__pk in URL) > estabelecimento (if estabelecimento__pk in URL) > empresa
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-010.justification: The setor>estabelecimento>empresa single-scope resolution priority for GrupoAcesso queryset filtering is a reusable tenancy pattern, but must be reimplemented against the v2 data model rather than Django's objects_without_deleted soft-delete manager.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-011.source_quote: resolves which permission-computation function to call using the same priority order as GrupoAcesso
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-011.justification: Mirrors RULE-010's setor>estabelecimento>empresa dispatch for permission lookup rather than queryset filtering; same tenancy-scope-priority lesson applies and should be unified with RULE-008/010 in a single v2 scope-resolution utility.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-012.source_quote: is added to the response payload for every view action EXCEPT 'list' and 'destroy'
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-012.justification: Purely a Django REST Framework serializer performance optimization (skip nested user serialization on list/destroy) with no security or clinical semantics; v2's API schemas will define field inclusion explicitly per endpoint, making this legacy toggle obsolete.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-013.source_quote: hiding sector memberships from other companies (multi-tenant scoping)
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-013.justification: Filtering a user's sector list to the current empresa before serialization is a genuine multi-tenant data-isolation control worth keeping, but must move from a conditional serializer branch to a mandatory tenant-scoped query in v2's data access layer.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-014.source_quote: only returns users with is_active=True, additionally filtered to users belonging to request.empresa
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-014.justification: Active-user + tenant-scoped queryset filtering is a load-bearing tenant-isolation and account-lifecycle control that must carry forward, reimplemented against the v2 user store rather than Django's ORM filter chain.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-015.source_quote: meaning read operations and partial updates are exempted from this trilha-permission check
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-015.justification: The exceto_metodo (GET/PATCH-bypass) mechanism is a legacy method-exemption shortcut that runs counter to CON-0038/CON-0056's mandate of explicit per-route deny-by-default permission declarations; v2 replaces implicit bypass lists with declared per-method permissions.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: CON-0038/CON-0056 explicit per-route, per-method permission declarations (deny-by-default)
supersedes_note.lost: implicit method-exemption list (exceto_metodo); every method now requires an explicit declared permission, including GET/PATCH

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-016.source_quote: only users holding that permission may reject or close the protocol
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-016.justification: Gating sepsis-protocol rejection/closure behind a dedicated can_recusar_protocolo_sepse permission is a clinically load-bearing safeguard (prevents unauthorized protocol dismissal) that must be reimplemented in the v2 sepsis domain's authorization layer.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-017.justification: The distinct can_view_ocupacoes (navigation) vs can_view_auditoria (audit-button) permission split is a meaningful least-privilege UI pattern worth preserving in the new design system's sector dashboard, even though the legacy React component itself is being rebuilt.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-018.source_quote: shown only if the current user holds at least one of the administrative permissions
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-018.justification: Purely cosmetic legacy UI mechanics (a gear-icon visibility OR-of-three-permissions check) tied to the old React header component; the v2 design system's navigation shell will derive icon visibility fresh from the new permission model, not this specific component logic.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-019.source_quote: renders as a plain, non-clickable tag when the signed-in user has access to one or zero companies
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-019.justification: Legacy header-component UI mechanics for the company-switcher dropdown with no security or clinical consequence; the v2 design system will define its own company-switcher affordance independent of this specific TSX conditional-rendering logic.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-020.source_quote: the `!token` half of the guard condition can never be true
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-020.justification: DISCREPANCY forbids plain ADOPT; this is the cluster's single highest-impact finding — a dead-code token check lets 19+ pages render with zero server-side auth — and is exactly the failure mode CON-0038/CON-0056 mandate fixing via deny-by-default per-route server-side gates.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: CON-0038/CON-0056 mandatory server-side deny-by-default route guard ahead of shell render
supersedes_note.lost: legacy default-allow validateRoute(ctx) behavior and its dead token-presence branch — both intentionally discarded as a critical security defect, not carried forward

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-021.justification: DISCREPANCY forbids plain ADOPT; reusing can_manage_grupo_acesso to gate the unrelated Leitos (beds) page is an unresolved copy-paste-or-intentional ambiguity tied to a specific legacy page route that will not exist in the v2 design, so it is dropped rather than corrected.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-022.source_quote: the restriction is UI-only and can be bypassed by direct URL navigation
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-022.justification: DISCREPANCY forbids plain ADOPT; the intended semantics (analítico/inconsistências features are automatica-tenant-only) are worth keeping, but v2 must enforce the tipo check server-side on the destination route, not merely hide a button client-side.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-023.source_quote: the user is redirected to /empresa instead of seeing the login form
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-023.justification: Redirecting an already-authenticated user away from the login screen is a correct, low-risk UX affordance worth preserving in v2's auth flow, reimplemented against the new session/token mechanism rather than the raw trilhas.token cookie check.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-024.source_quote: zero companies is treated as an error and forces logout
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-024.justification: The 0/1/>1-empresas post-login routing decision tree is a load-bearing cluster fact (CLU-AUTH-USUARIOS-06) with sound business logic that v2 must reproduce, adapted to the new token-refresh and company-selector UI rather than the legacy AuthContext implementation.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-025.source_quote: only if the profile belongs to themself, or if they hold the can_manage_usuario permission
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-025.justification: The self-or-can_manage_usuario object-level access rule for professional profiles is sound intent, but it is enforced only client-side in legacy (server route has no permission check); v2 must move this check server-side per CON-0038's deny-by-default mandate.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-026.source_quote: only rendered when the viewer has can_manage_usuario permission AND the current company's tipo
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-026.justification: Gating the 'Gestão de Pacientes' tab on can_manage_usuario AND tenant tipo==homecare is a genuine tenant-type feature-flag pattern worth carrying into v2's clinical-forms screens, verbatim on the homecare vocabulary term, though the client-side-only enforcement must be hardened server-side.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-027.source_quote: shows/hides six navigation items strictly based on three permission flags
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-027.justification: The can_manage_empresa/can_manage_grupo_acesso/can_manage_usuario-to-menu-item mapping is a useful reference model for v2's admin navigation, but the legacy sidebar hook itself is being rebuilt in the new design system so only the permission-to-feature mapping carries forward.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-028.source_quote: additionally requires the global can_manage_evolucao permission
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-028.justification: Per-discipline can_preencher_formulario_<role> visibility plus a global can_manage_evolucao gate for submission is a real least-privilege clinical-forms control; the ten PT-BR professional roles (médico, enfermeiro, técnico de enfermagem, fisioterapeuta, musicoterapeuta, nutricionista, psicólogo, farmacêutico, fonoaudiólogo, intercorrência) must be preserved verbatim in v2's role-based authoring model.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-029.source_quote: the system first attempts Django local authentication (username+password) before trying any external identity provider
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-029.justification: Local-Django-auth-first login cascade with a non-standard HTTP 202 success status is a legacy Django-specific mechanism; ADR-001's security model mandates IAM Identity Center SSO as the authentication path, replacing this local-then-SSO ordering entirely.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: IAM Identity Center SSO (ADR-001 security model)
supersedes_note.lost: local Django-auth-first fallback ordering and the non-standard HTTP 202 (Accepted) success status; v2 login responses use standard 200-series codes

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-030.source_quote: the user is treated as authenticated: a local Usuario is looked up by email=username, and auto-created if missing
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-030.justification: DISCREPANCY forbids plain ADOPT; auto-provisioning any user who authenticates against the shared americashealth SSO with no Trilhas-entitlement check (an unresolved TODO) is a known security gap that ADR-001's IAM Identity Center SSO must not reproduce.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: IAM Identity Center SSO with explicit per-tenant entitlement checks (ADR-001)
supersedes_note.lost: auto-provisioning of a local account without any project-entitlement verification — intentionally not carried forward

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-031.source_quote: if the stored password is not already a recognized hash, it is hashed
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-031.justification: The defensive auto-hash-on-save safeguard exists only because legacy stores local passwords in the app database; ADR-001's IAM Identity Center centralizes credential management, so v2 has no local password field for this safeguard to protect.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: IAM Identity Center centralized credential management (ADR-001)
supersedes_note.lost: local plaintext-detection-and-hash-on-save behavior; no longer applicable since v2 stores no local password

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-032.source_quote: removes that company's access-group memberships and drops
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-032.justification: The invariant that unlinking a user from a company must cascade-remove that company's access-group memberships (preventing stale cross-tenant permissions) is a security-relevant lesson worth keeping, reimplemented against v2's tenancy model rather than the legacy 'u' access-code flag and Django signals.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-033.source_quote: Assigning a user to an establishment auto-creates the parent company membership
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-033.justification: Django FK-save-triggered membership cascade (estabelecimento->empresa) is legacy tenancy wiring; ADR-001 Implicacao 7 mandates inheriting Lake Formation ABAC + IAM Identity Center for access, so the cascade mechanism itself is replaced, not ported.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: AWS Lake Formation ABAC tag propagation + IAM Identity Center group hierarchy (ADR-001 Implicacao 7)
supersedes_note.lost: automatic Django-model save/post_delete cascade that auto-creates/cleans parent-level memberships; v2 must replicate equivalent hierarchical membership sync explicitly

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-034.source_quote: Assigning a user to a sector auto-creates the parent establishment membership
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-034.justification: Same cascade pattern as RULE-033 one level down (setor->estabelecimento); legacy Django tenancy wiring superseded by ADR-001's inherited Lake Formation ABAC + IAM Identity Center group model.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: AWS Lake Formation ABAC tag propagation + IAM Identity Center group hierarchy (ADR-001 Implicacao 7)
supersedes_note.lost: post_delete cascade cleanup of group memberships scoped to setor; v2 must define equivalent cleanup semantics in the new access model

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-035.source_quote: the group's Django permission set is fully REPLACED (not merged) with only the permissions
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-035.justification: Django ModelSerializer M2M replace-all permission assignment is legacy plumbing; new platform assigns access via Lake Formation ABAC policies (ADR-001 Implicacao 7), which uses a different attribute-based mechanism, not per-group Django Permission replace-all.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: Lake Formation ABAC policy assignment (ADR-001 Implicacao 7)
supersedes_note.lost: replace-all-on-truthy-dict semantics, including the quirk that unrecognized codenames are silently dropped

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-036.source_quote: an empty list DOES clear membership (unlike the permissions branch)
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-036.justification: Group membership replace-all-via-.set() with an inconsistent empty-list-clears-vs-empty-dict-does-not-clear asymmetry is a legacy Django serializer quirk that should not be carried into the ABAC-based membership model of the new platform.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: Lake Formation ABAC group membership management (ADR-001 Implicacao 7)
supersedes_note.lost: asymmetric empty-list-clears-membership behavior; v2 must define one consistent clear-vs-no-op semantic for both membership and permission edits

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-037.source_quote: returns the entire system permission catalog (codename->label) unconditionally to any authenticated user
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-037.justification: Unscoped full-permission-catalog exposure to any authenticated caller is a legacy over-exposure anti-pattern; ADR-001's Lake Formation ABAC model scopes data/action visibility per principal, so this unconditional endpoint has no place in the rebuilt platform.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-038.source_quote: the user is bulk-linked to every listed setor (UsuarioSetor) and to every distinct parent Estabelecimento
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-038.justification: Bulk tenancy-linking logic on user creation (empresa/setor/estabelecimento bulk_create) is Django-ORM-specific tenancy wiring; the new platform's identity/access provisioning is driven by IAM Identity Center + Lake Formation ABAC per ADR-001, not by application-level bulk FK creation.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: IAM Identity Center provisioning + Lake Formation ABAC tag assignment on user create (ADR-001 Implicacao 7)
supersedes_note.lost: bulk_create-based sector/establishment auto-linking; v2 must define an equivalent provisioning workflow

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-039.source_quote: the removal query is additionally scoped to setor__estabelecimento__empresa=request.empresa
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-039.justification: Diff-based setor sync scoped to current empresa is Django-ORM tenancy wiring for a multi-tenant model the new platform will not replicate as-is; ADR-001 mandates ABAC-based scoping via Lake Formation instead of application-level scoped delete/create diffs.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: Lake Formation ABAC scoped access management (ADR-001 Implicacao 7)
supersedes_note.lost: symmetric-difference sync logic that preserves other-empresa setores unaffected; v2 must reproduce the safety property of never touching out-of-scope memberships

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-040.source_quote: Deleting a user does not remove the record; it sets is_active=False and returns 204
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-040.justification: Soft-delete-via-flag preserving the record and its FK relations is a sound audit-retention pattern worth keeping for a clinical/regulated system even though the specific Django is_active mechanism will be reimplemented against the new identity/access stack.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-041.source_quote: the auth token cookie ("trilhas.token") is set with a 30-day lifetime
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-041.justification: Custom cookie-based session/token verify-refresh workflow with a 30-day lifetime is legacy app plumbing; ADR-001 Implicacao 7 mandates inheriting IAM Identity Center for SSO, which owns session/token lifecycle instead of an app-managed cookie scheme.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: IAM Identity Center SSO session management (ADR-001 Implicacao 7)
supersedes_note.lost: bespoke double-cookie (token/permissions) verify-then-refresh flow, including its double-notification failure-path defect; not to be reintroduced

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-042.source_quote: any permission not present in the system catalog is silently excluded from the effective map
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-042.justification: Client-side intersection of allPermissions x userPermissions to compute an effective-permission map is a frontend RBAC workaround; under ADR-001 the authoritative effective-access decision is made server-side via Lake Formation ABAC, not recomputed in the browser.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: server-side Lake Formation ABAC decision, returned to the client rather than recomputed (ADR-001 Implicacao 7)
supersedes_note.lost: the deny-unknown-permission safety property (unknown codenames never surface as true) should be preserved as an explicit invariant in the new authorization service

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-043.source_quote: Refuses to create a new API key when an API key with the same name already exists
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-043.justification: A Django management-command CLI for generating uniquely-named API keys is legacy operational tooling; ADR-001 Implicacao 7 places credential/secret issuance under the inherited IAM Identity Center model, obsoleting this bespoke command.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-044.source_quote: 6 extra codenames that have NO corresponding entry in the backend's GrupoAcesso.cargo choices
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-044.justification: The cargo (job-role) enumeration is a controlled clinical-staffing vocabulary asset worth keeping verbatim in PT-BR, but the frontend's cargosObject superset (medico, terapeuta, tecnico_enfermagem, enfermagem, musicoterapeuta, intercorrencia) is not backend-valid, so v2 must adopt only the backend's canonical 15-value GrupoAcesso.cargo list as the reference-correct form, cited per ESC-P3-188.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-045.source_quote: though no code in either partition mutates/enforces what the 'monitor' access level actually grants
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-045.justification: The 'm' (monitor) access code is defined and labeled on the backend but never mutated or enforced by any in-scope code path, so whether v2 should carry a distinct monitor access level (and what it should grant) cannot be decided from the catalog alone and requires a product/security ratification per ESC-AMBIGUOUS-345.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-046.source_quote: CRM(medicine), COREN(nursing), CREFITO(physio/OT), CRN(nutrition), CRFA(speech), CRP(psychology), AGMT
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-046.justification: The 7-value professional-council (conselho) codeset plus 27-value estado_conselho enumeration is a controlled clinical-credentialing vocabulary with no functional divergence between backend and frontend (only cosmetic label wording), so it carries forward verbatim as a credentialing form asset.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-047.source_quote: the owning Permissao model is commented out
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-047.justification: The legacy nivel_acesso (r/rw) and coarse admin/medico/enfermeiro access enum belongs to a Permissao model that is fully commented out in the legacy codebase, i.e. dormant dead code superseded even within the legacy stack by Django group permissions, so per the AMBIGUOUS-band policy it is not worth ratifying for v2.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-048.source_quote: must be attached to exactly one of empresa, estabelecimento, or setor
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-048.justification: The exactly-one-scope-level invariant for an access group is a sound authorization design constraint worth preserving under the new ABAC model even though the Django three_xor validation mechanism itself will not be ported.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-049.source_quote: reject requests lacking a resolved company context with 404
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-049.justification: Deny-by-default rejection of requests lacking a resolved tenant (empresa) context is exactly the kind of deny-by-default authorization lesson worth carrying into the new platform's security design, independent of the Django mixin implementation.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-050.source_quote: it is renamed to 'assign_permissions' before internal validation
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-050.justification: The permissoes-to-assign_permissions payload key rename is a legacy DRF-serializer input-shaping quirk tied to a specific field-name history; the new API's schema will not need this compatibility rename.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-051.justification: The security invariant that a user must already belong to a group's empresa/estabelecimento/setor scope before joining that group is sound and worth keeping, but the reference-correct form must fix the *kwargs-instead-of-**kwargs typo (super().save(**kwargs)) rather than porting the latent defect, per ESC-P3-148.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-052.source_quote: LoginViewSet.create() never instantiates/validates the serializer - it reads request.data.get
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-052.justification: The intent (require username/password on login) is sound but the legacy implementation never actually invokes the declared LoginSerializer validation, so the reference-correct form for v2 must actually enforce required-field validation on the login endpoint rather than porting the bypass, per ESC-P3-149.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-053.source_quote: meaning a client cannot use this endpoint to remove ALL of a user's access groups by sending an empty array
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-053.justification: The field-rename shaping (setores/foto_perfil/grupos_acessos -> *_id/_b64/_ids) with its truthy-check bug that makes grupos_acessos=[] silently unreachable is a bespoke legacy request-shaping quirk tied to this exact payload schema, which the new API will not reuse, per ESC-P3-150.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-054.source_quote: Computes two check digits from the first 9 digits with descending weights and modulo-11
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-054.justification: The CPF check-digit validator implements the standard published Brazilian CPF algorithm (weights 10..2 then 11..2, modulo 11) and was cross-verified as matching the reference algorithm, so it carries forward as-is for identity validation in v2.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-055.source_quote: the username field is trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace and lower-cased
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-055.justification: Trimming and lower-casing the username before login submission is a small, sound input-normalization practice with no discrepancy, worth carrying into the new login flow as-is.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-056.justification: The exceto_metodo tuple's PATCH-misspelled-as-PATH is a plain typo in legacy viewset configuration, not a clinical or architectural asset worth ratifying; the new API's method-exception configuration will be written correctly from scratch, per ESC-AMBIGUOUS-317.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-057.source_quote: formatted as the literal string "JWT" followed by a space and the raw token value
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-057.justification: The custom 'JWT <token>' Authorization header scheme is legacy app-specific auth plumbing; ADR-001 Implicacao 7 mandates inheriting IAM Identity Center for SSO, which issues its own OIDC/OAuth2 bearer-token scheme rather than this bespoke header format.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: IAM Identity Center-issued OIDC/OAuth2 bearer tokens (ADR-001 Implicacao 7)
supersedes_note.lost: the bespoke 'JWT ' header prefix and the explicit header-clear-before-login step; not needed once SSO owns token issuance

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-058.source_quote: a closed catalog of 28 permission flags spanning patient/occupancy management
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-058.justification: The closed 28-flag permission catalog (patient/occupancy mgmt, camera, audit, per-discipline evolution-form authoring, sepsis-protocol refusal, etc.) documents a valuable capability taxonomy worth preserving as the seed for the new platform's ABAC action-tag catalog, even though its Django-Permission-backed mechanism is superseded.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-059.source_quote: modeled as a named bundle of the entire Permissions boolean map plus a list of member users
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-059.justification: The frontend Grupo type (named bundle of the full permission map plus untyped member list) is a thin plumbing mirror of the backend Django Group model; the new platform's ABAC groups (ADR-001 Implicacao 7) replace this representation rather than porting it.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: Lake Formation ABAC group/tag model (ADR-001 Implicacao 7)
supersedes_note.lost: the untyped 'usuarios: any[]' membership list should be replaced by a properly typed member reference in v2

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-060.source_quote: the identical three-value literal union already used for bed monitoring modality (manual / automatica / homecare)
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-060.justification: The Empresa.tipo enum (manual/automatica/homecare) is functionally sound and unaligned-yet-consistent with LeitoTipo, but its independent redeclaration is a known duplication-drift risk the notes flag explicitly, so v2 should adapt it to a single shared source-of-truth enum rather than re-duplicating it.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-061.source_quote: explicitly excluding "leito" (bed) as a switchable destination
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-061.justification: The organizational context-switcher's closed 3-value destination set (empresa/estabelecimento/setor, no leito) is legacy frontend UI navigation mechanics of low significance, captured only at low confidence, and will be redesigned from scratch as part of the new UI.

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-062.source_quote: Both the original token and its refresh token share the same 30-day validity window
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-062.justification: The 30-day JWT access-and-refresh-token lifetime is legacy Django-JWT settings plumbing already flagged in its own notes as too long-lived for a clinical system; ADR-001 Implicacao 7 places session/token lifecycle under the inherited IAM Identity Center SSO, whose own (shorter) session policies replace this configuration.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: IAM Identity Center session/token policy (ADR-001 Implicacao 7)
supersedes_note.lost: the flat 30-day access==refresh token lifetime; v2 should adopt materially shorter access-token lifetimes as the legacy notes themselves recommend

RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-063.source_quote: any user whose PIN was never rotated legally signs medical documents/prescriptions with an org-wide shared default secret
RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-063.justification: A deployment-wide shared default e-signature PIN that lets un-rotated users sign medical documents/prescriptions with a non-unique secret is a HIGH-priority security/compliance defect (defeats per-user attribution of clinical e-signatures) that cannot be resolved by an agent and must go to security/compliance ratification, per ESC-ADDENDUM-350.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-001.source_quote: Cumulative fluid balance across ALL BalancoHidrico days for the same bed and admission
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-001.justification: Cumulative balance is a pure ledger sum with no clinical scale attached; verdict VERIFIED against the ADQI/Malbrain standard definition (intake minus output), status OK, no escalation, and the v2 fluid-balance domain doc needs exactly this aggregate to trend volume status across the whole admission, not per shift.
evidence: Malbrain MLNG et al. Anaesthesiol Intensive Ther 2014

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-002.source_quote: Total fluid intake for this balance day = sum of non-deleted entrada quantities.
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-002.justification: Elementary intake aggregation with verdict VERIFIED against the ADQI/Malbrain daily-gains definition; status OK and no escalation band, so it carries forward unchanged as the v2 domain's per-day intake total feeding the cumulative and shift-level balances.
evidence: Malbrain MLNG et al. Ann Intensive Care 2018;8:66

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-003.source_quote: Total fluid output for this balance day = sum of non-deleted saida quantities.
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-003.justification: Mirrors RULE-002 for output volumes; verdict VERIFIED against the same ADQI/Malbrain daily-losses definition, status OK, no escalation, so the daily output total is adopted unchanged as the counterpart aggregate for hemodynamics volume-status tracking.
evidence: Malbrain MLNG et al. Ann Intensive Care 2018;8:66

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-004.source_quote: Day-shift fluid balance = (intake - output) for entries/outputs created between 07:00 and 19:00
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-004.justification: Escalation band UNVERIFIABLE mandates ratification: the intake-minus-output arithmetic is sound but the hard-coded 07:00-19:00 day-shift split is an institutional nursing convention with no Sepsis-3/KDIGO/ASPEN basis, so whether v2 keeps a fixed day/night split or makes it facility-configurable needs a human ruling before design.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-005.source_quote: Night-shift fluid balance = stored 24h balance minus computed day-shift balance.
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-005.justification: Escalation band UNVERIFIABLE mandates ratification: the day+night=24h subtraction identity is arithmetically valid, but it depends on the same unratified 07:00-19:00/19:00-07:00 shift-split convention from RULE-004 and on a stored balanco_24h field with its own accumulator risk (RULE-014/015), so the night-shift figure cannot be adopted independently.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-006.source_quote: Window construction defect (shared across BE-09 helpers): criado_em__day matches day-of-month
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-006.justification: Status DISCREPANCY with escalation band P1 mandates ratification even though the fix is well understood: the 07:00-07:00 nursing-day window uses a month-agnostic __day filter that breaks across month boundaries and an unsatisfiable second-queryset predicate that silently drops 00:00-07:00 data, and this helper feeds sepsis-adjacent volume totals, so it needs sign-off before the v2 correction is finalized.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-007.source_quote: Sum of all fluid-intake quantities (Entrada.quantidade, no type filter) for an attendance
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-007.justification: Status DISCREPANCY with escalation band P1 mandates ratification: this shares the exact month-agnostic __day window defect and unsatisfiable else-branch as RULE-006, plus its own 0-sum-coerced-to-None quirk, and it is one of the load-bearing 24h ganhos totals consumed by the evolution form (RULE-012), so the corrected windowing must be ratified before adoption.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-008.source_quote: Sum of urine-output quantities (Saida with tipo in {diurese_espontanea, diurese_sonda})
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-008.justification: Status DISCREPANCY with escalation band P1 mandates ratification: urine-output totals over the buggy 07:00-07:00 window directly underpin the KDIGO 2012 AKI oliguria criterion (<0.5 mL/kg/h), so the month-agnostic window bug and dropped 00:00-07:00 data have real AKI-detection consequences and must be ratified, not silently auto-corrected.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-009.source_quote: Same window defect as RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-006. Evacuacoes filter tipo=='evacuacao'
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-009.justification: Status DISCREPANCY forbids plain ADOPT; clinical_impact is low and the escalation band is only P2, so unlike its P1 siblings this can be corrected directly: keep the intent (count/volume of bowel-movement output over the 24h nursing day) but replace the month-agnostic __day window and both-inclusive boundary-hour double-count with the corrected temporal windowing mandated by ledger constraint CLU-BALANCO-HIDRICO-C-01.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-010.source_quote: Highest recorded body temperature (SinaisVitais.temperatura) for an attendance over the 07:00-07:00
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-010.justification: Status DISCREPANCY with escalation band P1 mandates ratification: Tmax over the buggy nursing-day window feeds fever-threshold downstream logic (SCCM/IDSA new-fever evaluation) and the same month-agnostic window and dropped-data defects apply, so the corrected 24h-max windowing needs sign-off before it becomes the v2 vital-signs rollup.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-011.source_quote: Highest recorded capillary glucose (HGT = hemoglicoteste, SinaisVitais.hgt) for an attendance
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-011.justification: Status DISCREPANCY forbids plain ADOPT; the escalation band is P2 (low clinical impact per the catalog, no scored calculator dependency), so the max-HGT-over-nursing-day intent is preserved while the shared month-agnostic window bug and None-strip defect are replaced by the corrected windowing from ledger constraint CLU-BALANCO-HIDRICO-C-01.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-012.source_quote: the ACTIVE computation lives in trilha_homecare.utils
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-012.justification: Escalation band UNVERIFIABLE mandates ratification: the evolution form's commented-out intended 24h formula (gains minus diuresis-plus-vomit) conflicts with the entrada/saida ledger definition used everywhere else (RULE-006), and the actual active implementation lives outside this partition in trilha_homecare/utils.py, so the authoritative 24h fluid-balance formula for v2 cannot be decided without opening that file and a ruling.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-013.source_quote: Bucket [22,00] becomes range [22:00:00, 00:00:00]; since 22:00 > 00:00 this SQL BETWEEN is empty
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-013.justification: Status DISCREPANCY with escalation band P1 mandates ratification: the 2-hour overview grid's 22:00-00:00 bucket is an inverted, always-empty SQL range that silently loses late-evening fluid data on every rendered balanco PDF, so the bucketing redesign (and whether v2 keeps 2-hour granularity at all) needs sign-off before implementation.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-014.source_quote: Creating an Entrada (intake) record incrementally adds its quantidade to the parent
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-014.justification: Verdict VERIFIED confirms the sign convention (intake increases the running balance) matches the standard nursing I&O rule, but ledger constraint CLU-BALANCO-HIDRICO-C-05 flags the underlying mechanism as unsound: a mutable incremental accumulator never corrected when quantidade is edited, so v2 must keep the clinical semantics while replacing the accumulator with a recompute-from-source-rows pattern.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-015.source_quote: Creating a Saida (output) record incrementally subtracts its quantidade from the parent
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-015.justification: Mirrors RULE-014 for output records: verdict VERIFIED on the sign convention (output decreases the running balance) but the same ledger constraint CLU-BALANCO-HIDRICO-C-05 against mutable incremental accumulators applies, so v2 keeps the clinical semantics and replaces the accumulate-on-create/never-correct-on-edit mechanism with recomputation from source rows.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-016.source_quote: Uses timedelta.seconds (the seconds-within-a-day component, 0..86399), NOT total_seconds()
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-016.justification: Status DISCREPANCY with escalation band P1 mandates ratification: the shared elapsed-hours helper uses timedelta.seconds instead of total_seconds(), wrapping into [0,24) for any record over a day old, which silently defeats the sepsis recency window guards (<=24h, <=12h, <2h) consumed cross-cluster by multiple sepsis criteria, so the fix must be ratified given its blast radius outside this cluster.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-017.source_quote: Returns the SECOND most recent vital-signs record for an admission (the reading before the current
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-017.justification: Escalation band UNVERIFIABLE mandates ratification: this internal data-access helper for the previous vital-signs reading baseline does not filter soft-deleted rows, so a deleted reading can silently become the sepsis change-detection baseline for criterio_3/criterio_7, and there is no published clinical reference to check the index[1]-of-order_by convention against, so it needs a ruling on excluding deletado_em rows in v2.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-018.source_quote: Composes the arterial blood pressure string from systolic (pas) and diastolic (pad) fields
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-018.justification: Escalation band UNVERIFIABLE mandates ratification: this pure display-formatting rule treats a pressure of exactly 0 as missing (shown as dashes) and hides the entire Pressao Arterial row when both sides are falsy, while a sibling PDF surface (RULE-028, pdf_medico.html) applies a stricter both-present gate, so v2 needs a ruling on one canonical zero/missing-value display convention across surfaces instead of preserving the cross-surface drift.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-019.source_quote: 0-10 range is consistent with the Numeric Rating Scale; the 3-12 range is consistent with the Behavioral
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-019.justification: Catalog status is AMBIGUOUS, not OK, so a plain ADOPT is not permitted even though verdict is VERIFIED; the escalation P3 review confirms the enforced 0-10 and 3-12 bounds are exactly canonical for NRS and BPS respectively, so the reference-correct form for v2 explicitly labels the two branches as Numeric Rating Scale (verbal complaint) and Behavioral Pain Scale (observed signs) instead of leaving the scale identity implicit in code.
evidence: Payen et al. Crit Care Med 2001;29(12):2258-2263

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-020.source_quote: the system defaults the volume to 200 (presumably mL)
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-020.justification: Escalation band UNVERIFIABLE mandates ratification: the hard-coded 200 mL default for an unmeasured enteral-diet intake entry is an internal charting convenience with no ASPEN or other enteral-nutrition guideline basis, so v2 needs a ruling on whether to keep a numeric default at all or force an explicit measured volume, since silently charting a fabricated 200 mL affects the cumulative balance.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-021.source_quote: spontaneous presence), the quantity is defaulted to 200
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-021.justification: Escalation band UNVERIFIABLE mandates ratification: this default-200-mL-on-presente rule unconditionally overwrites any client-supplied quantidade whenever presenca_espontanea equals presente, which is a more aggressive override than the enteral-diet default in RULE-020 and has no clinical guideline basis, so v2 needs a ruling on whether overwriting user-entered volumes is acceptable at all.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-022.source_quote: Because of the precedence bug, any record with tipo
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-022.justification: Status DISCREPANCY forbids plain ADOPT, but clinical_impact is none and the escalation band is only P3, so the semiquantitative presente+/++/+++ to 100/200/300 mL mapping intent is preserved while the operator-precedence bug is fixed to the evident intent, presenca and (tipo evacuacao or tipo vomito_retorno_sonda), so a vomito record no longer bypasses the presenca gate and an evacuacao record with falsy presenca is still excluded.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-023.source_quote: This encodes a 24h clinical-day boundary starting at 07:00 rather than midnight
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-023.justification: Escalation band UNVERIFIABLE mandates ratification: this default-day-parameter cutoff is the same unratified 07:00 nursing-day convention as RULE-004, has no authoritative clinical source, and relies on server-local timezone via astimezone() with no explicit tz, so v2 needs a single ruling shared with RULE-024/027 on whether the boundary is fixed, explicit-timezone, and facility-configurable.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-024.source_quote: The fluid-balance (balanço hídrico) page always computes/displays balances using a fixed 24-hour
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-024.justification: Escalation band UNVERIFIABLE mandates ratification: the frontend hard-codes the same 07:00-07:00 turno window regardless of facility-specific shift configuration, is not configurable from this page, and mirrors the unratified backend cutoff in RULE-023, so a single cross-surface ruling on the nursing-day boundary (fixed vs facility-configurable) is required before v2 design.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-025.source_quote: GridView shows a cell when value !== 0 (so negative values ARE shown), while the MobileView shows
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-025.justification: Status DISCREPANCY forbids plain ADOPT; this is legacy desktop/mobile UI display-mechanics drift (GridView vs MobileView visibility predicates) with clinical_impact null and no clinical scale involved, so under the platform change to a new AMH Gold consumer frontend it is retired rather than ported, and v2's dashboard component defines one canonical zero/negative-value visibility rule instead.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-026.source_quote: company-scoped permission OR is the original author, AND the record has not already been soft-deleted
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-026.justification: Status DISCREPANCY forbids plain ADOPT, but the underlying deny-by-default authorization pattern (permission-or-author AND not-already-deleted) is a lesson worth keeping per policy, so v2 adapts it into the shared security/LGPD authorization layer while fixing the divergence where Entrada's missing-empresa case is caught with a clear ValidationError but Saida/SinaisVitais instead raise an unhandled AttributeError.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-027.source_quote: an entry timed before 07:00 on the current calendar day belongs to the PREVIOUS day
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-027.justification: Status OK with verdict NOT_APPLICABLE and no escalation band permits plain ADOPT: the pre-07:00-reassigns-to-previous-day mechanism is a sound, uncontested implementation detail of whatever nursing-day boundary value is ultimately ratified in RULE-004/023/024, so it is adopted now with the caveat that its 07:00 constant must track the outcome of those ratifications.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-028.source_quote: each 24h indicator row (diurese, ganhos, balanco, temperatura max, HGT) is CONDITIONED on the truthiness
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-028.justification: Escalation band UNVERIFIABLE mandates ratification: the medical-evolution PDF gates each 24h indicator row on formulario_data.sinais_vitais.<field> but prints the value from formulario_data.indicadores_24h.<field>, a namespace mismatch that can hide a row with real data or show a stale one, and the serializer that builds this context is out of partition, so v2 needs a ruling on the single source of truth before the report template is rebuilt.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-029.source_quote: A fluid intake ("entrada") record selects one of 8 types; each type conditionally reveals type-specific
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-029.justification: Status OK, verdict NOT_APPLICABLE, no escalation: this is a controlled clinical-forms asset (the 8-type fluid-intake decision tree with its per-type required fields) rather than a clinical calculation, so it is adopted verbatim into the v2 clinical-forms design doc, preserving the PT-BR type vocabulary and the sub-vocabularies referenced in RULE-055..058.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-030.source_quote: For an oral-diet intake, acceptance level determines whether a volume is required.
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-030.justification: Status OK, verdict NOT_APPLICABLE, no escalation: the aceitou/reduzida/recusou branching, with recusou intentionally collecting no volume (implicit zero), is a straightforward form-vocabulary asset, so it is adopted verbatim into the v2 clinical-forms design doc as the oral-diet sub-form of RULE-029.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-031.source_quote: A fluid output ("saida") record selects one of 5 types; each reveals type-specific required fields
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-031.justification: Status OK, verdict NOT_APPLICABLE, no escalation: the 5-type output decision tree with presence-grading and stool-aspect enums is a controlled clinical-forms asset, so it is adopted verbatim into the v2 clinical-forms design doc, preserving the PT-BR presenca/aspecto vocabularies verbatim per the cluster's constraint on controlled vocabularies.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-032.justification: Status OK, verdict NOT_APPLICABLE, no escalation: the ventilacao-conditional requirement for fluxo_o2_sup or fio2 is a straightforward clinical-forms decision-tree asset that the v2 vital-signs sub-form needs verbatim, alongside the unbounded numeric fields noted for the sibling vitals in this same sub-form.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-033.justification: Status OK, verdict NOT_APPLICABLE, no escalation: the CPF+PIN digital-signature eligibility check (byte-identical across entradas/saidas/sinais_vitais serializers) embodies the medical-legal sign-to-finalize pattern CFM 2.299/2021 requires, so the authz/audit logic is adapted into the security doc rather than ported as three duplicated serializer copies.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-034.justification: Status OK, no escalation, but the notes flag that the sign Popconfirm's disabled prop is bound to !can_delete instead of a sign-specific permission (BalancoHidricoContent.tsx:276) -- the manage/delete permission-gating intent is sound but this specific delete-controls-sign coupling is a mechanism bug that v2's unified action-permission model must not reproduce.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-035.source_quote: List endpoint required-day + auto-create with mismatched day value
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-035.justification: Status DISCREPANCY, escalation band P3: the list() action requires a caller-supplied 'dia' but then silently substitutes 'yesterday' for record auto-creation before 07:00, mis-dating first-time records; this entire GET-triggers-create endpoint is legacy homecare-app plumbing retired under ADR-001 (Intensicare reads AMH Gold, it does not run this Django viewset), so the bug is moot rather than corrected.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-036.source_quote: Balanco hidrico auto-provisioning, no direct write endpoints
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-036.justification: Status OK, no escalation: this rule only documents that the legacy BalancoHidricoViewSet exposes list/retrieve with Create/Update/Destroy mixins commented out, i.e. record writes only happen as a GET side-effect -- this is homecare-app CRUD plumbing that ADR-001's Athena-only/no-own-ingestion mandate retires outright, not a clinical rule to carry forward.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-037.source_quote: Daily fluid-balance auto-creation for occupied homecare beds
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-037.justification: Status OK, no escalation: the Celery shared_task that pre-creates one empty BalancoHidrico row per occupied homecare bed per day is scheduling/deployment plumbing tied to the retired Django/Celery homecare stack; v2 computes fluid-balance aggregates on demand from AMH Gold source events rather than pre-provisioning empty daily containers.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-038.source_quote: Entrada soft-delete adjusts 24h fluid balance and logs audit action
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-038.justification: Verify true, verdict VERIFIED against the ADQI/Malbrain fluid-balance framework, but ledger constraint CLU-BALANCO-HIDRICO-C-05 flags that balanco_24h is a mutable incremental accumulator corrected only on create/delete, never on edit -- the soft-delete-plus-audit-log intent is preserved, but the running-total subtraction on destroy is replaced by v2 recomputing the balance from source rows.
evidence: Malbrain MLNG et al. Anaesthesiol Intensive Ther 2014;46(5):361-380

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-039.source_quote: Saida soft-delete adjusts 24h fluid balance and logs audit action
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-039.justification: Mirrors RULE-038 (verify true, verdict VERIFIED, same ADQI/Malbrain basis) for output records; the soft-delete audit-logging intent is kept but the balanco_24h += quantidade increment on destroy is the same accumulator anti-pattern flagged in CLU-BALANCO-HIDRICO-C-05, so v2 recomputes from source rows instead of adjusting a mutable running total.
evidence: Malbrain MLNG et al. Anaesthesiol Intensive Ther 2014;46(5):361-380

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-040.source_quote: Entrada/Saida write manage_data payload injection (balanco id + assinar passthrough)
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-040.justification: Status OK, verdict NOT_APPLICABLE, no escalation: injecting the parent balanco id from a nested route kwarg and conditionally passing through an 'assinar' payload flag is Django REST Framework view-plumbing specific to the retired homecare API surface, with no independent clinical semantics beyond what RULE-033/042 already capture for signing.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-041.source_quote: Fluid-balance (balanco hidrico) row type-label resolution and signature-date format
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-041.justification: Status OK, no escalation: showing the free-text nome for the 'outra_entrada'/'outra_saida' sentinel types instead of the humanized label, and formatting signature timestamps as DD/MM/YYYY HH:mm, are sound PT-BR presentation conventions worth keeping, but the per-route switch-statement mechanism (no default branch, silent undefined) is legacy frontend code adapted rather than ported as-is.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-042.justification: Status OK, no escalation: the showSignButton gate (ativo AND not-yet-signed AND can_assinar AND can_manage_balanco_hidrico) is the core medical-legal sign-to-finalize check, but edge_cases note ItemEntrada/ItemSaida omit the explicit can_manage check that ItemSinaisVitais and the desktop table enforce, so v2 adapts this into one unified, consistently-enforced signature gate.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-043.source_quote: Saida record sign posts to the "entrada" route (bug)
