RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-043.justification: Status DISCREPANCY, escalation band P3: ItemSaida's card sign handler calls onSign("entrada", item.id) instead of onSign("saida", item.id), mis-routing the sign PATCH to the wrong endpoint for a saida record id -- this is a well-understood copy-paste bug, so the corrected form (sign always targets the record's own route) is designed rather than escalated for clinical judgment.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-044.justification: Status OK, no escalation: the four fixed sub-routes (entrada, saida, sinais-vitais, and the read-only visao-geral aggregate) define the fluid-balance module's navigation structure and payload-shape contract, which the v2 clinical-forms screen adopts verbatim as its four fluid-balance views.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-045.justification: Status AMBIGUOUS with escalation band AMBIGUOUS: the ativo/data_assinatura/deletado_por/can_delete/can_assinar record-lifecycle is inferred only from field shapes with no visible state-transition code, and the notes call this a recurring medical-legal 'sign to finalize' theme worth keeping, so per policy AMBIGUOUS-but-clinically-relevant rules are ratified rather than guessed at.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-046.source_quote: Fluid-balance PDF export with optional signatures
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-046.justification: Status OK, no escalation: the confirm-then-download PDF export with an optional 'assinaturas' flag is a legitimate reporting feature clinicians need in v2, but the unguarded balanco!.id non-null assertion (runtime throw if balanco isn't loaded) is a fragile mechanism that v2's export screen must guard against rather than reproduce.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-047.source_quote: Entrada always marked checado on creation
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-047.justification: Status OK, no escalation: forcing checado=True on every Entrada regardless of client input, with no equivalent forced flag on SinaisVitais, is an internal legacy bookkeeping quirk of the Django serializer with no surfaced clinical meaning or UI exposure identified in this partition, so it is retired along with the platform it belonged to.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-048.source_quote: Entrada/Saida quantidade fallback to zero before persist
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-048.justification: Status OK, no escalation: coercing a still-falsy quantidade to 0 after the dieta_enteral default keeps quantidade non-null for arithmetic, which the correct null-safe sum from CLU-BALANCO-HIDRICO-C-04 must also guarantee -- the zero-fallback intent is kept, but unified into one consistent null/zero-sum convention across entrada/saida rather than this ad hoc post-hoc patch.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-049.source_quote: Entrada/Saida default display name from tipo
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-049.justification: Status OK, no escalation: defaulting the display name to get_tipo_display() when nome is blank is a sensible UX convention, but the edge_cases flag that the default only persists because of a trailing obj.save() call later in create() -- a fragile ordering dependency that v2 must make explicit and not rely on incidentally.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-050.source_quote: Explicit 'dia' parameter parsing/validation for balanco hidrico
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-050.justification: Status OK, no escalation: strict 'YYYY-MM-DD' parsing with a Portuguese 400-error message is validation logic scoped entirely to the retired homecare 'dia' query-param endpoint (see RULE-035/036); with that endpoint retired under ADR-001, this specific validation mechanism has no v2 target to carry forward.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-051.justification: Status DISCREPANCY, escalation band P3: EntradasViewSet/SaidasViewSet use the default .objects manager instead of objects_without_deleted, so soft-deleted rows remain visible in list responses -- contrasting with BalancoHidricoViewSet's correct convention -- so v2 adapts this into a deny-by-default exclude-soft-deleted rule applied uniformly, matching the convention the catalog itself names as correct.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-052.source_quote: Vital-signs field set and units (sinais vitais)
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-052.justification: Status OK, verdict NOT_APPLICABLE, no escalation: the vitals field-to-unit mapping (bpm, mmHg, C, %) is the clinical contract for this form and is kept, but the edge_cases-flagged truthy-render filter that hides a genuine reading of 0 (e.g. escala_dor 0, saturacao 0) is a display bug v2 must not reproduce, so the field/unit set is adapted with explicit is-not-null rendering.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-053.source_quote: Fluid intake/output field set and volume unit (entrada/saida)
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-053.justification: Status OK, verdict NOT_APPLICABLE, no escalation: the entrada/saida field set with quantidade fixed in milliliters is kept, but the edge_cases note a truthy/toString rendering drift between the card view (a 0 renders) and the overview grid (a 0 is suppressed, RULE-025) -- v2 adapts this into one consistent zero-visible rendering rule across surfaces.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-054.justification: Status OK, verdict NOT_APPLICABLE, no escalation, no edge cases flagged: showing an empty-state message only when both entradas and saidas are empty, and rendering each non-empty section independently, is a simple, bug-free presentation rule adopted verbatim into the v2 overview tab.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-055.source_quote: Controlled list of intravenous hydration solution types.
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-055.justification: Status OK, verdict NOT_APPLICABLE, no escalation: the 7-item IV hydration solution list (Agua destilada, Solucao Hipertonica, Soro Fisiologico, etc.) is a controlled PT-BR clinical vocabulary asset with no free-text escape hatch, adopted verbatim with accents preserved per CLU-BALANCO-HIDRICO-C-08.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-056.source_quote: Controlled list of continuous-infusion (BI/bomba de infusao) drugs and hydration selectable for
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-056.justification: Escalation band UNVERIFIABLE mandates ratification: this 32-item continuous-infusion (BI) drug/hydration pick-list is a facility-specific formulary with no guideline dictating menu contents, and the escalation flags a source-array count discrepancy plus spelling/brand-name variants (Clanidina, Hidrocortizona, Ketamin vs Cetamina) that a human must resolve before the vocabulary is finalized for v2.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-057.source_quote: Controlled list of antibiotics/antifungals/antivirals selectable as an intake.
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-057.justification: Escalation band UNVERIFIABLE mandates ratification: this 40-item antimicrobial pick-list is a facility-specific formulary with no authoritative reference, and the escalation flags trailing-whitespace values ('Bedfordpoly B ', 'Novamin ') that need a data-quality ruling before the vocabulary is carried into the v2 clinical-forms design as-is.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-058.source_quote: Controlled list of electrolytes for a replacement intake.
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-058.justification: Verdict UNVERIFIABLE but escalation band P3 (decide normally, not mandatory ratification): the 8-item electrolyte vocabulary is chemically sound overall, but the escalation flags that the phosphate label 'Fosfato (PO42-)' renders a non-standard charge notation, so the vocabulary intent is adopted while the label is adapted to the chemically-correct ion-charge convention.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-059.justification: Escalation band UNVERIFIABLE mandates ratification: this 7-state consciousness enum resembles the AVPU/AVDI reactivity ladder but is not identical to it (7 states vs AVPU's 4), and the escalation confirms it is a categorical UI enum rather than a validated scale, so a human ruling on whether to keep, rename, or map this to a standard scale is required before v2 design.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-060.source_quote: If a complaint is flagged, a free-text description becomes required.
RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-060.justification: Status OK, no escalation: requiring motivo_queixa free text only when queixa is flagged is a minimal, unambiguous conditional-required-field rule with no edge cases or divergence noted, adopted verbatim into the v2 vital-signs sub-form.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-061.justification: Status DISCREPANCY, escalation band P3: this Horario mask enforces strict HH:MM with no empty-string branch, while a sibling discipline-form regex (RULE-003) allows empty -- since fluid-balance intake/output/vital-sign timestamps feed the 07:00-07:00 window and 2h-bucket aggregations elsewhere in this cluster, the corrected form makes the field required (this regex's behavior) rather than optional (RULE-003's), resolving the two divergent regexes in favor of the stricter one.

RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-062.justification: Status AMBIGUOUS with escalation band AMBIGUOUS: the BalancoHidricoFilter on 'dia' is dead code, never attached to any viewset since BalancoHidricoViewSet implements its own manual day-cutoff logic (RULE-023) instead -- per policy, AMBIGUOUS rules not worth keeping are retired, and this unused filter class has no unique behavior to preserve.

RULE-CADASTROS-UI-001.source_quote: passes whichever string value is chosen straight through to onFilter
RULE-CADASTROS-UI-001.justification: This is legacy trilhas-frontend bed-occupancy filter UI plumbing whose entire component tree is replaced by the v2 design system, so the unresolved 'unknown'-sentinel ambiguity is moot; any no-filter semantics must be re-specified explicitly in the new filter API contract rather than inherited.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: v2 design-system filter components with explicit optional-filter semantics
supersedes_note.lost: the ambiguous 'unknown' literal sentinel behavior, which is not clinically load-bearing

RULE-CADASTROS-UI-002.source_quote: these fields are always disabled, regardless of the form's own disableAll/mode setting
RULE-CADASTROS-UI-002.justification: The intent of locking nome/código for externally-synced (automatica) records to preserve data integrity from the source-of-truth system is sound and worth keeping, but the duplicated ad hoc per-component predicate must be replaced by a shared field-lock rule keyed off record provenance in the v2 forms.

RULE-CADASTROS-UI-003.source_quote: only rendered when the page is in editing mode AND the current user holds the can_manage_usuario permission
RULE-CADASTROS-UI-003.justification: The deny-by-default pattern of gating a destructive delete-professional action on both edit mode and an explicit management permission is a security lesson worth preserving, but the client-only enforcement must be adapted into a server-enforced authorization check in the v2 architecture.

RULE-CADASTROS-UI-004.source_quote: are only rendered for users holding the can_manage_camera permission
RULE-CADASTROS-UI-004.justification: Gating sensitive camera credential fields (login/senha/IP) behind a dedicated can_manage_camera permission is a deny-by-default authorization pattern worth keeping, but must be re-implemented with server-side enforcement rather than client-side field visibility alone in v2.

RULE-CADASTROS-UI-005.source_quote: typing into the CPF field also writes the unformatted CPF digits into the password field
RULE-CADASTROS-UI-005.justification: Whether defaulting a brand-new user's password to their own semi-public CPF digits was an intentional convenience overridden before submit or an actual security gap cannot be resolved from the code alone and directly affects the v2 authentication threat model, so this AMBIGUOUS, security-relevant rule requires mandatory human ratification.

RULE-CADASTROS-UI-006.source_quote: initialized to the same fixed literal string, rendered in a disabled
RULE-CADASTROS-UI-006.justification: A shared hardcoded 'signature PIN' literal ('Homecare@Vidaconecta') applied to every user could be either an inert placeholder overridden server-side or an actual shared electronic-signature credential, and this distinction has direct LGPD/CFM electronic-signature compliance implications, so it requires mandatory human ratification before any v2 design decision.

RULE-CADASTROS-UI-007.source_quote: the "usuarios" (member list) field is only forwarded to the parent submit handler when every entry
RULE-CADASTROS-UI-007.justification: This is legacy antd Form onValuesChange autosave plumbing specific to the trilhas-frontend group-edit component, including a type-guard that silently drops the usuarios field mid-selection; the v2 stack's own form/autosave mechanism replaces this entirely rather than porting the fragile type-narrowing guard.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: v2 design-system form autosave/save mechanism with explicit typed payloads
supersedes_note.lost: the legacy typeof-narrowing guard that silently drops 'usuarios' from partial autosave payloads

RULE-CADASTROS-UI-008.source_quote: only renders the "Tipo" (manual/automática) selector when opened in "modal" (creation) mode
RULE-CADASTROS-UI-008.justification: The business semantic that a company's manual/automatica type is immutable after creation is a genuine data-governance invariant worth carrying forward, while the hex-color '#' strip/re-add round-trip for the native color input is an implementation detail to be replaced by a typed color field in the v2 forms.

RULE-CADASTROS-UI-009.source_quote: input is masked as 000.000.000-00 for display/entry, then the mask punctuation is stripped
RULE-CADASTROS-UI-009.justification: The CPF mask (000.000.000-00) and unformat-before-submit pattern is a verified, Receita-Federal-conformant Brazilian identifier convention used consistently across the user filter and registration forms, so it is carried forward unchanged into the v2 clinical forms vocabulary.

RULE-CADASTROS-UI-010.source_quote: trims the name, trims+lowercases the username, reduces the profile photo to its base64 payload
RULE-CADASTROS-UI-010.justification: The normalization intent (trim/lowercase identity fields, strip CPF mask, drop sector assignment when the submitter lacks can_manage_usuario) is sound server-trust-boundary hygiene worth keeping, but must move from client-side submit-time normalization into server-side validation in the v2 architecture.

RULE-CADASTROS-UI-011.source_quote: only marked as required (defaultFormRules / emailFormRules applied) when the form is rendered in "modal" mode
RULE-CADASTROS-UI-011.justification: Requiring core identity fields at creation time is sound, but dropping that same required-ness silently in edit mode (verified by the Phase-1 correction note to be unique to FormUsuario, unlike the three sibling forms) is an inconsistent mechanism that v2 must correct to enforce required-field rules uniformly across create and edit modes.

RULE-CADASTROS-UI-012.source_quote: Shared antd-style rule object marking a field mandatory with Portuguese error message
RULE-CADASTROS-UI-012.justification: The shared required-field rule with the Portuguese error message 'Este campo é obrigatório!' is a simple, verified, framework-agnostic validation convention applied uniformly across all forms, so it is adopted verbatim (PT-BR message text preserved) into the v2 clinical-forms validation vocabulary.

RULE-CADASTROS-UI-013.source_quote: E-mail fields must match emailPattern regex; error message "E-mail invalido!"
RULE-CADASTROS-UI-013.justification: The emailPattern regex and its Portuguese 'E-mail inválido!' error message form a verified, consistently duplicated (constants.ts + emailFormRules.ts) validation rule with well-understood edge cases (optional TLD, permissive local part), so it is adopted into the v2 clinical-forms email validation vocabulary.

RULE-CADASTROS-UI-014.justification: The patient sex-code-to-PT-BR-label mapping (M/F/O/N -> Masculino/Feminino/Outro/Não informado) is a controlled clinical vocabulary asset with no ambiguity, so it is adopted verbatim with accents preserved into the v2 clinical-forms domain vocabulary.

RULE-CADASTROS-UI-015.source_quote: The 27 Brazilian states/federal district (UF code -> name) used for address
RULE-CADASTROS-UI-015.justification: The complete 27-entry IBGE UF code-to-state-name enumeration is a standard, externally governed controlled vocabulary with no legacy-specific deviation, so it is adopted unchanged into the v2 address/council-registration forms vocabulary.

RULE-CADASTROS-UI-016.source_quote: CEP is governed by Correios: official display is the 5-3 form NNNNN-NNN with a single hyphen and NO period
RULE-CADASTROS-UI-016.justification: Verification confirms CPF/CNPJ/BRL/phone masks match authoritative Receita Federal/ANATEL/pt-BR standards exactly, but the CEP mask inserts an unauthorized period ('00.000-000' instead of the Correios-mandated 'NNNNN-NNN' single-hyphen form), so the CEP branch must be corrected while the other verified masks are carried forward as-is.

RULE-CADASTROS-UI-017.justification: Rendering datetime versus date versus plain text is a legitimate display need, but inferring the value's type purely from raw string length (>=25, >=10 thresholds) is a fragile heuristic that silently mis-renders any non-date >=10-char string, so v2 must use explicit type-tagged fields instead of a length heuristic.

RULE-CADASTROS-UI-018.source_quote: any key whose name CONTAINS "data_" or "dt_" is treated as a date
RULE-CADASTROS-UI-018.justification: Auto-detecting date fields for serialization is a reasonable convenience, but keying detection on a substring match of object keys (with divergent falsy-filtering behavior between the inbound moment and outbound string variants) is an unreliable mechanism that v2 should replace with explicit schema-declared date fields.

RULE-CADASTROS-UI-019.justification: The intent of whitelisting known image extensions (including RAW camera formats) to drive inline preview vs generic icon is sound, but the confirmed case-sensitive matching (excluding uppercase PNG/JPG) is a latent bug that v2 must fix with case-insensitive comparison while keeping the same extension set.

RULE-CADASTROS-UI-020.source_quote: only accepts a single file at a time and restricts the file picker to images or PDF documents
RULE-CADASTROS-UI-020.justification: Restricting the avatar/logo uploader to a single image-or-PDF file is a sound constraint worth keeping, but the fake setInterval-based progress bar that does not reflect actual upload/conversion completion must be replaced by real progress reporting in the v2 upload component.

RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-001.source_quote: Total SOFA is the arithmetic sum of the six organ sub-scores
RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-001.justification: The total SOFA score as a straight arithmetic sum of six verified organ sub-scores (0-24) matches Vincent et al. 1996 exactly with no unit or boundary defects, so it is adopted unchanged as the SOFA aggregation formula for the early-warning-scores domain.
evidence: Vincent JL et al. Intensive Care Med. 1996;22(7):707-710

RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-002.source_quote: SOFA organ-1 (respiration) points from the PaO2/FiO2 ratio
RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-002.justification: This P0-escalated FiO2 percent-vs-fraction unit conflict makes the SOFA respiratory sub-score numerically wrong by roughly 100x whenever the validator's 21-100 percentage convention is used instead of the 0.21-1.0 fraction assumed by every threshold, so clinical ratification of the canonical FiO2 unit is required before the sub-score can be adopted or corrected.
evidence: Vincent JL et al. Intensive Care Med. 1996;22(7):707-710

RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-003.source_quote: SOFA organ-2 (coagulation) points from platelet count.
RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-003.justification: The platelet-based coagulation sub-score bands (>=150000=0 down to <20000=4) reconcile exactly with the standard SOFA coagulation cutoffs published by Vincent et al. 1996, so it is adopted verbatim for the early-warning-scores SOFA composite.
evidence: Vincent JL et al. Intensive Care Med. 1996;22(7):707-710

RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-004.source_quote: SOFA organ-3 (liver) points from total bilirubin (mg/dL).
RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-004.justification: The P1-escalated bilirubin sub-score uses strict '<' at the 1.9/5.9/11.9 mg/dL upper bounds instead of the standard '<=', creating dead value gaps that silently return None and could crash or miscount the SOFA total, so the boundary-fix intent must be clinically ratified before the corrected closed-interval bands replace the legacy gaps.
evidence: Vincent JL et al. Intensive Care Med. 1996;22(7):707-710

RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-005.source_quote: SOFA organ-4 (cardiovascular) points from noradrenaline dose, dobutamine presence, and MAP.
RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-005.justification: This P0-escalated cardiovascular sub-score scores a raw noradrenaline VOLUME in ml against a cutoff of 10 instead of a weight-normalized mcg/kg/min vasopressor rate required by standard SOFA, a unit defect severe enough that the correct rate-based mapping must be clinically ratified before adoption.
evidence: Vincent JL et al. Intensive Care Med. 1996;22(7):707-710

RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-006.source_quote: SOFA organ-5 (neurological) points from Glasgow Coma Scale.
RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-006.justification: The GCS-based neurological sub-score bands (15=0 through <6=4, with glasgow 1-5 correctly resolving to 4) match the standard SOFA neurological component exactly per the MDCalc/Medscape reconciliation, so it is adopted unchanged.
evidence: Vincent JL et al. Intensive Care Med. 1996;22(7):707-710

RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-007.source_quote: SOFA organ-6 (renal) points from serum creatinine OR 24h urine output.
RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-007.justification: This P0-escalated renal sub-score widens the '2 points' creatinine band to 2.0-4.0 mg/dL instead of the standard 2.0-3.4 and leaves a dead gap at (4.9,5.0], and the ledger notes the corrected v2 renal score must take the MAX (not any-first-match) of the creatinine and urine-output sub-scores, so the fix requires clinical ratification.
evidence: Vincent JL et al. Intensive Care Med. 1996;22(7):707-710

RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-008.source_quote: Computes the PaO2/FiO2 oxygenation ratio used by SOFA and by sedation/ventilation/sepse pathways
RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-008.justification: This shared PaO2/FiO2 ratio helper is the P0-escalated root cause of the cluster-wide FiO2 percent-vs-fraction ambiguity feeding SOFA respiratory, ventilation, and sepsis pathways, plus a False-as-sentinel return type that behaves as zero downstream, so the canonical FiO2 unit and sentinel handling must be ratified before any v2 domain reuses this formula.

RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-009.source_quote: Derives mean arterial pressure from systolic (PAS) and diastolic (PAD) pressures
RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-009.justification: The MAP = (2*PAD + PAS)/3 formula is the universally standard weighted mean arterial pressure estimate confirmed by StatPearls/MDCalc with no unit or boundary defects, so it is adopted verbatim as the shared hemodynamics MAP calculation feeding SOFA cardiovascular scoring and the hemodynamic-instability domain.

RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-010.source_quote: Uses a fixed 365-day year (leap years ignored).
RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-010.justification: The legacy age-in-years helper floors (now - birthdate).days // 365 using a fixed 365-day year, which slowly overcounts age relative to the Gregorian 365.2425-day year and returns an inconsistent None-vs-int type; this P2-escalated drift is corrected to standard calendar-year age with birthday adjustment before feeding age-gated alerts such as the vision's delirium-risk-in-patients-over-65 rule.

RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-011.source_quote: On save, SOFA copies raw values from the linked DadosProntuario before scoring; noradrenaline dose
RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-011.justification: The verified attribute-sourcing logic (copy fio2/po2/plaquetas/bilirrubinas/pam/dobutamina/noradrenalina/glasgow/creatinina from the prontuario, then recompute every sub-score) is clinically sound, but its mechanism is a Django model save()-hook side effect that has no place in the vision's deterministic-function processing pillar, so it is adapted into an explicit SOFA compute service invoked on each new observation rather than an implicit ORM save hook.

RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-012.source_quote: Builds the input set for the SOFA organ-dysfunction score from prontuario data plus the noradrenaline
RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-012.justification: This rule's substance -- the ten SOFA-feeding variables (fio2, po2, plaquetas, bilirrubinas, pam, dobutamina, glasgow, debito_urinario_24h, creatinina, noradrenalina) assembled at first ICU admission -- is clinically correct and worth keeping, but its mechanism is bound to a legacy Django use-case class with no equivalent in the FastAPI-based v2 stack, so it is adapted into the SOFA domain's documented input contract rather than ported as a use-case class.

RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-013.source_quote: GCS total score constrained to the inclusive range 3..15 across the homecare neurological model
RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-013.justification: The GCS 3-15 inclusive range validator reconciles exactly across the backend model and both frontend capture forms with only cosmetic form-type differences, matching the original Teasdale & Jennett 1974 scale bounds, so it is adopted verbatim as the canonical GCS input-range constraint for v2 clinical forms.

RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-014.justification: Whether trilha_homecare's divergent RASS labels (-1 Sonolento, -4 Sedacao Intensa, -5 Nao desperta, and omission of the '' Nao informado sentinel) reflect an intentional homecare-specific wording choice or unnoticed drift from the trilha_manual/frontend canonical set is an open clinical question, and the cluster constraint explicitly conditions disposition on that intent, so ratification of the single canonical RASS label set is required before adoption.
evidence: Sessler CN et al. Am J Respir Crit Care Med. 2002;166(10):1338-1344

RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-015.justification: The 0-10 inclusive numeric pain rating scale validator matches the standard NRS-11 pain instrument used across critical care with no boundary or unit defects, so it is adopted verbatim as the canonical pain-scale input range for v2 clinical forms.
evidence: NRS-11 Numeric Rating Scale for pain intensity

RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-016.justification: The 3-12 inclusive behavioral pain scale range matches the published Behavioral Pain Scale (BPS) total of three 1-4 subscales with no defects found, so it is adopted verbatim as the canonical behavioral pain-scale input range for non-communicative ICU patients in v2 clinical forms.
evidence: Payen JF et al. Crit Care Med. 2001;29(12):2258-2263

RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-017.source_quote: ARDS severity levels defined but the sdra field is commented out of DadosProntuario.
RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-017.justification: The leve/moderada/grave ARDS severity taxonomy matches the Berlin Definition classes exactly and directly maps onto the vision's SDRA Leve/Moderada/Grave alert tiers, but its legacy carrier field is commented out of DadosProntuario and dead, so it is adapted into a computed PaO2/FiO2-derived classification in the respiratory domain rather than revived as a manually-entered form field.

RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-018.source_quote: Functional Oral Intake Scale captured as a 7-level select with the standard level descriptions.
RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-018.justification: The seven ordinal FOIS levels (nivel1 nothing-by-mouth through nivel7 no restrictions) match Crary et al. 2005 exactly aside from a cosmetic label typo, so the scale is adopted verbatim, preserving the PT-BR level text, as the canonical Functional Oral Intake Scale vocabulary for v2 clinical forms.
evidence: Crary MA et al. Arch Phys Med Rehabil. 2005;86(8):1516-1520

RULE-COMUNICACAO-001.source_quote: groupby only merges emoji runs that are already consecutive
RULE-COMUNICACAO-001.justification: Confirmed live DISCREPANCY (Reacao has no Meta.ordering, so the Python-groupby endpoint can silently undercount a single emoji's total into multiple entries) but escalation band is UNVERIFIABLE with no clinical/published authority to arbitrate correctness intent vs acceptable-at-scale tradeoff, so it is escalated rather than agent-decided per policy.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-002.source_quote: get_minha_reacao_id returns the pk of the requesting user's own Reacao
RULE-COMUNICACAO-002.justification: Status OK and reconciled-safe (unique_together on observacao+usuario confirmed, so .get() cannot raise MultipleObjectsReturned), but the escalation band is UNVERIFIABLE and per policy any P0/P1/UNVERIFIABLE-banded rule is mandatorily ratified regardless of its own status, distinct from RULE-COMUNICACAO-001's live-bug rationale.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-003.source_quote: the code references the bound method `get_pk` without calling it
RULE-COMUNICACAO-003.justification: Confirmed serialization defect (missing () call on a @property yields a bound-method repr instead of a pk) in the homecare fluid-balance feed field, but band UNVERIFIABLE with no clinical authority to resolve whether the field is load-bearing enough to warrant a corrected v2 design versus simple removal, so it is escalated.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-004.source_quote: updates that user's Firestore unread-message-count document
RULE-COMUNICACAO-004.justification: Per-user Firestore document read-modify-write for unread-message counters is a legacy transport tied to a data store outside the AMH-consumer stack; ADR-001 Implicacao 3 confines operational app state to PostgreSQL/TimescaleDB and vision's interface pillar mandates WebSocket + Redis pub/sub for real-time push, so the Firestore mechanism itself cannot be ported as-is.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: Redis pub/sub + WebSocket push over an operational PostgreSQL/TimescaleDB counter table (ADR-001 Implicacao 3, VIS-2-08/09)
supersedes_note.lost: direct per-document Firestore set/update semantics; v2 must define an equivalent atomic increment/decrement-floored-at-0 counter primitive in the new store

RULE-COMUNICACAO-005.source_quote: NO reactions, NO checked confirmations (checagens with checado=True), and NO other replies
RULE-COMUNICACAO-005.justification: The three-part eligibility test for whether an unread contribution may be decremented (no reactions, no checked checagens, no other replies) is store-agnostic business logic worth preserving verbatim even though its consumer (RULE-COMUNICACAO-004's Firestore counter) is being superseded by a new counter primitive.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-006.source_quote: its 'checado' field becomes truthy, and reduzir_qtd(observacao) confirms the shared unread-count was decremented
RULE-COMUNICACAO-006.justification: The trigger condition (checklist item checked off AND eligibility confirmed) for firing an unread-count decrement is a reusable workflow rule distinct from RULE-COMUNICACAO-005's pure eligibility predicate; it must be re-wired against whatever counter primitive replaces the superseded Firestore call in RULE-COMUNICACAO-004.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-007.source_quote: to avoid simultaneously incrementing and decrementing the same message-count total
RULE-COMUNICACAO-007.justification: Suppressing a duplicate increment notification when a reply already caused a decrement on its parent is a reusable double-counting guard independent of the Firestore transport being superseded, though the deliberate absence of @transaction.atomic (noted in edge_cases) should NOT be carried forward given the new operational-store durability requirements.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-008.source_quote: with a leito filter, messages up to 96 hours (4 days) old are retained
RULE-COMUNICACAO-008.justification: The 48h-default/96h-with-leito-filter chat retention windowing is a store-agnostic product policy worth carrying forward as-is into whatever operational data store backs v2 chat history, independent of the Firestore-counter mechanism changes affecting the neighboring unread-badge rules.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-009.source_quote: a lodash debounce wrapping showNotification with a 2000ms wait
RULE-COMUNICACAO-009.justification: A misnamed lodash-debounce popup throttle ('trotthleNotification') tied to a specific legacy React/antd component tree is legacy frontend UI mechanics with a known naming/behavior mismatch bug; v2's notification UI will be designed fresh against the new frontend stack rather than porting this defect.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-010.source_quote: every other message type renders with a fixed neutral gray (#959595)
RULE-COMUNICACAO-010.justification: Conditional ball-color derivation from a legacy statusTrilha lookup map, scoped only to leito-type messages, is legacy frontend styling mechanics tied to the old trilhas UI; v2's alert severity color-coding will follow the clinical.* severity scale defined fresh in the new design system, not this component's ad hoc branch.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-011.source_quote: navigates to the associated trilha (care-pathway) page for that leito/occupation
RULE-COMUNICACAO-011.justification: This click-through routing tree hardcodes window.location.replace URLs into the legacy trilhas-frontend page structure (empresa/estabelecimento/setor path segments); v2's information architecture and routing will be designed fresh, so this specific navigation mechanism does not carry forward.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-012.source_quote: 'manual' uses the real Paciente record (nested serializer); 'automatica' uses a stored JSON snapshot
RULE-COMUNICACAO-012.justification: Branching patient-payload resolution on a legacy leito.tipo enum (manual/automatica/homecare) with per-branch JSON snapshot fields is tenancy/data-model plumbing specific to the old Django bed model; ADR-001 Implicacao 2 makes the MPI's mpi_id the sole patient-identity key, which supersedes this branching scheme entirely rather than adapting it.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-013.source_quote: resolves the URL kwarg 'ocupacoes__pk' as EITHER a Movimentacao pk
RULE-COMUNICACAO-013.justification: The dual-mode Movimentacao-or-Leito pk overload for resolving bed occupation on an observation is legacy ORM plumbing coupling this cluster's data model to the old Trilhas bed/admission schema; the new platform reads patient/encounter context from AMH Gold via mpi_id (ADR-001 Implicacao 1-2), making this specific resolution scheme obsolete.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-014.source_quote: can be toggled by the current user unless it has already been checked by someone else
RULE-COMUNICACAO-014.justification: The deny-re-toggle-once-confirmed-by-another-user rule (plus the reply-preview toggle lockout) is a sound audit/authorization pattern worth keeping for any v2 checklist or protocol-item UI, independent of the specific MessageBallon/chat component it currently lives in.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-015.source_quote: only triggers a delete call when that entry belongs to the currently logged-in user
RULE-COMUNICACAO-015.justification: Restricting deletion of a reaction to its own author, and treating an absent own-reaction as a silent no-op rather than an implicit create, is a general own-resource-only authorization pattern worth preserving for any v2 UI surface with per-user deletable annotations.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-016.source_quote: only offers an "Entrar na sala" (join call) button if at least one video input device is detected
RULE-COMUNICACAO-016.justification: This join-gate is wired specifically to Agora.io's RTC SDK and the browser mediaDevices API inside the legacy BuildVideoChat component; teleUTI video calling is absent from the v2 vision/ADR scope entirely, so this vendor-coupled device-detection gate has no target document to carry it into.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-017.source_quote: generates one UsuarioNotificacao (unread) per user in the observation's sector and dispatches a websocket task
RULE-COMUNICACAO-017.justification: Fanning a new event out to every user in the affected sector via a bulk-created per-user unread record plus a websocket dispatch is a generically reusable staff-notification pattern that the v2 Alert Engine's staff-facing alert delivery (with its own ack_sla requirement) can adapt, independent of the legacy Notificacao/UsuarioNotificacao Django models.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-018.source_quote: an Indicador of type 'interacao_notificacao' is recorded (analytics of notification engagement)
RULE-COMUNICACAO-018.justification: This first-view engagement logging writes to the legacy Indicador analytics model on a Django consumer class specific to the old notification-center websocket; v2's alert response tracking is already a first-class field (response.ack_sla) in the alert schema, making this bespoke engagement-indicator mechanism redundant rather than adaptable.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-019.source_quote: its websocket message type is "leito" if bound to a bed, else "setor"
RULE-COMUNICACAO-019.justification: Auto-spawning a Notificacao from a saved Observacao and branching its tipo_mensagem on the presence of a legacy leito FK is model-coupled Trilhas plumbing (Observacao/Notificacao/Leito); the v2 Alert Engine generates alerts from clinical rule evaluation over AMH Gold data, not from a generic chat-message save hook, so this trigger mechanism does not carry forward.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-020.source_quote: ignore any incoming message whose responsavel (author) id matches the currently logged-in user's id
RULE-COMUNICACAO-020.justification: Suppressing real-time self-notifications (never showing a user a toast/badge for their own authored message) is a small, store-agnostic UX rule worth carrying into whatever websocket notification stream replaces the two legacy hardcoded FE socket connections in DisplayNotificaoes.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-021.source_quote: loads messages in fixed pages of 20, newest-first from the API but reversed for display
RULE-COMUNICACAO-021.justification: This hardcoded page-size-20, reverse-on-display, refetch-the-whole-window-on-any-action pagination scheme is legacy frontend implementation detail specific to the old MessagesList component; v2's chat/messaging surfaces (if retained) will be designed fresh against the new frontend stack rather than porting this quirk.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-022.source_quote: the first item in the fetched list is automatically selected
RULE-COMUNICACAO-022.justification: Auto-selecting the first sector conversation on the establishment-wide chats index page is a page-specific legacy frontend micro-behavior tied to a route (chats/index.tsx) that does not exist in the v2 information architecture; not worth adapting on its own.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-023.source_quote: resets qtd_mensagens to 0 for that user/sector; the badge is not reset merely by rendering
RULE-COMUNICACAO-023.justification: This client-side reset writes directly to the same Firestore document family as RULE-COMUNICACAO-004's server-side counter; since that Firestore counter mechanism is being superseded by a Redis/Postgres-backed primitive (ADR-001 Implicacao 3), this client-side mark-all-read reset must be superseded in lockstep against the new store rather than left pointing at Firestore.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: mark-all-read reset against the Redis/Postgres-backed unread-counter primitive replacing RULE-COMUNICACAO-004's Firestore document
supersedes_note.lost: direct client SDK write to Firestore; v2 needs an API endpoint or optimistic client mutation against the new store

RULE-COMUNICACAO-024.source_quote: the feed is only auto-refreshed and scrolled to top if the user has NOT scrolled away
RULE-COMUNICACAO-024.justification: Legacy Next.js homecare feed WebSocket scroll-preservation UI mechanic is frontend plumbing tied to the retired trilhas-frontend stack; v2 activity-feed UX is a new-stack design task, not a ported client-side scroll heuristic, and the checkScroll()-undefined edge case is not worth preserving.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-025.source_quote: Additional pages are loaded 20 at a time via a "Mais" (load more) button
RULE-COMUNICACAO-025.justification: The offset/limit=20 pagination intent for an activity feed is sound and worth keeping, but the feed.length-gated (not count-based) load-more visibility check is a mechanism bug; v2 should paginate off canonical count/nextOffset semantics in the new design system, not the legacy client-side gate.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-026.source_quote: each with a fixed alerta value of "NEUTRO", instead of deriving pathway/alert state from the actual occupancy data
RULE-COMUNICACAO-026.justification: Hardcoded trilhasMock with fixed NEUTRO alert state is flagged DISCREPANCY and its escalation band is P3, but it masks real patient alert/urgency state in the recommendations drawer opened from the feed, which is a clinical-safety-adjacent gap that a clinician/product owner must confirm is safe to drop versus needing a corrected wiring to real occupancy data in v2.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-027.source_quote: Deleting a reaction bypasses normal (soft-delete) semantics by calling delete(force_delete=True)
RULE-COMUNICACAO-027.justification: The reaction hard-delete override is an explicit deviation from the platform's dominant soft-delete convention (objects_without_deleted, is_active flags) noted as an open question in the cluster brief, so whether v2 should preserve permanent-delete-on-reaction or align it with the deny-by-default soft-delete pattern needs an explicit product/architecture decision, not a unilateral agent call.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-028.source_quote: populated from Firestore users under that sector filtered to online_call === true
RULE-COMUNICACAO-028.justification: The Firestore-backed online_call roster query is legacy teleUTI video-room plumbing tied to the Firebase/Agora stack being replaced under ADR-001's AMH Gold consumer architecture; the clinical intent (show who is currently in a sector's call) is a UI feature to be redesigned against the new-stack presence mechanism, not this Firestore query shape.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-029.source_quote: the listener is registered unconditionally on every render (not inside a useEffect and not cleaned up/removed)
RULE-COMUNICACAO-029.justification: Router.events-based auto-leave-on-navigate is Next.js/Agora client plumbing with a known listener-leak defect (registered every render, never removed); the underlying intent (leave a call cleanly on navigation) is legacy-app mechanics tied to the frontend stack ADR-001 supersedes, not a clinical rule to port forward.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-030.source_quote: forces a full browser page reload immediately after a user leaves the call/closes tracks
RULE-COMUNICACAO-030.justification: Agora client.join single-join-guard plus unconditional window.location.reload() on leave is legacy video-call frontend plumbing (Agora RTC SDK lifecycle workaround) explicitly tied to the trilhas-frontend stack being retired under the platform change; not a clinical rule worth carrying forward.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-031.source_quote: both browser tab-close/refresh and in-app route navigation are intercepted with a confirmation prompt
RULE-COMUNICACAO-031.justification: The unsaved-changes exit-confirmation guard for the video-call room has a known double-confirm() UI defect and is implemented against Next.js Router events specific to the retired frontend stack; the UX intent (warn before leaving an active call) is a v2 design concern to be rebuilt fresh, not this mechanism.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-032.source_quote: agora-rtc-react ^1.1.1 (Agora.io real-time video/audio conferencing SDK for React)
RULE-COMUNICACAO-032.justification: This dependency-manifest-only rule documents the legacy teleUTI stack (Agora RTC, Firebase, raw websocket) as evidence that live tele-consultation is a core care-delivery workflow; under ADR-001's AMH Gold consumer architecture the concrete SDKs are replaced, but the product requirement for real-time video/audio + realtime sync is preserved and re-specified against the new platform's realtime mechanism.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: AMH Data Platform-native realtime channel (per ADR-001 streaming/pub-sub mechanism) plus a v2-selected video/audio SDK
supersedes_note.lost: the specific Agora RTC + Firebase + raw websocket dependency pins themselves

RULE-COMUNICACAO-033.source_quote: overwrites checado_por_id with the current authenticated user's pk, preventing a client from checking off an observation on someone else's behalf
RULE-COMUNICACAO-033.justification: Server-forcing checado_por_id from the authenticated session rather than trusting client body is a sound deny-by-default authorization pattern worth keeping, but the concrete DRF manage_data override mechanism is legacy-stack specific and must be reimplemented against the new API layer's auth context per ADR-001.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-034.source_quote: the intended rule (block feed actions on unoccupied beds) is not enforced
RULE-COMUNICACAO-034.justification: Both the homecare-bed-type guard and the unoccupied-bed guard for feed actions are confirmed entirely unenforced at the model layer (validar_leito never called, and its inner check is dead code besides), an open question the cluster brief explicitly raises about whether v2 should now enforce the unoccupied-bed rule legacy code never enforced -- a policy decision outside agent authority.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-035.source_quote: that union only enumerates 7 values, omitting 'editar'
RULE-COMUNICACAO-035.justification: The acaoDict (8 keys, including PT-BR verbatim labels Criar/Alterar/Editar/Inativar/Assinar/Liberar/Administrar/Não Administrar) versus the Feed.Acao TS union (7 values, missing editar) is a confirmed DISCREPANCY the cluster brief explicitly flags as an open question -- whether editar is a live action the v2 vocabulary must retain or dead code to drop -- requiring product/clinical confirmation before the canonical 7-or-8-value enum is fixed for v2 forms.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-036.source_quote: sorriso | choro | aprovacao | reprovacao | forca | gratidao | coracao
RULE-COMUNICACAO-036.justification: The 7-value PT-BR emoji-reaction enum is cross-verified identical in both frontend TS union and backend ReacaoChoices with no divergence, so it is a verified controlled vocabulary asset that should carry forward verbatim as the reaction taxonomy for v2 chat/feed features.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-037.source_quote: A user may have at most one reaction on a given observation; saving/deleting a reaction adjusts Firebase unread-message counters
RULE-COMUNICACAO-037.justification: The one-reaction-per-user-per-observation invariant (unique_together) and the save/delete-triggers-counter intent are sound and worth keeping in v2, but the concrete Firebase-counter side-effect mechanism must be reimplemented against the new platform's notification/counter store rather than Firestore per ADR-001's platform change.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-038.source_quote: forces usuario_id to the current authenticated user and 'observacao' to the observacoes__pk URL kwarg on every reaction create
RULE-COMUNICACAO-038.justification: Server-forcing usuario_id and observacao from auth/URL context rather than the request body is the same deny-by-default authorization pattern as RULE-COMUNICACAO-033/042/043 and is worth preserving, but the DRF manage_data mechanism itself must be rebuilt on the new API stack.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-039.justification: The two-value setor|leito message-scope enum is cross-verified against backend derivation logic (tipo_mensagem = leito if self.leito else setor) with no divergence found, making it a verified controlled vocabulary safe to carry forward verbatim into v2 chat/message scoping.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-040.source_quote: the "Pontos de atenção" (points of attention) checkbox checklist section is rendered only if the trilha has at least one criterio
RULE-COMUNICACAO-040.justification: Conditionally showing a care-pathway criteria checklist only when the trilha has criterios (each carrying nome/alerta/recomendacoes) is a sound clinical-form UX rule to keep, but the concrete useModalSender hook implementation is legacy-frontend-specific and needs to be rebuilt as a v2 clinical-forms screen rule.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-041.source_quote: a client cannot set the setor via the request body
RULE-COMUNICACAO-041.justification: Status AMBIGUOUS with escalation band AMBIGUOUS: the intended validation message for a missing setor__pk never actually fires because str(None) does not raise, so whether v2 should preserve the (broken) exception-swallowing behavior or fix it to genuinely reject requests missing a URL-scoped setor needs explicit product/architecture sign-off given it touches a security-relevant server-forced-field invariant.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-042.source_quote: overwrites request.data['responsavel_id'] with request.user.pk on every create
RULE-COMUNICACAO-042.justification: Forcing responsavel_id to the authenticated user on every observation create is the same deny-by-default server-derived-identity pattern worth keeping across the cluster, but the try/except-based DRF view mechanism must be reimplemented on the new API stack rather than ported verbatim.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-043.source_quote: manage_data renames incoming 'resposta' to 'resposta_id' when present
RULE-COMUNICACAO-043.justification: A field-rename shim from 'resposta' to 'resposta_id' (with the falsy-value edge case leaving it un-renamed) is legacy DRF serializer plumbing specific to the old API's field naming inconsistency; v2's new API schema can simply define the canonical field name directly, so this rename mechanism has no target to carry forward.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-044.source_quote: online_call=true when the triggering action is "in", and false for any other action
RULE-COMUNICACAO-044.justification: The in/out-to-boolean online_call presence-flag derivation is written directly to Firestore, a legacy dependency being replaced under the platform change; the underlying presence concept is folded into the broader video-call roster redesign (see RULE-COMUNICACAO-028/032) rather than kept as a standalone rule.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-045.source_quote: updates the locally tracked file list only when the new fileList is shorter than the current one
RULE-COMUNICACAO-045.justification: Status AMBIGUOUS with escalation band AMBIGUOUS: the cluster brief itself cannot determine whether the removal-only reconciliation in FilePicker.handleChange is an intentional dedup guard (additions handled elsewhere via customRequest) or an incomplete handler that silently drops same-length replace-in-place edits, so it needs explicit confirmation before deciding whether v2's chat file-attachment UX should adopt or fix this behavior.

RULE-COMUNICACAO-046.source_quote: returns True only when the observation, after the triggering change, has zero reactions, zero checked confirmations
RULE-COMUNICACAO-046.justification: The reduzir_qtd predicate's clinical-communication intent (decrement an unread/notification badge only once an observation is fully un-engaged: no reactions, no checked checagens, no other replies) is a sound eligibility rule worth preserving, but it must be re-implemented against v2's own notification/counter store rather than the legacy Firebase-counter fanout it currently gates.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-001.source_quote: converted from ISO 'YYYY-MM-DD' to Brazilian 'DD/MM/YYYY' format
RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-001.justification: Mechanical date-format helper embedded in the legacy Trilhas Django PDF-export view; IntensiCare v2 has no homecare PDF-prescription export surface, so this formatting rule is obsolete with the platform change, not a clinical rule to carry forward.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-002.justification: Glosa-Zero is a Tasy-era billing/documentation-compliance pathway with two disagreeing alert computations (persisted alerta vs view tipo_alerta, ESC-P3-189); it is legacy billing-administrative plumbing outside IntensiCare v2's clinical decision-support scope, and its known divergent bug must not be ported.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-003.source_quote: If ANY url is invalid (timeout, HTTP error
RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-003.justification: combinar_documentos is a legacy S3-upload/PDF-merge utility specific to the Trilhas document-export pipeline; v2 does not implement its own PDF-merge-and-upload mechanism, so this all-or-nothing validation gate is retired with the plumbing it protects.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-004.justification: Selects between two legacy HTML templates for a homecare fluid-balance PDF export based on a case-sensitive query flag; this is legacy document-rendering plumbing tied to the retired PDF-export subsystem, not a clinical computation for v2's fluid-balance/hemodynamics domain.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-005.source_quote: taken from the first MicroIndicadores record
RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-005.justification: Admission-date lookup for a legacy fluid-balance PDF header, sourced from the proprietary MicroIndicadores table; IntensiCare v2 reads clinical data from the AMH Gold layer via Athena per ADR-001, not from this legacy homecare reporting table, so the lookup is obsolete.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-006.source_quote: ordered into 5 priority tiers by item type (excluding soft-deleted rows)
RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-006.justification: Print-ordering rule for a legacy continuous-prescription PDF keyed to Tasy's DS_TIPO_ITEM_PRESCR field taxonomy; IntensiCare v2 does not generate printed prescription documents, so this scheduling/ordering rule has no target surface and is retired with the export view.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-007.justification: Vendor-specific format-to-envelope mapping for the CryptoCubo electronic-signature integration used only by the legacy Trilhas homecare document pipeline; IntensiCare v2 has no equivalent document e-signature feature, so this plumbing mapping is retired wholesale.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-008.justification: PIN fallback path both risks an unhandled crash on None and silently sends a differently-encoded (non-base64) PIN on empty string (ESC-P3-156); this is a known-broken Cryptocubo constructor bug in a vendor integration being retired wholesale, so the discrepancy is not carried forward.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-009.justification: The intent -- persist an audit record and hard-fail on any non-200 response from a signing integration -- is a sound fail-closed, audit-on-error pattern worth preserving even though the concrete CryptoCubo vendor call is retired; carry the pattern into v2's integration-failure audit logging, not the Cryptocubo-specific mechanism.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-010.source_quote: digitally signed via Cryptocubo only if the acting user has both a CPF and a PIN
RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-010.justification: The deny-by-default eligibility gate (no signature without both CPF and PIN, silent skip plus warning) is a sound credential-completeness pattern worth adapting into v2's identity/authorization design, even though the specific CryptoCubo signing mechanism itself is retired with the legacy stack.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-011.source_quote: only render when at least one file has been selected
RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-011.justification: Pure legacy React UI-visibility rule for a bulk-file-download button in the Trilhas frontend's file-management screens; IntensiCare v2's design/screens are alert- and score-centric per vision.json and do not inherit this file-download widget, so it is retired.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-012.source_quote: must have picked at least one image or a PDF
RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-012.justification: Client-side form-submission gate for the legacy leito document-upload drawer (images/PDF plus categoria/observacao); this UI mechanic belongs to the retired Trilhas file-management surface and has no counterpart in IntensiCare v2's clinical-alert-focused design.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-013.source_quote: only rendered/enabled for users holding the can_upload_files_amhdocs permission
RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-013.justification: The specific upload-tab UI is retired legacy plumbing, but the underlying deny-by-default, permission-gated-tab authorization pattern (hide/disable UI when a named permission is absent) is a lesson worth preserving for v2's own permission-gated screens per ADR-001's inherited IAM model.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-014.source_quote: gated twice by conceptually the same permission, via two different mechanisms
RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-014.justification: Whether the client-side re-check of can_access_relatorio_evolucao is deliberate defense-in-depth (useEffectivePermissions may compute a fresher, plan-aware permission than the static SSR cookie) or dead redundant code cannot be resolved from this partition alone (ESC-AMBIGUOUS-311), so a human ratifier must decide whether v2's authz design should replicate dual server+client gating.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-015.justification: Auto-posts a billing record directly into the Tasy Oracle stored procedure amh_gerar_lancamento_auto with an unexplained magic constant (501) and silently swallows connection errors (ESC-AMBIGUOUS-347); ADR-001 mandates Athena-only reads from AMH Gold and forbids proprietary ingestion/write pipelines to Tasy, so this direct-Tasy-write mechanism is retired outright rather than ratified.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-016.justification: Multi-level defensive response-envelope unwrapping for a legacy 'agrupar PDFs' frontend workflow, reflecting an unstable historical backend contract; the entire PDF-grouping feature is retired with the Trilhas document-export subsystem, so this validation chain has no v2 target.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-017.justification: Default sort order (oldest-first, no leading minus) for a legacy per-leito uploaded-files list is flagged ambiguous only as to whether the missing '-' prefix was intentional (ESC-AMBIGUOUS-324); the file-listing feature itself is legacy document-management UI not carried into IntensiCare v2, so the sort-order question is moot and the rule is retired.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-018.justification: Legacy PDF filename-construction and raw unquoted Content-Disposition header pattern for homecare document exports; IntensiCare v2 does not generate these PDF artifacts, so the filename convention (and its known header-quoting edge case) is retired with the export view.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-019.justification: Evolution-note count-by-type reporting aggregation feeds a legacy Trilhas billing/reporting dashboard specific to the homecare evolution-report screen; IntensiCare v2's dashboards are score/alert-centric per vision.json, so this aggregation utility is retired with its source screen.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-020.justification: The 54-code document-category vocabulary (fiscal notes, TISS/SADT guides, EHC* per-discipline evolution notes, Vida Conecta codes) is a genuine PT-BR controlled vocabulary asset, but its three hand-maintained copies disagree (54 vs 34 vs 33, EI missing from Leito per ESC-P3-190); adopt the reference-correct form as a single canonical 54-code enum (the runtime categoriaArquivo.ts superset) rather than porting any of the drifted subsets.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-021.justification: Both PDF-export endpoints decide base64-vs-raw response format via a raw truthy string check on 'b64' (even 'false'/'0' triggers base64 mode), inconsistent with the sibling 'assinatura' param's explicit equality check (ESC-P3-157); the entire PDF-export subsystem is retired, so the known-broken flag-parsing bug is not corrected or ported forward.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-022.justification: Missing presence-check on the 'dia' query parameter causes an unhandled 500 TypeError instead of a 400 response, unlike the sibling balanco-hidrico endpoint (ESC-P3-158); since the whole legacy PDF-export view is retired with the platform change, this input-validation defect is retired along with it rather than corrected in place.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-023.justification: Unguarded .first() access on an empty prescriptions queryset raises an unhandled 500 AttributeError, unlike the sibling balanco-hidrico lookup which guards with .exists() (ESC-P3-159); this defect lives entirely inside the legacy prescription-PDF export view being retired, so it is dropped with that view rather than corrected.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-024.source_quote: against its corresponding item in the CPOE
RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-024.justification: This record type implies a Trilhas-vs-Tasy-CPOE data-integrity reconciliation whose actual mismatch algorithm is server-side and out of partition scope (ESC-AMBIGUOUS-325); IntensiCare v2 reads clinical/order data from a single AMH Gold source of truth per ADR-001, eliminating the dual-system divergence this record exists to track, so it is retired rather than ratified.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-025.source_quote: formatted as a date via formatDataItem
RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-025.justification: Prefix-based ('dt_') display-formatting convention for a legacy signature-inconsistency report's filter-summary UI; the report screen itself belongs to the retired Trilhas documentation-compliance surface, so this formatting convention has no v2 target.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-026.justification: Fixed-offset (non-robust) data-URI MIME sniff for a legacy PDF-picker upload widget, paired with a fake, non-network-tied progress bar; this upload-widget mechanic is legacy Trilhas frontend plumbing with no equivalent surface in IntensiCare v2's design, so it is retired.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-027.source_quote: resolves nr_atendimento from the Leito identified
RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-027.justification: Marked UNVERIFIABLE because it is proprietary internal routing/integration code with no external standard governing 'nr_atendimento' resolution (ESC-UNVERIFIABLE-280); per policy every UNVERIFIABLE-banded rule is mandatorily routed to human ratification rather than agent-decided disposition.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-028.source_quote: rewritten to point back at this API
RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-028.justification: Marked UNVERIFIABLE because the pagination-link rewrite policy is proprietary DRF-proxy behavior with no governing external standard beyond RFC 3986's optional query component (ESC-UNVERIFIABLE-281); per policy every UNVERIFIABLE-banded rule is mandatorily routed to human ratification rather than agent-decided disposition.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-029.source_quote: 'ids' is a non-empty list before proceeding
RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-029.justification: Non-empty-array request-body validation guard for the legacy 'agrupar-pdf' bulk PDF-grouping action; the entire AMHDocs-proxy PDF-grouping feature is retired with the Trilhas document-export subsystem, so this input-shape guard has no v2 counterpart to adopt into.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-030.source_quote: maps distinct failure modes from the external
RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-030.justification: HTTP-status decision tree for the legacy AMHDocs PDF-grouping proxy (401/500/400/200 mapping); this proxy endpoint and the PDF-grouping feature it serves are retired with the Trilhas document-export subsystem under the v2 platform change, so the status-mapping logic is retired with it.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-031.source_quote: must be greater than or equal to the start date
RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-031.justification: Date-range validator (with an inclusive-boundary/wording mismatch against its own 'maior que' error text) for the legacy Arquivos list filter UI; that file-listing screen is retired legacy document-management UI with no equivalent in IntensiCare v2's alert/score-centric design, so this validator is retired.

RULE-DOCUMENTACAO-FATURAMENTO-032.justification: ADDENDUM-band item (Phase-6 security/coverage finding) — policy mandates RATIFY; prior analysis retained: The specific CryptoCubo signing call is retired with the legacy stack, but the underlying compliance risk -- silently defaulting to an 'advanced' (not 'qualified') signature profile with ICP-Brasil disabled, which affects legal validity under MP 2.200-2 -- is a lesson worth carrying into v2's security/compliance design as an explicit, reviewed signature-strength decision rather than a silent keyword default.

RULE-EFICIENCIA-001.source_quote: Maps the computed criteria booleans of the Eficiencia v3 model to the final alert enum
RULE-EFICIENCIA-001.justification: The count-based AMARELO/VERMELHO aggregation mechanism is structurally sound, but only criterio_3, criterio_5 and criterio_10 of the ten defined criteria are ever computed (the other seven remain permanently null and commented out), so most clinical signal never reaches the alert and v2 must rewire the full criteria set through the same aggregation shape.

RULE-EFICIENCIA-002.source_quote: the Hb>=7 threshold is NEVER actually applied
RULE-EFICIENCIA-002.justification: P1-escalated operator-precedence bug (`diurna_hemoglobina or 0 >= 7`) defeats the restrictive Hb>=7 RBC-transfusion gate entirely, firing on any non-empty hemoglobin value plus an RBC order and leaving SCA/AVC exclusions inert, so clinical ratification of the corrected AABB-aligned threshold is required before v2 encodes it.
evidence: Carson JL et al. AABB RBC Transfusion Thresholds JAMA 2016;316(19):2025-2035

RULE-EFICIENCIA-003.justification: The Hb-strictly-between-6-and-7 and >=2-unit thresholds are already implemented correctly, but the fromkeys-None-values bug makes the SCA/AVC diagnosis exclusions vacuously true, so v2 must adopt the reference-correct form that fixes only the exclusion-membership predicate while preserving the sound single-unit reassessment thresholds.
evidence: Carson JL et al. AABB RBC Transfusion Thresholds JAMA 2016;316(19):2025-2035

RULE-EFICIENCIA-004.justification: P1-escalated wrong-model-field bug reads a nonexistent ultima_cpoe.diurna_plaquetas attribute, so the sole wired VERMELHO platelet-transfusion trigger is permanently False, silently disabling the highest-severity alert in the cluster; the correct field and 4h catheter-exclusion window must be clinically ratified before v2 encodes AABB platelet guidance.
evidence: Kaufman RM et al. AABB Platelet Transfusion CPG Ann Intern Med 2015;162(3):205-213

RULE-EFICIENCIA-005.source_quote: GCS threshold is 13 in code, not 6 as documented
RULE-EFICIENCIA-005.justification: P0-escalated coma-without-sedation criterion uses GCS<13 instead of the documented GCS<6, combined with an AND-combined sedative-absence filter that requires all five infusion sedatives present simultaneously to exclude, so it almost never excludes a sedated patient; this suspected-brain-death donor-potential flag must be clinically adjudicated before v2 design.

RULE-EFICIENCIA-006.justification: P1 band and AMBIGUOUS extraction status both mandate ratification; the mechanical-restraint-without-agitation criterion checks PRESENCE of delirium instead of the documented ABSENCE of delirium, so clinicians must confirm which polarity is clinically correct before the wired AMARELO restraint alert is encoded in v2.
evidence: Devlin JW et al. PADIS Guidelines Crit Care Med 2018;46(9):e825-e873

RULE-EFICIENCIA-007.source_quote: across ALL windows COMBINED (not a per-exam count)
RULE-EFICIENCIA-007.justification: Flagging redundant/repeated exam ordering to cut cost and patient burden is a sound operational-efficiency intent, but the implemented mechanism sums positive window-hits across unrelated exam-class windows (5d/7d/14d/21d/30d) into one undifferentiated threshold rather than firing per exam-class, and omits several exams listed only in the docstring, so v2 must rebuild the per-class detection logic.

RULE-EFICIENCIA-008.source_quote: the whole all([...]) is always False
RULE-EFICIENCIA-008.justification: The composite ICU-discharge-readiness gate is clinically sound in intent, but the wrong 'temp' aggregate key (should be 'max_temp') permanently forces the gate False, the Glasgow check tests truthiness rather than >=11, and TEC>5/lactate>2 are required TRUE which inverts the intended absence-of-instability condition, so v2 must rebuild these four sub-predicates while keeping the overall composite structure.
evidence: Singer M et al. Sepsis-3 JAMA 2016;315(8):801-810

RULE-EFICIENCIA-009.justification: UNVERIFIABLE escalation band mandates ratification; the three-tier frailty/palliative-appropriateness OR-bundle (age 80/70, LOS 7/15d, SAPS3 50/70, SOFA 3/5 cutoffs) is an internal institutional rule with no single authoritative published source, so a clinical committee must confirm the exact cutoffs before v2 encodes a palliative-care trigger.

RULE-EFICIENCIA-010.source_quote: the RASS branch crashes rather than testing +1..+4
RULE-EFICIENCIA-010.justification: The delirium/agitation risk-factor OR-bundle qualitatively aligns with PRE-DELIRIC predictors (age, metabolic acidosis, infection, sedation/coma, agitation) but the RASS clause crashes on `range[1, 5]` subscripting, the mutually-exclusive pain sub-block is logically unsatisfiable, and the antecedent membership check is inert, so the mechanism must be rebuilt while preserving the underlying risk-factor set.
evidence: van den Boogaard M et al. PRE-DELIRIC model BMJ 2012;344:e420

RULE-EFICIENCIA-011.source_quote: the opposite of the documented step-down
RULE-EFICIENCIA-011.justification: Step-down-readiness intent is directionally correct per ACCP/ATS ventilator-liberation criteria (low FiO2/PEEP, light sedation) but the implemented clause list requires FiO2>45, PEEP>10, GCS<13 and RASS<-2 all TRUE, describing a HIGH-support deeply-sedated patient, the exact inverse of readiness, and duplicates one spontaneous-ventilation clause, so the boolean polarity must be corrected end-to-end.
evidence: Ouellette DR et al. ACCP/ATS Ventilator Liberation Guideline Chest 2017

RULE-EFICIENCIA-012.justification: The verified facade payload correctly documents AABB/TRICC-aligned restrictive transfusion thresholds (Hb>=7, Plt>25,000) plus discharge, delirium, restraint and organ-donation guidance text; it is the reference-correct clinical intent that the broken predicate rules 002-011 must be rebuilt to actually reach, so it is adopted as the canonical alert-text source.
evidence: Carson JL et al. AABB 2023 JAMA
Kaufman RM et al. AABB Platelet CPG Ann Intern Med 2015;162(3):205-213

RULE-EQUILIBRIO-001.source_quote: so criterio_2 and criterio_4 alert text exists in the payload but is NOT shown
RULE-EQUILIBRIO-001.justification: Fluid-balance criteria 1-4 (BH>3000 ml/24h very-positive, accumulated positive balance, unneeded soroterapia, falling diurese) carry sound institutional intent, but legacy get_detalhe() surfaces only criterio_1 and criterio_3 -- criterio_2 (accumulated BH) and criterio_4 (falling diurese, which triggers sepsis-risk assessment) are silently dropped from clinician view; v2 AKI/fluid-management domain must restore full surfacing while preserving the unreferenced institutional thresholds (3000 ml/24h, 5 ml/h, 2400 ml/dia) verbatim.

RULE-EQUILIBRIO-002.justification: Nephrotoxic drug substitution (Cefepime to Tazocin, Vancomicina to Linezolida), morphine avoidance under impaired clearance, and Na>160 hypernatremia correction (NaCl 0.22% 84 ml/h, hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg) are VERIFIED against Sterns/Adrogue-Madias, but criterio_7 (morphine clearance) and criterio_10 (hypernatremia protocol) are never surfaced by get_detalhe() -- v2 electrolyte/pharmaco domains must restore visibility of the hypernatremia rescue path, a genuine patient-safety gap in the legacy UI.
evidence: Adrogue HJ, Madias NE. N Engl J Med 2000;342:1493-1499

RULE-EQUILIBRIO-003.justification: This trilha-specific VERMELHO/AMARELO/NEUTRO color aggregation (vermelho if criterio_1 or criterio_9, amarelo if 3/6/8) is legacy Django-model alerting plumbing coexisting with a dead count-based path and an unmerged TrilhaSeteSintetico variant using different thresholds; v2's unified Alert Engine severity scale (normal/watch/urgent/critical) replaces it, restoring full 10-criteria surfacing instead of the legacy 1,3,6,8,9 subset.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: v2 Alert Engine unified severity scale (normal|watch|urgent|critical) applied uniformly across all 10 equilibrio criteria
supersedes_note.lost: legacy VERMELHO/AMARELO/NEUTRO enum and the restricted surfacing of criteria 1,3,6,8,9 only; the dead count-based define_tipo_alerta path and the unmerged TrilhaSeteSintetico variant (amarelo:4/vermelho:5, all 10 criteria) are also discarded

RULE-EQUILIBRIO-004.source_quote: Initial trigger K > 6; reassessment/escalation threshold K > 5.5 at +4h.
RULE-EQUILIBRIO-004.justification: Hyperkalemia (K>6) rescue bundle -- calcium gluconate, furosemide 20 mg x3 bolus, polarizing solution, fenoterol/salbutamol q15min, +4h reassessment with bicarbonate 8.4% 1 ml/kg and Sorcal if K>5.5 -- matches the KDIGO/UpToDate reference bundle on clinical content (ESC-P2-066, clinical_impact low), but the legacy code mislabels serum potassium as mg/dl instead of mEq/L; the reference-correct form must use mEq/L (or mmol/L) throughout and never propagate the unit error.
evidence: KDIGO conference summary, Eur J Emerg Med 2020;27(5):329-337 (PMC7448835)
Mount DB, Sterns RH. Treatment of hyperkalemia (UpToDate)

RULE-ESTABILIDADE-001.justification: Code inverts the documented -2000ml sign to +2000, sums balance over ALL history instead of a 6h/4h window, and swaps the Ringer-bolus check for a lactate test; the P1 escalation band on this triple divergence mandates clinical ratification before any fluid-balance criterion can be designed into v2 hemodynamics.
evidence: Evans L et al. Crit Care Med 2021;49(11):e1063-e1143

RULE-ESTABILIDADE-002.justification: Shock index (HR/SBP >0.7, HR/MAP >0.9) with beta-blocker and vasopressor absence is a validated hypoperfusion marker, but this criterion is dead code never invoked by calcular_criterios; per CON-0162 it MUST NOT be ported as live behavior without re-wiring, so intent is sound but wiring mechanism must be corrected before it feeds the early-warning-scores domain.
evidence: Koch E et al., Open Access Emerg Med 2019, PMC6698590

RULE-ESTABILIDADE-003.source_quote: capillary refill time > 3s OR arterial lactate >= 2 mmol/L
RULE-ESTABILIDADE-003.justification: Noradrenaline-plus-hypoperfusion (CRT>3s OR lactate>=2) matches Sepsis-3/ANDROMEDA-SHOCK anchors exactly, but the criterion is unwired dead code in calcular_criterios; the clinical semantics are correct so this is a re-wiring/mechanism fix into the sepsis domain, not a threshold correction.

RULE-ESTABILIDADE-004.justification: New-vasopressor-without-sepsis-workup logic encodes SSC Hour-1 bundle elements (cultures, antibiotics, fluid load) correctly per verification, but is unwired dead code and the Ringer clause checks a single record instead of an aggregate volume, so the mechanism needs correction while keeping the SSC-aligned clinical semantics.
evidence: Evans L et al. Crit Care Med 2021;49(11):e1063-e1143

RULE-ESTABILIDADE-005.source_quote: docstring documents ABSENCE of noradrenaline but the code checks PRESENCE
RULE-ESTABILIDADE-005.justification: The presence-vs-absence contradiction between docstring and code for the occult-shock criterion, paired with the facade text's own 'sem DVA' (without vasopressor) framing, leaves true clinical intent undetermined; the P1 escalation band forces ratification of which reading (occult shock vs treated shock) v2 sepsis screening should encode.

RULE-ESTABILIDADE-006.justification: Persistent-shock-on-spontaneous-ventilation (RR>20, nora>10ml/h, lactate>2 or TEC>3, room-air/spontaneous breathing) is verified against Sepsis-3/ANDROMEDA-SHOCK/SIRS anchors with none clinical impact, but sits unwired among the dead v3 criteria, so it needs re-wiring rather than a threshold fix to reach the sepsis domain.

RULE-ESTABILIDADE-007.source_quote: no vasopressin recorded in balanco OR no hydrocortisone prescribed
RULE-ESTABILIDADE-007.justification: This is the WIRED VERMELHO high-dose-noradrenaline-without-adjuncts trigger whose paired facade text (RULE-016) asserts an unconvertible mcg/kg/min cutoff over an ml/h predicate — a P1-banded live-alert discrepancy that requires clinical ratification of the correct dosing unit and cutoff before the vasopressor-adjunct alert can be redesigned.
evidence: Evans L et al. Crit Care Med 2021

RULE-ESTABILIDADE-008.justification: Refractory-shock triple-therapy gating (nora>70ml/h AND vaso>5ml/h in the same record, no adrenaline) matches the SSC escalation ladder concept but its paired facade text (RULE-016) uses an unconvertible mcg/kg/min cutoff; the P1 escalation band mandates ratification of unit/threshold before this unwired criterion is designed into hemodynamics.
evidence: Evans L et al. Crit Care Med 2021

RULE-ESTABILIDADE-009.justification: Dobutamine-with-high-dose-noradrenaline predicate never checks the FC>130bpm tachycardia condition its own facade text (RULE-016) displays as the clinical trigger; this P1-banded text-vs-code mismatch on a live escalation-ladder rung requires ratification of which condition (dose co-occurrence vs tachycardia) governs before redesign.

RULE-ESTABILIDADE-010.justification: The WIRED VERMELHO antihypertensive-vs-vasopressor conflict rule checks only 16 of the 18 documented drugs (omitting anlodipino, metoprolol), and CON-0164/CLU-ESTABILIDADE-C-03 explicitly route this institutional, no-clinical-anchor UNVERIFIABLE rule to RATIFY rather than any autonomous disposition.

RULE-ESTABILIDADE-011.justification: The inappropriate-bicarbonate flag uses pH>7.2 as its boundary while SSC-2021 sets the 'bicarbonate not indicated' cutoff at pH>=7.15, so v2 electrolyte design must adopt the reference-correct pH 7.15/BIC 16 pairing (matching the facade text's own 'pH<7,15 e BIC<16' framing) rather than the code's 7.2 threshold, and re-wire it since it is currently unwired dead code.
evidence: Evans L et al. Crit Care Med 2021;49:e1063-e1143

RULE-ESTABILIDADE-012.source_quote: more than 2 balance records with PAS<90 AND PAD<60
RULE-ESTABILIDADE-012.justification: The WIRED AMARELO recurrent-hypotension-plus-antihypertensive alert ANDs PAS<90 with PAD<60 in the same record where the docstring specifies OR, and shares RULE-010's 16-vs-18 drug-list gap; catalog status DISCREPANCY forbids plain ADOPT, and since there is no clinical-guideline cutoff at stake (verify:false, verdict NOT_APPLICABLE) this is a boolean-logic mechanism fix, i.e. ADAPT, not a reference-correction.

RULE-ESTABILIDADE-013.justification: The WIRED AMARELO recurrent-hypertension alert ANDs PAS>155 with PAD>90 where OR was documented, requires an exact count()==2 instead of 'last 2 records', and its I64/AVCi exclusion filters dict KEYS not values so it is permanently vacuous; all three are mechanism bugs with no guideline anchor (verify:false), warranting ADAPT to correct the AND/OR, count, and exclusion-filter logic.

RULE-ESTABILIDADE-014.source_quote: VERMELHO if criterio_7 or criterio_10 set
RULE-ESTABILIDADE-014.justification: The three-tier VERMELHO/AMARELO/NEUTRO aggregation shell (byte-identical calcular_alerta/calcular_alerta_v2) is a sound, code-verified pattern eligible for plain ADOPT (status OK, verdict NOT_APPLICABLE); it becomes the canonical v2 shock alert-tiering mechanism once its four feeder criteria (007, 010 RATIFY; 012, 013 ADAPT) are individually resolved.

RULE-ESTABILIDADE-015.justification: This clinician-facing facade text for criteria 1-6/11 asserts lactate>2, ScVO2<70, deltaPCO2>6, and pH<7.15/BIC<16 conditions that the paired v3 predicate code never tests (three-plus documented mismatches), a P1-banded live-alert-text-vs-logic discrepancy that must be ratified before any single source of truth can be designed.

RULE-ESTABILIDADE-016.source_quote: septic-shock vasopressor and inotrope escalation steps
