RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-019.justification: This Django ORM queryset scoping by setor/empresa hierarchy is legacy-app plumbing tied to the ahlabs-trilhas Postgres schema; IntensiCare v2 reads clinical/administrative scope from the AMH Gold layer via Athena per ADR-001, so this bespoke query pattern is retired outright.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-020.source_quote: falling back to an external Oracle patient record
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-020.justification: Direct cross-database reads against the legacy Oracle InfoPaciente table and ad hoc Paciente creation from Tasy pathway fields are exactly the proprietary ingestion pattern ADR-001 prohibits; IntensiCare v2 must resolve patient identity solely via the MPI's mpi_id, so this rule is retired.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-021.source_quote: Beds sourced from the Tasy occupancy view are split into two care streams
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-021.justification: This Tasy-ETL bed-classification and hardcoded whitelabel tenant-routing rule (americashealth/homecare) is legacy ingestion/tenancy plumbing; ADR-001 replaces direct Tasy consumption with AMH Gold reads, so the ETL mechanism retires while the homecare-vs-ICU distinction may be reintroduced in the new tenancy model.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-022.source_quote: prevents deletion of a bed that has a Movimentacao with atual=True
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-022.justification: The intent of refusing to delete a bed with an active occupation is a sound referential-integrity invariant worth keeping in v2's operational Postgres model per ADR-001's operational-state design, even though the specific Django ORM guard and its misspelled error message are retired.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-023.source_quote: no button of any kind is rendered for an empty
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-023.justification: Bespoke client-side permission-flag gating of bed-card action buttons is legacy trilhas-frontend UI plumbing; IntensiCare v2 inherits IAM Identity Center SSO and Lake Formation ABAC per ADR-001, which supersedes this ad hoc can_add_movimentacao-style permission-flag scheme entirely.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-024.source_quote: only returns occupied beds whose patient is assigned to the requesting user
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-024.justification: Per-tenant occupancy filtering keyed on UsuarioSetorPaciente links is legacy tenancy/access-scoping plumbing, with a confirmed unreachable-ternary-arm defect noted in the extraction; IntensiCare v2's ABAC via Lake Formation (ADR-001) replaces this bespoke access-scoping mechanism.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-025.source_quote: A manual admission is blocked on automatic-type beds and on already-occupied beds
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-025.justification: The intent - block manual admission on automatica-type or already-occupied beds - is a sound workflow guard IntensiCare v2 should keep in its admission form validation, even though the underlying Django validator/exception mechanism is replaced by the new stack.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-026.source_quote: Default applied when tipo not provided at creation
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-026.justification: The leito.tipo controlled vocabulary (manual default, automatica, homecare) is a PT-BR enum asset that IntensiCare v2's bed-management data model should adopt verbatim, including manual as the creation default, consistent with RULE-021/RULE-023's use of the same enum.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-027.justification: This DRF serializer field-set-by-action optimization (has_camera only exposed on retrieve) is legacy API-layer plumbing specific to the ahlabs-trilhas Django REST Framework implementation, retired along with the rest of the bespoke Leito API surface under the new stack.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-028.justification: This camera-listing queryset filter on ip_camera is legacy Django ORM plumbing tied to the RTSP camera-integration feature (see RULE-010, itself routed to ratification); the query mechanism retires along with the rest of the legacy camera API surface.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-029.source_quote: NO ordering is applied at all (falls back to default model ordering)
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-029.justification: The setor-scoped/unscoped Movimentacao queryset pattern, duplicated verbatim across three viewsets with an inconsistent missing-ordering edge case, is legacy Django ORM query plumbing superseded by AMH Gold/Athena-backed data access in IntensiCare v2.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-030.source_quote: only returns patients whose leito matches the given setor__pk AND whose leito is currently occupied
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-030.justification: The intent of only listing currently-occupied-bed patients in a sector view is a sound care-team-facing scoping rule worth keeping in v2's patient-list screens, even though the specific Django queryset mechanism and its vacated-bed edge case are replaced by the new stack.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-031.source_quote: deactivates the movimentacao (atual=False), and frees the bed (leito.ocupado=False)
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-031.justification: The discharge lifecycle invariant - only the current movimentacao can be closed, closing it deactivates the record and frees the bed while triggering alert recomputation - is core ADT workflow semantics IntensiCare v2 must preserve in its operational occupancy state model, with the Django mechanism replaced.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-032.source_quote: assembles the SOFA score inputs, marks the bed occupied, then creates pathways and updates the alert
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-032.justification: The admission sequence - create prontuario/sub-records, compute SOFA, occupy the bed, then create pathways and recompute the aggregate alert, in that order - is the ADT admission workflow IntensiCare v2's correlation engine must reproduce with equivalent ordering semantics, using the new stack's mechanism.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-033.source_quote: forces registrado_por to the current user and leito to the leitos__pk URL kwarg
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-033.justification: This is Django REST Framework viewset-layer field-injection orchestration (forcing registrado_por/leito server-side) around the admission use case already captured in RULE-032; the DRF-specific mechanism retires with the legacy API while its server-authoritative-field intent is preserved wherever RULE-032's target lands.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-034.source_quote: omitted sub-collections (e.g. sedativos) inherit from the previous movimentacao
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-034.justification: The carry-forward-with-override semantics for a new movimentacao - clone the prior prontuario/SOFA/noradrenalina/sedativos and apply only newly supplied fields, with omitted collections inherited and provided collections fully replacing - is critical continuity-of-care logic IntensiCare v2 must reproduce exactly.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-035.source_quote: forces the new record's paciente and leito to match that previous record
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-035.justification: This is the MovimentacaoNovaViewSet's Django-layer field-injection wrapper around the carry-forward use case already captured in RULE-034 (same bed/patient enforced server-side); the DRF viewset mechanism retires with the legacy API while its invariant is preserved via RULE-034's target.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-036.source_quote: static fixed template, not per-patient data
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-036.justification: Homecare trilhas must be per-patient like the automatica pathway (RULE-037); the class-level get_payload() call and dead piora_clinica/sepse lookups show the intended per-patient shape that v2 must implement instead of the broken static template.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-037.source_quote: trilhas only populate when the bed is occupied
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-037.justification: Sound care-pathway aggregation semantics for occupied automatica beds (sepse/ventilacao/sedacao/estabilidade latest-record lookup) map directly to the correlation-engine's per-bed pathway rollup in v2, unoccupied-bed empty-list edge case preserved.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-038.source_quote: Trilha recompute orchestration on prontuario data update
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-038.justification: The clinical intent (recompute all four pathways on prontuario update, then re-aggregate the bed alert) is sound but the fixed-order synchronous Django save cascade with SOFA excluded is legacy plumbing; v2 needs event-driven recompute preserving the same four-pathway + aggregation semantics.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-039.source_quote: Builds the set of prontuario PKs to search for 12h/24h historical criteria
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-039.justification: The 12h/24h historical-lookback windowing that underpins sepse and estabilidade criteria is clinically load-bearing, but the linked-list chain walk with a documented potential infinite loop is a fragile legacy mechanism that v2 must replace with bounded time-windowed queries.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-040.source_quote: Celery task recomputes all manual pathways for current (atual=True) movimentacoes
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-040.justification: The Celery-specific shared_task with an 'trilhas' exchange and ENVIRONMENT-prefixed routing key is legacy-stack plumbing; the periodic full-recompute intent is superseded by the AMH Data Platform's own batch/streaming refresh cadence per ADR-001, which the correlation engine consumes instead of self-scheduling.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: AMH Data Platform Silver-Entities batch/streaming refresh triggers (ADR-001)
supersedes_note.lost: Celery exchange/routing-key mechanism and the SOFA-lag caveat (SOFA not resaved in the manual loop)

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-041.justification: This is a thin Django use-case wrapper around PacienteSerializer for minting local patient records; per ADR-001 Intensicare consumes the AMH MPI's mpi_id and mints no identifiers of its own, so local patient-creation plumbing has no v2 role.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-042.source_quote: computes the difference between the submitted pacientes_id list and the currently-linked patient
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-042.justification: The diff-sync pattern for professional-to-patient care-team assignment (bulk-delete removed, bulk-create added links) is a reusable access-control lesson, but the full-clear-on-empty-list edge case must be made explicit/deny-by-default rather than implicit in v2's security model.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-043.source_quote: always overwrites 'paciente', 'setor', and 'usuario' from the corresponding URL kwargs
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-043.justification: Forcing link-identity fields (paciente/setor/usuario) from trusted URL kwargs rather than client-supplied body is a deny-by-default authorization lesson worth carrying into v2's security-lgpd design, though the hard-delete (vs soft-delete/audit trail) mechanism needs correction.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-044.source_quote: Default active sector selection for patient management
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-044.justification: Auto-selecting the professional's first assigned sector on screen mount is a legacy React UI mechanic specific to the GestaoPacienteContent component that v2's redesigned screens will replace wholesale.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-045.justification: Pre-checking rows for patients already linked to the professional is a legacy patient-linking screen UI mechanic; v2's design/screens work will define fresh pre-selection behavior for the new patient-assignment UI rather than porting this component.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-046.source_quote: Marking a specific care-pathway (trilha) on a bed occupancy as attended-to
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-046.justification: The clinical workflow of marking a care pathway 'assistido' (attended-to), gated by a can_assist_ocupacao permission, is sound intent for alert-acknowledgement in v2, but the untyped POST response and absent client-side permission check need correction in the new mechanism.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-047.source_quote: Records whether a cardiac arrest occurred and its datetime, in a nullable group
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-047.justification: The parada cardiorrespiratoria (PCR) nullable boolean+datetime capture is a controlled clinical form field feeding downstream pathways; carried into v2's clinical form vocabulary verbatim, including the independently-editable boolean/datetime pairing noted as an edge case.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-048.source_quote: forces a loading spinner to display for exactly 3000ms after mount
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-048.justification: A hardcoded 3-second spinner delay on the bed-camera page, with intent ambiguous between UX masking and missing readiness-event wiring, carries no clinical weight; v2's camera/video screens will define real readiness-event-driven loading states instead.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-049.source_quote: the exclusion only removes a row when ALL THREE conditions hold together
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-049.justification: This Tasy-direct Oracle ETL test-bed exclusion filter (with unintentionally narrow AND semantics instead of the apparently-intended OR) is legacy Tasy-ETL plumbing tied to a direct Oracle connection that ADR-001's AMH-consumer platform change eliminates entirely.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-050.source_quote: a bed is occupied iff ie_ocupado == "S" (Sim/Yes), and active iff ie_situacao == "A"
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-050.justification: Deriving Leito occupancy/active flags from raw Tasy single-character indicator codes is direct-Oracle-ETL plumbing specific to the legacy stack's Tasy integration, which ADR-001's AMH Data Platform consumer model replaces with curated Gold/Silver-Entities bed-state facts.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-051.source_quote: an occupied bed with no movimentacao is an error; an empty bed returns only the bed payload
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-051.justification: The invariant that an occupied bed must have a corresponding encounter record is clinically sound, but in v2 encounter/occupancy consistency is guaranteed upstream by the AMH MPI/encounter feed (Intensicare mints no identifiers per ADR-001), superseding this local ParseError guard with an AMH data-quality contract.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: AMH MPI/encounter feed as authoritative occupancy-encounter source (ADR-001)
supersedes_note.lost: local 'leito ocupado sem movimentacao' ParseError guard

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-052.source_quote: manual data entry, automatic (device-integrated) monitoring, or homecare
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-052.justification: The closed three-value monitoring-modality enum (manual|automatica|homecare) is a controlled vocabulary asset governing which pathway/data-entry mechanism applies to a bed, and is carried verbatim into v2's clinical-forms/governance vocabulary.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-053.source_quote: restricted to a fixed catalog of 10 procedure types spanning central-venous-catheter placements
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-053.justification: The 10-value invasive-procedure-type enumeration (CVC variants, orotracheal intubation, tracheostomy, urinary catheter) is a controlled PT-BR clinical vocabulary carried verbatim into v2 clinical forms, including the noted diacritics/ASCII naming inconsistency recorded as-is.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-054.source_quote: "M" maps to a male icon, "F" maps to a female icon, and any other value
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-054.justification: This is purely a legacy Material Design icon-choice mapping (mdiGenderMale/mdiGenderFemale/mdiAccountQuestion) duplicated across two frontend pages; v2's new design system will define its own icon set, while the underlying M/F/O gender vocabulary itself is carried separately (RULE-064).

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-055.source_quote: Discharging a patient requires both data_saida (exit date) AND motivo_baixa (reason)
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-055.justification: The discharge (baixa) requirement of both exit-date AND reason is independently reconciled and verified aligned between backend and frontend with no divergence, so it is carried as-is into v2's discharge form; the 'movito_baixa' keyword typo is legacy-internal and not user-facing.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-056.source_quote: a follow-up movimentacao created via the /nova/ endpoint against an ultima_movimentacao does not require paciente
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-056.justification: The admission-vs-chained-transfer distinction (initial admission requires paciente payload; a chained /nova/ movimentacao inherits patient identity) is a sound, test-verified continuity rule for v2's ADT movement tracking and is carried through unchanged.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-057.source_quote: may be omitted (accepted, defaults applied) but must not be an empty string and must not be in the future
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-057.justification: The admission-timestamp acceptance contract (absent-OK/empty-string-invalid/future-rejected) is a test-asserted API-level rule that governs data integrity for every movimentacao creation and is carried into v2's admission-timestamp validation unchanged.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-058.source_quote: Test sets tipo='automatica' AND empty data_entrada simultaneously.
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-058.justification: The AMBIGUOUS confound (empty data_entrada already explains the 400 for any bed type per RULE-057) means no distinct automatica-specific clinical rule can be confirmed here; it carries no clinical weight of its own, so it is retired rather than ratified separately from RULE-057's already-adopted behavior.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-059.source_quote: Validates a datetime string against an expected format and rejects future dates
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-059.justification: The UTC-normalized future-date rejection for admission timestamps is a sound, reusable data-integrity guard; carried into v2's temporal validation, noting the hardcoded 'data_entrada' error key as a legacy-internal quirk not affecting the adopted clinical semantics.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-060.source_quote: Missing/malformed data_nascimento raises unhandled TypeError/ValueError (no explicit guard)
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-060.justification: Requiring a non-future birthdate and a mandatory patient payload is sound clinical registration logic, but the unhandled TypeError/ValueError on malformed input and the 'nasicmento' typo are implementation defects that v2 must fix with explicit input guards while preserving the same validation intent.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-061.source_quote: The request must contain a dados_prontuario key
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-061.justification: A simple, unambiguous required-payload-key check for dados_prontuario on movimentacao creation; carried unchanged into v2's composite validation since there is no discrepancy or ambiguity to correct.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-062.source_quote: Movimentacao creation runs bed, prontuario, patient, and (conditionally) entry-date validations together
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-062.justification: The composite-validation orchestration (bed + prontuario + patient + conditional entry-date checks run together on movimentacao creation) correctly aggregates RULE-025/059/060/061 and is carried into v2's admission validation pipeline as a single composite rule.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-063.source_quote: eme (Estado de Mal Epileptico), shic (Sindrome de Hipertensao Intra Craniana)
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-063.justification: This set of boolean comorbidity/pathway flags (covid_19, dpoc, eme, shic, historico_cirurgia_abdominal_recente, baixa_aceitacao_dieta_vo) is a controlled PT-BR clinical-form vocabulary that drives downstream respiratory (dpoc), neuro (eme/shic) and nutrition pathways, and is preserved verbatim into v2's clinical forms.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-064.source_quote: CPF mask enforces 11 digits in NNN.NNN.NNN-NN layout but no check-digit validation
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-064.justification: Requiring attendance number, name, birthdate and masked CPF for patient registration is sound, LGPD-relevant identity capture, but the absence of CPF check-digit validation is a data-quality gap v2 must close while preserving the M/F/O gender vocabulary and required-field set.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-065.justification: This viewset scopes a legacy homecare InfoPaciente model (an out-of-partition Oracle/Tasy-backed table per the cluster's out-of-partition notes) by encounter number; it is Tasy-direct legacy plumbing eliminated entirely by ADR-001's AMH Data Platform consumer model.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-066.source_quote: reference equality (===) used where deep/value equality was evidently intended
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-066.justification: This is a legacy React button-disable bug (array reference-equality check that is effectively always true) confined to the GestaoPacienteContent frontend component; v2's redesigned patient-linking screen will implement correct deep-equality selection-diffing from scratch rather than porting this defect.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-067.source_quote: the API cannot distinguish "user cleared this section" from "user never touched it"
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-067.justification: Submit-side normalization of noradrenalina/PCR/ventilacao-mecanica sub-records (annul-if-empty, reformat datetimes) is sound clinical-form intent, but the noted inability to distinguish an explicit clear from an untouched field is a mechanism gap v2 must close with an explicit null-vs-undefined contract.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-068.source_quote: horario_fim of ventilacao_mecanica is NOT re-hydrated here
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-068.justification: Rehydrating stored prontuario date strings into moment objects for display (companion to RULE-067's submit-side transform) is sound intent, but the noted gap where ventilacao_mecanica's horario_fim is never rehydrated must be fixed in v2 to keep read and submit transforms symmetric.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-069.source_quote: FormProntuario fields have no bound input controls
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-069.justification: Every Form.Item in this component is declared without a child input, so the form captures no patient/admission data at all; it is a non-functional legacy stub with no working behavior to carry forward, so v2's patient-record form will be built fresh rather than adapting a broken component.

RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-070.source_quote: defaults "Leitos" (ocupado) to "todos" and explicitly converts that sentinel to undefined
RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-070.justification: The 'todos' sentinel-to-undefined filter mapping and the asymmetric lack of a no-filter option for 'tipo' are legacy FilterOcupacoes UI mechanics specific to the current frontend; v2's redesigned occupancy list filtering will define its own consistent sentinel handling.

RULE-NUTRICAO-001.source_quote: computed live as weight divided by height squared
RULE-NUTRICAO-001.justification: BMI = peso/altura^2 is verified against the standard Quetelet index with no discrepancy and no escalation, so the live 2-decimal calculation carries forward unchanged; the unguarded altura=0 Infinity edge (CON-0181) is a build-time guard to add, not a formula defect.
evidence: WHO / standard Body Mass Index (Quetelet index)

RULE-NUTRICAO-002.source_quote: classifying oral intake in 7 ordinal levels
RULE-NUTRICAO-002.justification: FOIS is a 7-level ordinal oral-intake scale verified verbatim against the published Crary et al. 2005 instrument with no discrepancy; the nivel1-7 PT-BR labels (including the source typo 'oucompensacoes' at nivel6) are a controlled vocabulary asset preserved verbatim into the clinical-forms catalog.
evidence: Crary MA, Mann GD, Groher ME. Arch Phys Med Rehabil. 2005;86(8):1516-20

RULE-NUTRICAO-003.source_quote: Gastric residual cutoff 500 ml/6h (interrupt infusion for 2h).
RULE-NUTRICAO-003.justification: The 10-criterion nutrition pathway carries a P1 escalation (ESC-P1-048) flagging a DISCREPANCY verdict against ASPEN/SCCM 2016 (GRV>500mL) and Villanueva 2013 (Hb<7 transfusion trigger); P1 bands are mandatory-RATIFY regardless of the OK catalog status, so clinical ratification of the 10 criteria must precede any v2 nutrition-pathway port.

RULE-NUTRICAO-004.source_quote: AMARELO requires amarelo > 2 but the amarelo list has only 2 entries
RULE-NUTRICAO-004.justification: calcular_alerta hardcodes amarelo>2 against a 2-entry amarelo list, so AMARELO is structurally unreachable; this is legacy trilha-specific 3-tier alert-coloring plumbing with no clinical anchor (verify:false), so it is superseded by the v2 Alert Engine's uniform normal/watch/urgent/critical severity taxonomy rather than patched in place.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: v2 Alert Engine 4-tier severity scale (normal/watch/urgent/critical) applied uniformly across domains
supersedes_note.lost: legacy AMARELO(>2)/VERMELHO(==1) trilha6 coloring, already dead code so nothing operative is lost

RULE-NUTRICAO-005.source_quote: diet-route booleans, acceptance enum, vomit/gastric-residue/HGT numeric ranges
RULE-NUTRICAO-005.justification: The frontend Terapia Nutricional fieldset carries P2 escalation ESC-P2-077 and a DISCREPANCY verdict on the SUP indication timing, but its own numeric ranges are UI input caps with no clinical anchor; sound intent (diet-route capture, tolerance monitoring) is kept while the HGT max/min cross-validation gap is corrected and the SUP threshold defers to RULE-NUTRICAO-006's ratification.

RULE-NUTRICAO-006.justification: The vm_mais_72h stress-ulcer eligibility option sets a 72h ventilation threshold against ESC-P2-086, but ASHP 1999 anchors the classic SUP indication at >48h while the SCCM/ASHP 2024 update de-emphasizes ventilation-alone entirely; this is disputed clinical intent between two guideline eras, not a simple numeric fix, so it routes to ratification rather than ADOPT-CORRECTED.
evidence: ASHP Therapeutic Guidelines on Stress Ulcer Prophylaxis (1999)

RULE-NUTRICAO-007.source_quote: is capitalized (case-sensitive persistence key), unlike the surrounding snake_case keys
RULE-NUTRICAO-007.justification: The conditional prescribed-volume reveal logic is sound (diet-route toggle reveals a matching detail field) but its persistence keys Volume_prescrito_enteral/parenteral are capitalized against the surrounding snake_case convention; the intent is adopted while the case-sensitive naming defect is corrected in the v2 clinical-forms field-key convention.

RULE-NUTRICAO-008.source_quote: constrained to the numeric range [0, 3] meters
RULE-NUTRICAO-008.justification: The altura field's [0,3] meter range validation has no discrepancy, no escalation, and feeds directly into the BMI calculation (RULE-NUTRICAO-001); this bound is a benign UI input guard that adopts unchanged into the v2 clinical-forms nutrition fieldset.

RULE-NUTRICAO-009.justification: The intolerancia/aversao {sim, nao, desconhece} enum with conditional free-text reveal is a verified, unescalated controlled vocabulary with no computation, so it adopts verbatim as a nutrition-assessment form asset.

RULE-NUTRICAO-010.source_quote: multi-select of care risks {lpp = pressure skin injury, broncoaspiracao, alergia_alimentar}
RULE-NUTRICAO-010.justification: The riscos assistenciais checklist (lpp, broncoaspiracao, alergia_alimentar) with conditional allergy free-text is an unescalated, non-computed PT-BR vocabulary asset that adopts verbatim into the v2 clinical-forms risk checklist.

RULE-NUTRICAO-011.source_quote: adds colostomy, ascites, diuresis-route and stool-aspect fields
RULE-NUTRICAO-011.justification: The dietitian's extended abdominal-assessment enums (colostomy, ascites, diuresis route, stool aspect) are unescalated, verify:false descriptive form fields with no cross-copy comparison available, so they adopt as-is into the v2 clinical-forms abdominal assessment block.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-001.source_quote: unguarded overflow bug at the top of the day
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-001.justification: get_hora_cheia/justOclock is a homecare-specific hour-labeling utility with an unguarded 23:xx-hour overflow that manifests differently BE ("24:00") vs FE (silent "00:00"); IntensiCare v2 reads normalized timestamps from AMH Gold via Athena so this bespoke rounding utility has no home in the new stack.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-002.source_quote: keeps the first two words in full
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-002.justification: nome_abreviado_paciente crashes with IndexError on single-word or double-space names and has no authoritative external reference for its connective-aware (da/de/do/das/dos) abbreviation rule; UNVERIFIABLE band mandates ratification of the corrected display-name behavior before v2 patient-identity display adopts it.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-003.justification: Offline prescription grouping-by-day relies on itertools.groupby requiring pre-sorted input (confirmed safe here only because the upstream queryset is ordered by '-dia'); UNVERIFIABLE band mandates ratification that the v2 offline/replacement mechanism preserves this ordering contract rather than silently reintroducing the groupby hazard.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-004.source_quote: reflects a clinical shift boundary at 07:00
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-004.justification: The dia_real 07:00 shift-boundary rollover (>=07:00 same day, <07:00 next day) is an institutional nursing-shift convention with no universal published standard; UNVERIFIABLE band mandates ratification of the exact cutoff and inclusive-boundary semantics before v2 temporal windowing adopts it for prescription scheduling.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-005.source_quote: ordered by day descending, then by a computed
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-005.justification: The 3-day (QTD_DIAS=2 inclusive) offline retention window and the DIETA/MEDICAMENTO_SOLUÇÃO/SN_ACM/RECOMENDACAO_ENFERMAGEM/RECOMENDACAO priority ordering are proprietary business rules with no clinical reference; UNVERIFIABLE band mandates ratification of both the window length and the priority map before v2 carries them forward.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-006.source_quote: length of stay = days since admission
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-006.justification: TEMPO_PERMANENCIA computes length of stay as a calendar-date whole-day difference (not elapsed 24h periods) with no null guard and no authoritative reference distinguishing the two conventions; UNVERIFIABLE band mandates ratification of which LOS convention v2 dashboards must use.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-007.source_quote: computes total page count as ceil(count/page_size)
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-007.justification: Pagination page-count arithmetic (ceil(count/page_size), default page size 20, 0 on falsy inputs) is arbitrary internal UI convention with no external reference; UNVERIFIABLE band formally requires ratification even though the math itself is uncontroversial, so the default limit is confirmed rather than silently carried forward.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-008.source_quote: Computes whole minutes between two ISO/date strings
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-008.justification: minutesBetweenDates uses a date1-minus-date2 sign convention with Math.floor rounding toward -Infinity for negative intervals, a load-bearing but unreferenced time primitive likely feeding medication/observation timing math; UNVERIFIABLE band mandates ratification of the sign and rounding convention before v2 reuses it.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-009.source_quote: strips whitespace and, if it contains no ':' character
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-009.justification: format_horario treats ANY colon anywhere in the string as 'already formatted' rather than validating an HH:MM shape, an unreferenced internal normalization convention; UNVERIFIABLE band mandates ratification of whether v2 should tighten this validation or preserve the loose legacy check.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-010.source_quote: checking, IN ORDER, the count of
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-010.justification: parse_date_to_iso resolves date format purely by separator-count precedence checking '/' before '-', a fixed-order heuristic that can misroute mixed-separator strings into the wrong strptime pattern; UNVERIFIABLE band mandates ratification of the precedence order and the DD/MM (pt-BR) day-first assumption before v2 date parsing adopts it.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-011.source_quote: coerce a value to float; on ValueError or TypeError
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-011.justification: get_number silently coerces any non-numeric or missing value to 0 rather than surfacing an error, which risks masking a missing/invalid clinical measurement as a real zero reading; UNVERIFIABLE band mandates ratification of whether v2 should fail loudly instead of defaulting to 0.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-012.justification: The synthetic-data generator's 10-level RASS choice set (-5 through +4) matches the standard Richmond Agitation-Sedation Scale exactly, verified against Sessler 2002 and Ely 2003; this confirms the vocabulary IntensiCare v2's neuro-sedation domain must expose for RASS entry/display.
evidence: Sessler CN et al. Am J Respir Crit Care Med. 2002;166(10):1338-1344

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-013.justification: The synthetic-data generator's 3-tier ARDS/SDRA severity set (leve/moderada/grave) matches the Berlin Definition's mild/moderate/severe PaO2/FiO2 stratification verbatim; this PT-BR vocabulary is a controlled-vocabulary asset for IntensiCare v2's respiratory domain severity display.
evidence: ARDS Definition Task Force, Ranieri VM et al. JAMA. 2012;307(23):2526-2533

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-014.justification: assetlinks.json App-Link verification is scoped only to a single homol Android TWA package with no prod/demo entries, a legacy mobile-deploy artifact of the Tasy-era frontend; IntensiCare v2's ECS Fargate/AMH-consumer architecture carries no equivalent Android TWA distribution mechanism to preserve.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-015.source_quote: EXCLUDES records already belonging to that access group
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-015.justification: The grupo exclude-filter on SetorFilter/UsuarioFilter is a Django-ORM-specific admin-list plumbing detail (surface candidates not yet in an access group) with no clinical content; IntensiCare v2's access-group management, if any, will be redesigned against Lake Formation ABAC per ADR-001 rather than porting this filter class.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-016.source_quote: inclusive full-day date range on dt_registro
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-016.justification: The naive-datetime, time.min/time.max full-day filter on Formulario evolutions is a Django-filter-class mechanism tied to the legacy trilha_homecare model; it carries no clinical semantics beyond generic inclusive date-range filtering that v2's query layer will implement natively.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-017.justification: ParanoiaMixin.delete defaults admin-UI-path deletions to a REAL hard cascading delete unless soft_delete=True is explicitly passed, the inverse of a safety-first convention for a clinical-records system; AMBIGUOUS band plus the explicit product/security sign-off flag mandate ratification of the correct default before v2 designs its delete/audit semantics.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-018.source_quote: takes precedence over the inherited ParanoiaMixin.delete()
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-018.justification: SetUpModel.delete silently overrides ParanoiaMixin's cascade+admin-path delete via Python MRO, leaving cascading soft-delete and the admin hard-delete branch dead code for every model built on SetUpModel; the sound intent (auditable soft-delete with force_delete escape hatch) is worth keeping but must be reimplemented as one unambiguous mechanism, not a two-class MRO trap, for LGPD-compliant audit trails.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-019.justification: BalancoHidricoOfflineSerializer's last-2-records-per-occupied-bed slice with a dead _QT_DIAS=5 attribute is homecare-offline-sync-specific plumbing that also silently omits history for unoccupied beds; v2 reads fluid-balance data from AMH Gold rather than a bespoke offline cache, so this mechanism has no successor to adapt into.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-020.source_quote: draft forms authored by OTHER users are invisible to this user
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-020.justification: The author-only visibility of draft evolution forms versus universal visibility once status='liberado' is a sound deny-by-default authorship-privacy pattern worth preserving; the mechanism (offline Formulario queryset filtering) is legacy-specific and must be reimplemented against v2's own authorization model rather than ported as-is.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-021.justification: All four offline-sync views unconditionally resolve request.empresa via Empresa.objects.get(whitelabel='homecare'), an uncaught single-tenant hardcoding that raises a 500 if that row is absent or duplicated; this is exactly the multi-tenancy-hardcoding class the cluster brief flags as requiring v2 redesign, not a pattern to carry forward.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-022.justification: DadosOfflineView's AND-of-four membership check (patient, sector, establishment, company all independently required) is a sound deny-by-default, tenant-scoped access pattern worth keeping; the Django ORM filter-chain mechanism itself must be reimplemented against Lake Formation ABAC / v2's own authorization layer per ADR-001, not ported verbatim.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-023.justification: Restricting prescription/balance offline endpoints to occupied beds with tipo='homecare', without the setor/estabelecimento/empresa membership chain that DadosOfflineView enforces, is an inconsistent legacy homecare-offline-sync shortcut; v2 has no homecare-specific offline-cache tier to inherit this filter into.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-024.justification: Gating the can_delete_horario capability on a company-level permission set (get_permissoes_empresa) before exposing a destructive prescription-schedule action is a sound permission-gated-write pattern worth preserving; the concrete mechanism is legacy-specific and must be re-expressed through v2's own permission model, not carried over verbatim.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-025.justification: Forcing -c 1 concurrency on the seven pathway-mutating Celery queues across demo/homol/prod uwsgi configs is legacy PM2/uwsgi deploy plumbing tied to the current Celery worker topology; IntensiCare v2's ADR-001 stack (ECS Fargate, no proprietary task-queue mandate) will need its own concurrency-control design, not a ported uwsgi concurrency flag.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-026.justification: start.sh's production branch launches a uwsgi/uwsgi.ini file that does not exist in the repository (only uwsgi-prod.ini is tracked), a latent legacy deploy-script break; IntensiCare v2's ECS Fargate deployment per ADR-001 replaces uwsgi/start.sh entirely, so there is nothing here to adapt forward.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-027.justification: Deriving the runtime environment label from a substring match ("homol") on the API base URL is legacy frontend deploy plumbing specific to the trilhas-frontend build pipeline; v2's ECS Fargate deployment per ADR-001 will use explicit environment configuration, not URL string sniffing.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-028.source_quote: total record count is less than or equal to the current page size
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-028.justification: FooterPaginator's disable-when-count<=limit rule is a legacy React component's UI mechanics with no clinical content and no accounting for a zero/negative limit; v2's UI framework and component library will define its own pagination-control behavior rather than porting this widget's logic.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-029.source_quote: Handles overnight shifts (end <= start means the shift crosses midnight
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-029.justification: dateTurn's shift-day bucketing (overnight-shift midnight crossing, exclusive isBetween boundaries) is exactly the sound-intent-wrong-mechanism case CONTRACTS.md calls out for 07:00-07:00 shift windows; reconciliation confirmed it agrees with RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-004/035 for the 07:00 case, so v2 needs one correct, exclusive-boundary-free temporal-windowing implementation carrying the same clinical shift-day semantics.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-030.source_quote: fetching a maximum of 5 results per request
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-030.justification: The shared default limit=5 / 700ms debounce across the three legacy search-widget factories, including the non-memoized debounce-recreation-per-render quirk, is trilhas-frontend UI mechanics; v2's component library will define its own search-input defaults rather than porting this React hook pattern.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-031.source_quote: always requests exactly 5 results when the search keyword is empty
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-031.justification: CreateSearchInput's undocumented override to always request 5 results on an empty keyword, unlike its sibling CreateAutoComplete/CreateSearchTags widgets that honor the configured limit unconditionally, is a legacy UI-mechanics inconsistency confined to the trilhas-frontend search hooks; DISCREPANCY status forecloses plain adoption and there is no v2 target since the whole widget family is being rebuilt.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-032.source_quote: peep upper bound 41 here vs 40 in PeepValidator
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-032.justification: Escalation band P3 on an internal off-by-one (peep 41 vs 40) in a seed-data generator, not enforced production validation; still a discriminating discrepancy about which the correct numeric ceiling (40, per PeepValidator) needs ratification before any synthetic-data tooling is rebuilt for v2.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-033.source_quote: cadence in seconds at which automatic care pathways
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-033.justification: TEMPO_ATUALIZACAO_TRILHAS is a Django settings.py env-var cadence for the legacy pathway-recalculation scheduler; v2 pathway/alert scheduling is a new mechanism per ADR-001 platform change, so this legacy Django/env-var cadence definition is obsolete with the stack replacement.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-034.source_quote: disables selecting or advancing to any date beyond the current clinical shift-turnover boundary
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-034.justification: The clinical intent — pinning date-navigation controls to the current 07:00-07:00 shift-turnover boundary so users cannot browse into an unstarted shift — is sound and worth preserving, but the legacy React/moment.js CustomDatePicker mechanism is replaced by the new v2 frontend stack's date-navigation component.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-035.source_quote: if the current local hour is 7 (07:00) or later, the reporting day is today's date
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-035.justification: The 07:00-to-07:00 ICU shift/reporting-day convention is a durable clinical-operations semantic worth carrying forward, but the legacy stdlib datetime.now().astimezone() implementation (which bypasses Django's TIME_ZONE-aware now()) must be corrected to explicit America/Sao_Paulo timezone handling in the v2 mechanism.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-036.justification: Escalation band P3 flags that custom_exception_handler's flatten_errors uses collections.MutableMapping, which raises AttributeError on Python >=3.10 despite working under the legacy pinned 3.9 runtime; whether the DRF error-envelope shape itself (errors/status/exception) is worth preserving in the new stack needs a ratified decision since it is Django/DRF-specific plumbing.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-037.source_quote: representation behavior depends on whether the serializer was instantiated WITH a context
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-037.justification: AMBIGUOUS band on ListChoiceField.to_representation: the intent behind switching label-vs-raw-value representation solely on serializer context presence is not recoverable from this partition alone and could hide a meaningful UI-vs-API contract that v2 API design must replicate or intentionally drop.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-038.source_quote: service worker (public/sw.js) and workbox chunks (public/workbox-*.js) are explicitly gitignored
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-038.justification: This is Next.js/next-pwa build-tooling configuration (service-worker generation gated on NODE_ENV) for the legacy trilhas-frontend PWA; the v2 platform's frontend build/deploy mechanism is a new stack per ADR-001, so this specific PWA toggle is obsolete legacy-app plumbing, not a clinical or architectural asset.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-039.source_quote: three named PM2 applications, one per deploy target: trilhas-demo
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-039.justification: PM2 multi-environment process management for the legacy Next.js frontend is replaced by ECS Fargate deployment in the shared AMH VPC/accounts per ADR-001 Implicacao 8, which supersedes PM2 process orchestration entirely with container-orchestrated tasks; the per-environment port/env separation concept is lost in favor of ECS task definitions.
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-039.target: SUPERSEDE: ADR-001 ECS Fargate deployment mechanism

supersedes_note.replaced_by: ECS Fargate task definitions per ADR-001 Implicacao 8
supersedes_note.lost: PM2 watch-based file-change auto-restart and named per-environment process identity (trilhas-demo/prod/homol)

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-040.source_quote: only the homol and demo variants wrap the `next build` (and `next dev`) command with `env-cmd
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-040.justification: AMBIGUOUS band on whether prod's missing env-cmd wrapper reflects intentional reliance on Next's native .env.production loading or a genuine environment-variable gap in the legacy deploy scripts; this must be ratified because it could hide a real production configuration risk worth flagging before the v2 CI/CD pipeline is designed.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-041.source_quote: each deploy target's secrets file must be provisioned directly on that target's server/host out-of-band
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-041.justification: The underlying lesson — per-environment secrets must never be committed to version control and must be provisioned out-of-band — is a security pattern worth keeping, but the mechanism (per-env .env files loaded via env-cmd on bare hosts) is replaced by AWS Secrets Manager / KMS-backed secrets inherited from the AMH Data Platform security model.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-042.source_quote: short_name/name = "teleUTI" (i.e. the product's actual name is "teleUTI"
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-042.justification: The legacy PWA manifest.json (app identity 'teleUTI', icon set, standalone display mode) is presentation-layer plumbing tied to the retired Next.js/next-pwa frontend build; IntensiCare v2's product identity and installable-app configuration will be defined fresh in the new frontend stack, not carried from this legacy manifest.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-043.source_quote: any path segment whose string length is exactly 36 characters
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-043.justification: Breadcrumb-hides-UUID is a narrow legacy React component convention (Breadcrumbs.tsx) tied to the retired trilhas-frontend routing structure; the v2 frontend will define its own breadcrumb/routing scheme, so this length-based UUID-hiding heuristic (which is itself imprecise, matching by length rather than UUID shape) is obsolete with the platform change rather than a reusable asset.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-044.source_quote: clears the "assistido" (attended) flag on automatic trilhas that were updated in the last minute
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-044.justification: This Celery task resets an 'assistido' (attended-to) flag on legacy 'trilhas' (automatic care pathway) rows using Django ORM queries against the ahlabs-trilhas schema; v2's correlation-engine/alert-acknowledgement workflow is a new mechanism per ADR-001 and vision, so this Django-specific attended-flag reset job does not carry forward as-is.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-045.source_quote: scanning interactive sepsis trilhas for overdue checklist items and emitting a delay observation message
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-045.justification: The clinical operational intent — detecting overdue sepsis-pathway checklist items and surfacing a delay notification to care staff — is worth preserving in the v2 sepsis domain's correlation/alerting mechanism, but the legacy Celery task scanning Django TrilhaInterativaSepse rows is replaced by the new AMH Gold-consuming alert engine.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-046.justification: The lesson of isolating queues per deployment environment on a shared broker is sound, but Celery/RabbitMQ queue routing-key namespacing is superseded by the AMH Data Platform's own MSK topic/queue conventions and the new v2 messaging mechanism per ADR-001, which does not use Celery/RabbitMQ.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: AMH Data Platform MSK topics and Silver/Gold pipeline routing per ADR-001
supersedes_note.lost: Celery/RabbitMQ per-environment queue naming convention and the specific 12-queue enumeration

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-047.source_quote: TWO beat schedulers would run concurrently against the same database-backed schedule
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-047.justification: Escalation band P3 on a real duplicate-beat-daemon bug (demo/homol-old uwsgi configs run both a standalone Celery beat daemon and an embedded -B flag, double-firing periodic tasks); whether the demo environment is currently affected and whether any downstream clinical automation was double-triggered needs ratification before being dismissed as pure legacy plumbing.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-048.source_quote: automated ICU pathway engine (trilha_automatica) and homecare scoring engines (trilha_homecare) have NO CI test gate
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-048.justification: The core lesson — that the automated ICU pathway/scoring engines must have CI regression coverage, not just the manual-pathway app — is a critical quality-assurance gap to correct in v2's test strategy, even though the legacy GitHub Actions workflow (Python 3.8/Postgres 10/RabbitMQ) itself is retired with the platform change.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-049.source_quote: nursing shift running 07:00 to 07:00 (24h window starting at 7am)
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-049.justification: The 07:00-to-07:00 nursing-shift day-window convention for filtering prescriptions is a durable clinical-operations semantic to preserve in the v2 prescription/medication-review screens, but the legacy moment.js dateTurn implementation (with its same-start/end edge-case handling) is replaced by a corrected v2 temporal-windowing utility.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-050.source_quote: restricts Next.js's built-in Image Optimization (`next/image`) to only fetch/optimize remote images whose host is
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-050.justification: The security control this encodes — an explicit allow-list of trusted remote-image hostnames to mitigate SSRF-style abuse of an image-optimization proxy — is a deny-by-default pattern worth preserving, but the specific Next.js next/image domain whitelist mechanism is tied to the retired frontend stack and must be re-implemented against whatever v2 storage/CDN backends are chosen.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-051.source_quote: structure the company > establishment > sector > bed hierarchy and identity fields
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-051.justification: The tenant-hierarchy uniqueness model (company > establishment > sector > bed, scoped-not-global codes, plus username/cpf uniqueness) is a sound structural asset for v2's multi-tenant data model, but the concrete Django unique_together mechanism must be re-implemented as v2's own schema constraints against operational PostgreSQL/TimescaleDB per ADR-001.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-052.source_quote: returns observations on that bed OR replies whose parent observation is on that bed
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-052.justification: This is a narrow Django-ORM filter-composition detail (ObservacaoFilter.filter_leito using Q(leito=value) | Q(resposta__leito=value)) specific to the legacy observation/reply data model; it is legacy-app query plumbing that does not carry forward as a distinct architectural or clinical asset once the underlying observation schema is redesigned for v2.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-053.source_quote: if ANY field in a combo is falsy (None/empty) on self, that field is omitted from the filter
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-053.justification: UniqueTogetherManagerMixin.save is a legacy Django model-mixin implementation detail (with a documented edge-case gap where blank-component combos silently skip enforcement) specific to the ahlabs-trilhas ORM layer; v2's own PostgreSQL/TimescaleDB schema will express uniqueness via native DB constraints rather than porting this Python-level pre-save check mixin.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-054.source_quote: checks independently (not combined) whether another non-deleted row has the same value
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-054.justification: UniqueManagerMixin.save is the single-field counterpart to RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-053, another legacy Django model-mixin implementation detail of the ahlabs-trilhas ORM layer; it is retired alongside the rest of the custom uniqueness-mixin machinery in favor of native PostgreSQL/TimescaleDB constraints in v2.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-055.justification: The 4-category gender enum (M/F/O/N) is a controlled vocabulary asset used by the synthetic test-data generator and worth carrying into v2's patient-demographics form vocabulary; status is OK and verdict is NOT_APPLICABLE with no discrepancy, so plain adoption is appropriate for this enum's values.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-056.source_quote: presence-of-diagnosis gates unsatisfiable and absence-of-diagnosis gates vacuously satisfied
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-056.justification: Escalation band P3 on a systemic vars().fromkeys() misuse across multiple pathway files (trilha_sepse, trilha_estabilidade, trilha_eficiencia, trilha_profilaxia) making diagnosis-presence checks always-false and absence-checks always-true; because this silently defeats diagnosis-based clinical pathway gating, the intended diagnosis-matching logic must be ratified before any v2 sepsis/pathway criterion is authored from this source.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-057.source_quote: storage sub-folder prefix for an uploaded file based on the type of the owning model instance
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-057.justification: upload_to's model-type-to-folder-prefix convention is a narrow Django FileField storage-path helper tied to the legacy Usuario/ObservacaoArquivo/Empresa/Formulario model set; v2's file-storage layout will be designed against the new AWS-native storage backends inherited per ADR-001, not this Django upload_to callback.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-058.source_quote: validates that its estabelecimento's empresa matches the given empresa
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-058.justification: The deny-by-default tenant-hierarchy consistency check (a setor must belong to the same empresa as its parent estabelecimento) is a security pattern worth preserving in v2's authorization model, though the concrete Django handler function and its duplication with middleware-level checks should be consolidated into a single v2 ABAC enforcement point per the inherited Lake Formation ABAC model.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-059.source_quote: its unit (seconds vs milliseconds) and the code that actually applies it are not present
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-059.justification: AMBIGUOUS band on the per-company tempo_atualizacao auto-refresh interval field: neither its unit (seconds vs milliseconds) nor its consuming logic is recoverable from this partition, so whether v2's UI polling/auto-refresh design should preserve a per-tenant configurable interval at all needs ratification rather than a guessed default.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-060.source_quote: force-routed to the legacy Oracle 'Tasy' EMR database
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-060.justification: TasyModel's forced Oracle-connection routing (reads via TasyModelManager.using('oracle'), writes via save() overriding kwargs['using']) is direct Tasy-database coupling that ADR-001 explicitly forbids — Intensicare MUST NOT implement its own ingestion/direct-DB access to source systems and MUST read clinical data only from the AMH Gold layer via Athena, so this legacy Oracle-routing mechanism is retired outright.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-061.source_quote: capping the maximum size of an incoming request body Django will read
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-061.justification: The operational control of a 100 MiB request-body upload cap for clinical PDF/attachment and form uploads is a worthwhile safeguard to reproduce, but the concrete Django DATA_UPLOAD_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE setting must be re-implemented as an equivalent limit in v2's new API framework/ECS Fargate ingress configuration.

RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-062.source_quote: workers=4/processes=4; demo runs workers=2/processes=2; old runs workers=2/processes=4
RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-062.justification: Per-environment uWSGI worker/process capacity, max-requests recycling, and harakiri timeout thresholds are legacy bare-metal/VM process-management configuration for the retired Django/uwsgi deployment; ECS Fargate task sizing and health-check/timeout configuration per ADR-001 Implicacao 8 replaces this mechanism entirely, so these specific numeric thresholds do not carry forward.

RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-001.source_quote: No 2+/2- band exists for FR (magnitude jumps
RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-001.justification: UNVERIFIABLE escalation band mandates ratification; the respiratory-rate sub-score is directionally sound for both high and low RR but no published source fixes these exact proprietary cutoffs or the 1->3 magnitude jump with no 2-band, so clinical review must confirm the bands before v2 early-warning-score encoding.

RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-002.justification: The axillary-temperature sub-score's 1+ (37.6-37.9) and 2+ (38.0-38.2) bands leave a contiguity gap so 37.95C scores '0' instead of an elevated grade; v2 must adopt the corrected contiguous banding (e.g. 1+ extended up to <38.0) while keeping the same NEWS2-family fever/hypothermia direction and the unaffected 3-/1-/3+ bands.

RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-003.justification: UNVERIFIABLE escalation band mandates ratification; this is the only fully six-banded, directionally coherent sub-score in the cluster, but its proprietary PAS cutoffs are not traceable to NEWS2 (which does not score hypertension until >=220), so clinical review must confirm the exact bands, including the PAS=80 boundary that the facade label describes differently, before adoption.

RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-004.justification: The heart-rate sub-score has a branch-order bug that shadows fc==50 into the mild '1-' grade instead of the severe '3-' bradycardia grade because '50<=fc<=59' is checked before 'fc<=50'; the ADOPT-CORRECTED fix is restricted to that branch-order defect alone, reordering the bounds so 'fc<=50' (severe, '3-') is evaluated before '50<=fc<=59' (mild, '1-') and fc==50 correctly scores '3-'. The commented-out '2-' band (81<=fc<=99) must remain retired, not reinstated: 81-99 bpm is a normal/high-normal resting heart rate, NEWS2/MEWS grade 0, so scoring it as low-deviation bradycardia would itself be clinically wrong.

RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-005.source_quote: The value "acordado" (alert) and any unmapped value yield "0".
RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-005.justification: UNVERIFIABLE escalation band mandates ratification; the consciousness sub-score's high-deviation side aligns well with the AVPU/NEWS2 ladder but the proprietary low-side enum (sonolencia/agitacao/convulsao grading) has no single authoritative anchor, so clinical review must confirm the mapping before the PT-BR enum and its grades are adopted verbatim into v2 neuro-sedation scoring.

RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-006.justification: P0 escalation band mandates ratification; the buggy compound comparison `7 <= dor > 10` makes the severe-pain (NRS 7-10) band structurally unreachable so severe pain is silently scored as normal, a high clinical-impact defect that must be ratified before v2 pain-alerting encodes the corrected 7<=dor<=10 boundary.
evidence: Boonstra AM et al. NRS pain cut-offs Front Psychol 2016;7:1466

RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-007.justification: P0 escalation band mandates ratification; the identical bug pattern to criterio_6 makes the Behavioral Pain Scale's severe band (10-12) unreachable via `10 <= sinais > 12`, so maximal-pain patients score '0' on this sub-scale, a high-impact defect requiring clinical sign-off on the corrected 10<=sinais<=12 boundary before v2 encodes it.
evidence: Payen JF et al. Behavioral Pain Scale Crit Care Med 2001;29(12):2258-63

RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-008.source_quote: clinically inverted for a regular patient
RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-008.justification: P1 escalation band mandates ratification; the non-COPD SpO2 sub-score inverts NEWS2 Scale 1 intent by scoring normal/high saturation (>96%) as a positive 'hiperoxia' deterioration grade, and it double-counts the same saturacao_o2 field also read by criterio_9 with no mutual exclusivity, so clinicians must confirm correct polarity before v2 respiratory scoring.
evidence: Royal College of Physicians NEWS2 2017 SpO2 Scale 1

RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-009.source_quote: Penalizing high SpO2 (>94 -> 3+) is intentional for COPD.
RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-009.justification: P1 escalation band mandates ratification; although penalizing high SpO2 in COPD patients is directionally intentional and aligns with NEWS2 Scale 2 rationale, it shares the saturacao_o2 field with criterio_8 with no mutual exclusivity between regular/DPOC pathways, double-weighting SpO2 in the aggregate, so clinicians must confirm the pathway-selection mechanism before v2 encodes it.
evidence: Royal College of Physicians NEWS2 2017 SpO2 Scale 2

RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-010.source_quote: can DOWNGRADE an earlier grade-3 one
RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-010.justification: P0 escalation band mandates ratification; the aggregate alert has three internal defects -- last-writer-wins overwrite that can downgrade a VERMELHO to AMARELO, sign-discarding magnitude-only summation, and an unreachable 15-21 sum band -- any one of which can silently suppress a critical deterioration alert, so the corrected max-severity-wins, sign-preserving design must be clinically ratified.
evidence: Royal College of Physicians NEWS2 2017 two-tier trigger logic

RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-011.justification: AMBIGUOUS status and UNVERIFIABLE band both mandate ratification, and this payload is clinically worth keeping -- it carries actionable protocol text (fluid-challenge volumes, sepsis-protocol trigger, antipyretic routing) for criterio_1-3 only, while criterio_4-9 remain label-only and the PAS<80 label mismatches the pas<=80 predicate, so clinicians must resolve coverage and wording before v2 adopts it.
evidence: Evans L et al. Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021 Crit Care Med 2021;49(11):e1063-e1143

RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-012.justification: This is legacy Django-ORM signal-style plumbing that unconditionally spawns a worsening record on every vital-signs insert regardless of values; under ADR-001's AMH Gold/Athena-consuming architecture, score computation is triggered by the ingestion/scoring pipeline rather than an implicit per-INSERT side-effect, so v2 supersedes this mechanism with pipeline-triggered score computation.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: AMH Data Platform ingestion-triggered score-computation pipeline (per ADR-001 medallion architecture)
supersedes_note.lost: implicit synchronous unconditional per-vital-sign-insert record creation regardless of value

RULE-PRESCRICAO-001.source_quote: ml medications capture exported quantity for fluid balance
RULE-PRESCRICAO-001.justification: Escalation band UNVERIFIABLE mandates ratification regardless of status OK: whether a mandatory ml-quantity capture field for fluid-balance export is still needed under v2's design (which may compute balanco hidrico differently from FHIR-sourced administration events) is a product decision with no guideline to cite, so a human must rule on it before design proceeds.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-002.source_quote: shadowed, never-executed first definition with inverted logic
RULE-PRESCRICAO-002.justification: Status DISCREPANCY forbids plain ADOPT; this dose-level get_suspenso check is legacy home-care MAR internals (dead inverted-logic sibling methods, scope disagreement with RULE-PRESCRICAO-003) with no analog in IntensiCare v2, which per ADR-001 only reads FHIR MedicationRequest from AMH Gold rather than running its own dose-suspension state engine.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-003.justification: Status DISCREPANCY forbids plain ADOPT; this order-level now()-based suspension flag disagrees in scope with the dose-level check (RULE-PRESCRICAO-002) and is legacy home-care MAR plumbing with no counterpart in IntensiCare v2's read-only AMH Gold/FHIR consumption model per ADR-001, so it is retired rather than corrected.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-004.source_quote: the target balance-day is determined by a 07:00 shift boundary
RULE-PRESCRICAO-004.justification: Sound intent (a shift boundary decides which day's ledger a late-night dose export books into) but wrong mechanism: the cluster ledger constraint CLU-PRESCRICAO-C-02 explicitly requires v2 to replace the server-wall-clock 07:00 boundary with an encounter/bed-timezone-aware policy rather than porting datetime.now().astimezone() as-is, so the fluid-balance domain redesigns the boundary logic while keeping its day-bucketing semantics.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-005.source_quote: doses timed before 07:00 are grouped/sorted under the FOLLOWING calendar day
RULE-PRESCRICAO-005.justification: Unlike RULE-PRESCRICAO-004, this rule's 07:00 boundary only reorders a home-care dose-time display list, with no fluid-balance or clinical output attached, so it is purely legacy home-care UI sort mechanics that has no screen to carry forward in IntensiCare v2's ICU alerting/monitoring surface.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-006.source_quote: the number of days an offline prescription is considered valid
RULE-PRESCRICAO-006.justification: A bare constant (QTD_DIAS=2) with no confirmed caller in the audited repo tree, governing offline-prescription validity windows for the legacy home-care app; there is no offline-prescription concept in IntensiCare v2's architecture, which only consumes clinical data read-only from the AMH Gold layer, so the constant retires with the feature it configured.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-007.source_quote: any other type gets a NULL priority
RULE-PRESCRICAO-007.justification: Status DISCREPANCY forbids plain ADOPT; this 5-tier Tasy item-type sort priority diverges in soft-delete handling and secondary sort between its two call sites and only governs the ordering of a legacy prescription-listing/PDF screen, a home-care UI concern with no ICU clinical-decision content for v2 to inherit.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-008.source_quote: branches into one of four mutually exclusive flows
RULE-PRESCRICAO-008.justification: This administer/cancel/reschedule state machine, with its checador assignment, cryptocubo signature attempt, and AMH Docs resend side effects, is a home-care medication-administration write-path tied entirely to the legacy Tasy-backed stack; ADR-001 makes IntensiCare v2 a read-only consumer of AMH Gold FHIR MedicationRequest with no own administration-record engine, so this mechanism has no target to carry forward into.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-009.source_quote: rendered with a tri-state status icon
RULE-PRESCRICAO-009.justification: Pure PDF-rendering decision tree mapping administrado/motivo flags to an icon and actor-name label for the legacy prescription-check document; this document and its rendering pipeline do not exist in IntensiCare v2, which produces neither prescription PDFs nor its own administration bookkeeping.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-010.source_quote: materialises today's continuous prescriptions from the Tasy-sourced Prescricao
RULE-PRESCRICAO-010.justification: A Celery task that reads Tasy-mirrored Prescricao columns directly and materializes today's PrescricaoContinua rows is Tasy-direct ETL/scheduling internals of exactly the kind ADR-001 excludes: IntensiCare v2 MUST NOT implement its own ingestion and instead reads clinical data only from the AMH Gold layer via Athena, so this generation task retires with the legacy stack.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-011.justification: A fixed priority ladder mapping dose-state flags to a display color/tag on the legacy home-care schedule screen; purely presentational, order-sensitive UI logic with no clinical semantics of its own, tied to a screen IntensiCare v2's read-only ICU alerting surface does not replicate.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-012.source_quote: decides the backend operation for a horario from the presence of horarioId
RULE-PRESCRICAO-012.justification: A frontend dispatcher choosing POST/PATCH/DELETE from the presence of horarioId and body for the legacy home-care check UI; pure client-side request-routing plumbing with no clinical content and no equivalent write surface, since IntensiCare v2 does not perform medication-administration writes.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-013.source_quote: the primary button offers a two-way choice
RULE-PRESCRICAO-013.justification: Modal button branching distinguishing a full check-submission from a time-only reschedule in the legacy home-care check form; this is interaction-only UI logic for a screen and workflow that IntensiCare v2, a read-only ICU decision-support platform, does not implement.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-014.source_quote: explicitly returns None (not False) when user/empresa
RULE-PRESCRICAO-014.justification: Status DISCREPANCY forbids plain ADOPT; the two serializers diverge on missing-context fallback (None vs. False) for delete authorization, so while the delete-a-scheduled-dose feature itself retires with the legacy MAR, the deny-by-default-on-missing-context lesson is worth carrying into v2's own authorization layer as an explicit, consistent fallback rather than an ambiguous None.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-015.source_quote: rendered only when horario.can_delete is true
RULE-PRESCRICAO-015.justification: Button-visibility gate for the delete-a-scheduled-dose control whose underlying feature (RULE-PRESCRICAO-014) has no place in IntensiCare v2's read-only architecture, so this UI-rendering rule for the legacy home-care check modal retires alongside the capability it exposes.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-016.source_quote: rendered only when the prescription is not suspended AND the user has can_manage_prescricao
RULE-PRESCRICAO-016.justification: Permission-and-state gate for an add-new-administration-time button in the legacy home-care prescribing UI; the add-horario feature itself does not exist in IntensiCare v2's monitoring/alerting scope, so this specific visibility rule retires with the screen it controls, unlike the general permission-gating pattern preserved separately in RULE-PRESCRICAO-018.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-017.source_quote: shown only to the user who originally checked the horario
RULE-PRESCRICAO-017.justification: Status OK, but the cluster ledger constraint CLU-PRESCRICAO-C-03 names checador-only revert authorization a load-bearing invariant; the originator-only-may-undo pattern generalizes beyond home-care dose-checking to any reversible clinician action IntensiCare v2 offers (e.g., alert-acknowledgment undo), so the mechanism is redesigned for the new domain rather than ported as a home-care-specific feature.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-018.source_quote: the horario check/dots buttons are hidden and the horario is rendered view-only
RULE-PRESCRICAO-018.justification: Status OK, and the cluster ledger constraint CLU-PRESCRICAO-C-03 flags can_manage_prescricao gating as a load-bearing invariant; the general degrade-to-view-only-rather-than-hide pattern for permission-gated controls is carried into v2's own RBAC-gated clinical screens, even though the specific home-care administration controls it currently gates are retired.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-019.justification: Status OK and verdict NOT_APPLICABLE permit plain ADOPT; this is a controlled pharmacist-form vocabulary (high-risk drug-class multicheck, adherence enum with conditional reason, reconciliation-decision and completeness enums) rather than app plumbing, matching the cluster brief's characterization of the pharmacist sub-theme as a vocabulary asset to preserve verbatim in v2's clinical forms.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-020.source_quote: medication administration time-slots are auto-generated from the prescription's DS_HORARIOS string
RULE-PRESCRICAO-020.justification: Schedule-row generation parsed directly out of a Tasy-mirrored DS_HORARIOS column is ETL-adjacent legacy home-care internals; IntensiCare v2 only reads FHIR MedicationRequest from the AMH Gold layer per ADR-001 and generates no dose-schedule rows of its own, so this mechanism has nothing to carry forward into.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-021.source_quote: marks each corresponding schedule slot as administered (administrado=True)
RULE-PRESCRICAO-021.justification: A bulk medication-checkoff PATCH endpoint (no transaction wrapper, shared-dict mutation across loop iterations) for the legacy home-care MAR; IntensiCare v2 performs no medication-administration writes of its own under ADR-001's read-only AMH Gold/FHIR consumption model, so this endpoint and its bug surface retire with the platform it belongs to.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-022.source_quote: injected into the payload from nested-route kwargs
RULE-PRESCRICAO-022.justification: Django REST nested-route kwarg-to-payload injection is legacy Tasy-direct API view plumbing; ADR-001 bars Intensicare from operating this kind of proprietary write-path API, so this mechanism has no v2 home.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-023.source_quote: A suspended horario cannot be checked, edited, or acted upon
RULE-PRESCRICAO-023.justification: This is React button/tag click-handler gating for a legacy homecare medication-administration-record screen; v2 has no homecare MAR UI in scope, only ICU FHIR-sourced alerting, so the mechanism retires with the platform even though the underlying safety intent is sound.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-024.justification: Quick-check button PATCH shortcut is homecare-MAR frontend UI mechanics tied to HorarioCheck.tsx; the v2 platform consumes AMH Gold data and does not implement its own dose check-off UI, so this control has no v2 target.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-025.source_quote: exported quantity defaults to the prescribed quantity
RULE-PRESCRICAO-025.justification: This is a legacy React form payload-shaping transform (CheckModalContent) for a homecare administration-check modal; it is UI plumbing specific to a screen the v2 ICU alerting platform does not build, so it retires with the platform change.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-026.source_quote: requires a mandatory free-text cancellation justification
RULE-PRESCRICAO-026.justification: The mechanism (a specific homecare React modal) retires, but the underlying pattern of requiring a mandatory free-text justification before reversing prior clinical documentation is an accountability/audit lesson worth preserving in v2's alert-acknowledgment and correction workflows.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-027.source_quote: omits `label` and instead has a stray `Data` property
RULE-PRESCRICAO-027.justification: The antibiotic course tracking fields (start/end dates, renal/hepatic dose-adjustment note and date, positive-culture note) are a clinically valuable documentation asset for antimicrobial stewardship, but the legacy form descriptor is malformed (stray 'Data' key instead of 'label') and must be fixed, not ported broken, into the v2 clinical form.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-028.justification: This documents the shape of a homecare Horario lifecycle (administrado/suspenso/motivo/can_delete/qtd_exportada billing export) with an admittedly unenforced validation gap; it overlaps billing/pharmacy-export plumbing outside v2's ICU clinical-decision-support scope, so it is not clinically worth ratifying and retires with the platform.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-029.source_quote: the FRONTEND enum defines FOUR options including
RULE-PRESCRICAO-029.justification: The PT-BR reason-for-non-administration vocabulary is a load-bearing controlled vocabulary (CLU-PRESCRICAO-C-01) that must be preserved verbatim, but the legacy frontend (4 options incl. 'outros') and backend (3 options, keyless-dict KeyError on unmapped values) diverge; v2 must adopt a single reconciled enum that supports all four labels without a crash path, never the broken three-vs-four split.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-030.justification: The requirement that a not-administered dose carry a selected reason, with mandatory free text when 'outros' is chosen, is sound clinical documentation intent; the legacy Ant Design conditional-required-field mechanism retires but the same validation semantics (reason required, free-text required for 'outros') must be reimplemented against the reconciled enum from RULE-PRESCRICAO-029.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-031.justification: A required HH:mm time-picker field on a legacy 'add new horario' modal is homecare-MAR frontend scaffolding (AsButtonModalContent) with no equivalent screen in the v2 ICU alerting platform, so the control itself retires with the platform change.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-032.source_quote: if that user is missing a ValidationError is raised
RULE-PRESCRICAO-032.justification: Defaulting an unset creator field to a hard-coded seed user named 'sistema' is Django-model plumbing specific to the legacy homecare schema and its Tasy-fed auto-generation job; ADR-001's AMH-consumer model has no equivalent write-path model needing a system-author fallback.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-033.source_quote: only listable/retrievable when scoped to their parent continuous prescription id
RULE-PRESCRICAO-033.justification: Nested-route queryset scoping with soft-delete exclusion is Django REST viewset plumbing for the legacy HorariosPrescricao API; Intensicare v2 reads clinical data from AMH Gold via Athena rather than operating its own nested prescription-schedule REST resource, so this scoping mechanism has no v2 counterpart.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-034.source_quote: can only be cancelled by the same user who originally checked/administered it
RULE-PRESCRICAO-034.justification: Checador-only cancellation authorization is flagged as a load-bearing invariant in the cluster brief (CLU-PRESCRICAO-C-03, RULE-017/034/018) worth preserving; the legacy DRF serializer static-method mechanism retires, but v2 must reimplement the same 'only the actor who checked/acknowledged may reverse it' authorization pattern for alert acknowledgment/reversal actions.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-035.source_quote: is only ever invoked from a call site that has already guaranteed
RULE-PRESCRICAO-035.justification: validar_motivo's guard is provably unreachable because its sole call site pre-verifies motivo_nao_administrado is truthy before invoking it; this is dead legacy validation code in a serializer that retires with the platform, and its intended requirement is already correctly enforced client-side by RULE-PRESCRICAO-030.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-036.source_quote: but this method is not called anywhere within the files examined
RULE-PRESCRICAO-036.justification: validar_alteracao_dado encodes a real once-set-cannot-be-altered immutability rule for administration status but is unused in this partition and its call sites elsewhere are unconfirmed; whether v2's alert/administration audit trail should enforce this immutability invariant is a data-governance decision agents cannot resolve unilaterally.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-037.source_quote: risk categories present but their required-detail logic is disabled
RULE-PRESCRICAO-037.justification: The riscos_assistenciais multicheck vocabulary (queda, alergia, sangramento, trombose_venosa, delirium, broncoaspiracao, flebite, alteracao_glicemica, lpp, outras_lesoes) is a controlled clinical asset shared across disciplines, but the pharmacist form's detail-capture conditionals for alergia/lpp/outras_lesoes are commented out unlike the nursing equivalent; v2 must adopt the vocabulary with the detail fields re-enabled, not the disabled legacy behavior.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-038.source_quote: Free-text prophylaxis fields covering constipation/diarrhea, delirium
RULE-PRESCRICAO-038.justification: The prophylaxis category list (constipation/diarrhea, delirium, glycemic control, tube meds, VTE/TEV, stress-ulcer, analgesia, sedation) maps onto v2 domains (neuro-sedation, electrolyte, hemodynamics) as a documentation checklist; the legacy free-text-only capture is worth adapting into structured fields consistent with those domains rather than porting unstructured strings verbatim.
