RULE-PRESCRICAO-039.source_quote: is almost certainly a typo for 'alergia' (allergy)
RULE-PRESCRICAO-039.justification: The 33-option pharmacist-intervention vocabulary is a genuine controlled-vocabulary asset worth carrying into v2 clinical forms, but it contains persisted data-entry typos (sinalizacao_de_alegria, substitiicao, via_de_administracco, profixia) that must be corrected rather than ported verbatim, since silently inheriting a mistranslation of 'alergia' into a live enum is a patient-safety-adjacent labeling risk.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-040.source_quote: declares and exports a const named dataFormFisioterapeuta
RULE-PRESCRICAO-040.justification: A pharmacist form module exporting a symbol misnamed dataFormFisioterapeuta is a purely cosmetic legacy naming/traceability hazard with no clinical content of its own; it retires with the source file rather than being carried into v2's clean-slate form definitions.

RULE-PRESCRICAO-041.source_quote: can be filtered by an exact-match 'dia' (day) query parameter
RULE-PRESCRICAO-041.justification: An exact-match django-filter FilterSet wired into a Tasy-fed PrescricaoViewSet is legacy backend API plumbing; Intensicare v2 does not operate its own PrescricaoContinua REST resource against AMH Gold, so this filter mechanism has no v2 target and retires with the platform.

RULE-PROFILAXIA-001.source_quote: Subcutaneous insulin regimen for recurrent hyperglycemia in the ICU
RULE-PROFILAXIA-001.justification: The NPH 0.3-0.5 U/kg/day plus correction-scale insulin regimen is VERIFIED against ADA/RABBIT-2 inpatient glycemic protocols with no discrepancy and no escalation, so the dosing coefficients (CON-0188) adopt verbatim into the v2 pharmacotherapy domain.
evidence: Umpierrez et al. RABBIT-2, Diabetes Care 2007

RULE-PROFILAXIA-002.justification: The renal/BMI-adjusted VTE dosing (enoxaparina 40/60mg, UFH 5000U q8h/q12h) is VERIFIED against ACCP/ASH VTE prophylaxis guidance with the 8/8h-vs-12/12h split between criterio_4/criterio_5 explicitly documented as an intra-rule, non-divergent difference, so it adopts unchanged into the v2 pharmacotherapy domain.
evidence: ACCP/CHEST Antithrombotic Therapy for VTE Prophylaxis

RULE-PROFILAXIA-003.source_quote: criterio_1 raises AMARELO, criterio_4 or criterio_9 raise VERMELHO
RULE-PROFILAXIA-003.justification: The v1 amarelo/vermelho count-based aggregator (criterio_1 -> AMARELO; criterio_4 or criterio_9 -> VERMELHO) is an internal, unverified (verify:false) trilha-specific alert-coloring heuristic with no published anchor, so it is superseded by the v2 Alert Engine's uniform severity taxonomy rather than ported as a bespoke per-pathway aggregator.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: v2 Alert Engine 4-tier severity scale (normal/watch/urgent/critical) applied uniformly across domains
supersedes_note.lost: the v1-specific vermelho tier membership {criterio_4, criterio_9}, which becomes moot once criteria map onto the unified severity model

RULE-PROFILAXIA-004.source_quote: criterio_1 raises AMARELO, criterio_9 raises VERMELHO
RULE-PROFILAXIA-004.justification: The v3 amarelo/vermelho aggregator narrows vermelho to criterio_9 alone (criterio_4 commented out) yet is otherwise the same unverified, unescalated per-trilha coloring heuristic as v1 (RULE-PROFILAXIA-003), so it is likewise superseded by the v2 Alert Engine's uniform severity taxonomy instead of preserving two divergent version-specific aggregators.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: v2 Alert Engine 4-tier severity scale (normal/watch/urgent/critical) applied uniformly across domains
supersedes_note.lost: the v3-narrowed vermelho tier (criterio_9 only) and the save()-time assistido reset side-effect on NEUTRO

RULE-PROFILAXIA-005.source_quote: no PPI/cimetidine prescribed AND at least one stress-ulcer prophylaxis indication present
RULE-PROFILAXIA-005.justification: criterio_1's stress-ulcer predicate carries a P1 escalation (ESC-P1-049) and DISCREPANCY verdict for three verified implementation-vs-docstring gaps (48h->50h, 24h->26h windows, an inert diagnosis-list check, and a string-concat bug fusing two diagnoses); P1 bands are mandatory-RATIFY, matching ledger CON-0187's requirement to confirm clinical intent before any correction is designed.

RULE-PROFILAXIA-006.source_quote: double-lumen central venous puncture OR double-lumen hemodialysis catheter
RULE-PROFILAXIA-006.justification: criterio_9's device-associated-infection trigger (double-lumen CVC, double-lumen hemodialysis catheter, or indwelling bladder catheter) is an unescalated, internally-consistent institutional predicate (verify:false, NOT_APPLICABLE) using only the latest CPOE prescription, so it adopts unchanged into the v2 sepsis-adjacent device-infection-prevention logic.

RULE-PROFILAXIA-007.source_quote: stress-ulcer (LAMGD) prophylaxis start/stop, early mobilization, insertion bundles
RULE-PROFILAXIA-007.justification: The v1 facade's 11-criterion recommendation catalog (LAMGD start/stop, mobilization, insertion bundles, 10-day device reassessment) has sound clinical intent but is mechanically split across a duplicated v1 payload dict feeding a stale facade, and its scope directly conflicts with the narrower 2-criterion v3 facade (RULE-PROFILAXIA-008, pending RAT-PROFILAXIA-02); the recommendations are adapted into a single v2 bundle doc rather than porting two parallel facades.

RULE-PROFILAXIA-008.source_quote: criteria 2-8 are commented out in source
RULE-PROFILAXIA-008.justification: The v3 facade's AMBIGUOUS status (ESC-AMBIGUOUS-303) and its dead SEPSE-threshold comment block make it unclear whether the 2-criterion scope (vs v1's 11) is an intentional narrowing or an incomplete migration; AMBIGUOUS bands are RATIFY when clinically worth keeping, which both retained criteria (LAMGD, device bundle) are, resolving the CON-0190 'do not adopt as-is' constraint via ratification rather than a unilateral ADAPT.

RULE-SEDACAO-001.source_quote: Pain score 4-6 on the VISUAL analog scale OR 7-9 on the COMPORTAMENTAL
RULE-SEDACAO-001.justification: Wired AMARELO pain criterion using dual-scale VISUAL 4-6 / BPS 7-9 bands verified against SCCM PADIS 2018 pain-assessment standard and Payen 2001 BPS range; two-consecutive-balance confirmation logic is sound and directly portable to the v2 neuro-sedation pain domain.
evidence: Payen JL et al. Crit Care Med 2001

RULE-SEDACAO-002.source_quote: Pain score 7-10 on the VISUAL analog scale OR 10-12 on the COMPORTAMENTAL
RULE-SEDACAO-002.justification: Wired VERMELHO severe-pain criterion (VISUAL 7-10 / BPS 10-12) abuts criterio_7's boundary at value 7 but both bands are inclusive by design and verified against the same SCCM PADIS 2018 / Payen 2001 pain-scale anchors, so it carries forward unchanged as the severe-pain tier of v2 neuro-sedation pain assessment.
evidence: Payen JL et al. Crit Care Med 2001

RULE-SEDACAO-003.source_quote: Compound comparison -3 <= x <= -5 is unsatisfiable (requires x>=-3 AND x<=-5)
RULE-SEDACAO-003.justification: Deep-sedation RASS -3..-5 criterion is a real, guideline-anchored concept (Sessler 2002) but the code's bound order (-3<=x<=-5) is unsatisfiable and always False; since the rule is unwired the bug is inert, so the v2 design corrects the bound order to -5<=RASS<=-3 rather than porting the broken predicate, per the P3 escalation.
evidence: Sessler CN et al. Am J Respir Crit Care Med 2002

RULE-SEDACAO-004.source_quote: fio2>250 and fr>250 are physiologically impossible
RULE-SEDACAO-004.justification: Ventilator-weaning-readiness screen (RASS>=0, PSV, PINS<15, PEEP<=10, P/F>250, FiO2<50%, FR>22, sedative absence) is clinically sound per SCCM/ATS liberation guidance, but three independent field-name and comparison bugs (fio2>250, fr>250, presence-not-absence of dex/morphine) make it inert; v2 corrects all three per the P3 escalation rather than inheriting known-broken thresholds.

RULE-SEDACAO-005.source_quote: Any of midazolam>=15 OR propofol>=20 OR fentanil>=15
RULE-SEDACAO-005.justification: Wired VERMELHO overdose criterion uses fixed ml/h cutoffs per drug (midazolam>=15, propofol>=20, etc.) with no published clinical anchor because guideline dosing is weight-based (mcg/kg/min); the UNVERIFIABLE escalation band mandates ratification of whether to adopt an institution-specific drug-concentration table or replace with weight-based thresholds before v2 design.

RULE-SEDACAO-006.source_quote: Midazolam/Propofol/Cetamina present in last 4h AND P/F>200 AND FiO2<50
RULE-SEDACAO-006.justification: Unwired-but-well-defined criterion flags sedation continuing despite adequate oxygenation (P/F>200, FiO2<50, PEEP<=10) and is verified against the Berlin ARDS Definition and PADIS 2018 light-sedation target, so it is portable as a candidate v2 neuro-sedation alert regardless of its legacy wiring status.

RULE-SEDACAO-007.source_quote: P/F>150 AND (rocuronium OR cisatracurium) recorded in the last two consecutive balances
RULE-SEDACAO-007.justification: Neuromuscular-blockade-with-adequate-oxygenation criterion (P/F>150 + rocuronium/cisatracurium on two consecutive balances) is verified against ACURASYS and ROSE trial evidence on NMBA benefit thresholds in ARDS, so it carries forward as a sound BNM-de-escalation trigger for v2 despite being unwired in the legacy code.

RULE-SEDACAO-008.source_quote: True if ANY ONE sedative is absent, not requiring all absent
RULE-SEDACAO-008.justification: Undersedation-on-invasive-ventilation criterion's numeric thresholds (RASS>=-2, P/F<200, PINS>=20, FiO2>=60) verify cleanly against PADIS/Berlin anchors per the P2 escalation, but the 'absence of sedatives' clause uses any([not a,not b,not c]) which is true if only ONE drug is absent rather than all three; v2 corrects the boolean to require all three absent.

RULE-SEDACAO-009.source_quote: a reduction strictly greater than half flags true
RULE-SEDACAO-009.justification: Wired AMARELO morning-sedation-reduction criterion has moderate clinical impact per its DISCREPANCY verdict: an operator-precedence bug means a reduction of exactly half is never flagged as a failure to reduce, contradicting the intended >=50% reduction check; the mandatory P1 escalation requires ratification of the intended half-reduction boundary semantics before correcting.

RULE-SEDACAO-010.justification: High-dose neuromuscular-blockade criterion (rocuronium>16 / cisatracurium>10 ml/h) shares the same unresolved unit problem as criterio_1: no published ml/h anchor exists, standard NMBA dosing is mcg/kg/min-based, and the UNVERIFIABLE escalation band mandates ratification of a conversion approach before v2 design.

RULE-SEDACAO-011.justification: Prolonged-sedation->96h surveillance criterion correctly anchors to PADIS 2018 tolerance/withdrawal-risk guidance, but the code checks PRESENCE of 'Sedacao paliativa' where the docstring intends ABSENCE, inverting the exclusion logic; the P3 escalation confirms this is a clear code-vs-intent inversion, not disputed clinical intent, so v2 corrects the polarity.

RULE-SEDACAO-012.source_quote: Minor field-name typo (trigliceres) makes the triglycerides check inert
RULE-SEDACAO-012.justification: Propofol-infusion-syndrome surveillance criterion (96h propofol + absent CPK/TGO/TGP/triglyceride labs in 48h) is verified against PRIS literature (Mirrakhimov 2015); the 'trigliceres' field-name typo silently defaults that one leg to inert, which v2 implementation should fix as a data-mapping detail while porting the verified clinical logic unchanged.
evidence: Mirrakhimov AE et al. Crit Care Res Pract 2015

RULE-SEDACAO-013.source_quote: Sets DS_SEDATIVO_CRITERIO_1 to the first non-zero sedative
RULE-SEDACAO-013.justification: Active-sedative-label selection logic picks the first non-zero drug in a fixed midazolam>propofol>fentanil>cetamina>dexmedetomidina priority order with no clinical guideline governing this precedence; the UNVERIFIABLE escalation band mandates ratification of whether v2 needs any such priority-labeling mechanism at all before it can be adopted or dropped.

RULE-SEDACAO-014.justification: This is the production-wired v3 alert aggregator (VERMELHO if c1 or c8, else AMARELO if c5 or c7), one of three non-reconciled count/vote-based aggregation schemes (this, v1 RULE-SEDACAO-023, manual RULE-SEDACAO-021) coexisting for the same clinical concept with no published anchor for any of them; per the cluster brief's explicit open question, which scheme becomes the v2 canonical sedation alert requires clinical ratification.

RULE-SEDACAO-015.source_quote: Flags any single sedative dose above 15 ml as overdose.
RULE-SEDACAO-015.justification: Manual-pathway overdose criterion (any single sedative dose >15 ml) shares the same institutional-ml-volume-without-concentration-table problem as the wired v3 criteria (RULE-SEDACAO-005/010); the overdose-detection intent is sound but the mechanism should be adapted to a weight-based (mg/kg or mcg/kg/min) threshold model to be clinically anchorable in v2, preserving the same safety semantics.

RULE-SEDACAO-016.source_quote: Flags deeply sedated (RASS -2..-5) patients on low ventilatory support
RULE-SEDACAO-016.justification: Deep-sedation-with-low-ventilatory-support criterion is clinically anchored to the Sessler RASS scale and PADIS light-sedation targeting, but the code (PEEP<=10) drifts from its own embedded _REGRAS documentation (PEEP<10) per the P3 escalation; v2 corrects the boundary to match the documented intent (strict PEEP<10) rather than porting the drifted code behavior.
evidence: Sessler CN et al. Am J Respir Crit Care Med 2002

RULE-SEDACAO-017.source_quote: Flags patient with P/F > 200 who is still on a sedative
RULE-SEDACAO-017.justification: Good-oxygenation-on-sedation criterion (P/F>200 + sedative present, flags a sedation-lightening candidate) is verified against the Berlin ARDS Definition oxygenation boundary and PADIS 2018 light-sedation goal, with no code-vs-intent divergence, so it carries forward unchanged into v2 neuro-sedation logic.

RULE-SEDACAO-018.source_quote: Flags patient on a sedative who ALSO has P/F>=200 or EME or SHIC or PCR
RULE-SEDACAO-018.justification: Sedation-justified-by-severity criterion combines a chained Python comparison (0<ratio>=200, effectively P/F>=200) with EME/SHIC/PCR-24h flags in a proprietary composite that no published guideline defines exactly; the UNVERIFIABLE escalation band mandates ratification of whether this composite, or a corrected strict-P/F>200 form matching its own _REGRAS text, should anchor v2 sedation-justification logic.

RULE-SEDACAO-019.source_quote: Flags P/F<200 combined with light RASS (>= -2) or no sedative.
RULE-SEDACAO-019.justification: Poor-oxygenation-with-light-or-absent-sedation criterion embeds correct Berlin/PADIS numeric anchors (P/F<200, RASS light-target enumeration) but the overall composite boolean has no published external validation as a unit; the UNVERIFIABLE escalation band mandates ratification before this exact composite can be adopted into v2.

RULE-SEDACAO-020.source_quote: Flags absence of sedative while patient has poor P/F (<200)
RULE-SEDACAO-020.justification: Severity-without-sedation criterion (no sedative AND poor P/F or SHIC/PCR/EME) embeds a correct Berlin moderate-ARDS P/F<200 anchor but is itself an unvalidated proprietary composite, and its own _REGRAS text conflicts with the superseded _ANTIGAS_REGRAS <150 threshold; the UNVERIFIABLE escalation band mandates ratification of which threshold version is canonical for v2.

RULE-SEDACAO-021.source_quote: Maps count of satisfied sedation criteria (criterio_1..6) to a 3-level alert.
RULE-SEDACAO-021.justification: Manual-pathway alert aggregator (>=3 of 6 criteria -> VERMELHO, 1-2 -> AMARELO) is a second of the three non-reconciled sedation alert-aggregation schemes flagged in the cluster brief's systemic issues; whether the manual nurse-entered pathway's vote-count scheme, the wired v3 scheme, or a unified replacement becomes canonical for v2 requires explicit clinical ratification, same as RULE-SEDACAO-014.

RULE-SEDACAO-022.source_quote: Determines whether a cardiac-arrest event counts as 'in the last 24h'
RULE-SEDACAO-022.justification: PCR-24h helper implements 'last 24h' as a rolling timedelta.days==0 window (true only while elapsed time keeps the calendar-day difference at zero, not a true rolling 24h window), an AMBIGUOUS timing semantics that feeds two RATIFY-designated criteria (RULE-SEDACAO-018, -020); the AMBIGUOUS escalation band requires ratification of the intended 24h-window definition since the helper is clinically worth keeping.

RULE-SEDACAO-023.source_quote: Color alert for the v1 sedation trilha from a fixed subset of integer-flag criteria.
RULE-SEDACAO-023.justification: v1 alert aggregator (amarelo=[c1,c2], vermelho=[c4,c6]) is explicitly noted in the catalog as superseded conceptually by the wired v3 aggregator RULE-SEDACAO-014 and confirmed dead/unused in the source; it is superseded by whichever unified v2 canonical sedation-alert scheme emerges from the RAT-SEDACAO-05/09 ratification, with its distinct 4-criterion subset and vote thresholds not carried forward.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: unified v2 sedation alert scheme in clinical/domains/neuro-sedation.md, semantics pending RAT-SEDACAO-05/09
supersedes_note.lost: v1's distinct 4-criterion (c1,c2,c4,c6) count-based vote thresholds

RULE-SEDACAO-024.source_quote: Governs sedation-depth targets, neuromuscular-blocker (BNM) use, propofol PRIS monitoring, and the pain-management drug ladder.
RULE-SEDACAO-024.justification: Clinician-facing recommendation catalog spanning all 12 v3 criteria (RASS targets, BNM stop criteria, PRIS lab cadence, opioid/analgesia drug ladder) is verified against ACURASYS/ROSE/PADIS/Sepsis-3 published anchors with no code-vs-intent divergence, and its embedded numeric anchors (P/F>150, RASS<=-2, creatinina>2.5, propofol>4d, FR>22) are independently citable, so it carries forward as the v2 neuro-sedation clinician-guidance layer.

RULE-SEDACAO-025.source_quote: Maps the active continuous sedative (DS_SEDATIVO_CRITERIO_1) to a free-text recommendation
RULE-SEDACAO-025.justification: Per-drug reduction/substitution recommendation text (reduce active sedative, combine named alternatives/adjuncts) has no authoritative published source for the exact per-drug wording — it is proprietary institutional text, and the v1 variant is additionally confirmed broken (concatenation bug); the UNVERIFIABLE escalation band mandates ratification of whether and how this free-text mapping is retained in v2.

RULE-SEDACAO-026.source_quote: Allowed sedative drug choices on the manual-model Sedativo.nome_sedativo field.
RULE-SEDACAO-026.justification: Sedative drug choice enumeration (fentanil/midazolam/propofol/cetamina, with a 'Midazolan' display-label typo) is a controlled PT-BR vocabulary asset that would normally ADOPT/ADAPT into clinical-forms.md, but it carries a mandatory UNVERIFIABLE escalation band (no authoritative reference governs the enumeration, and the typo plus tests asserting against display-labels are unresolved), so it must be ratified before design rather than adopted directly.

RULE-SEDACAO-027.source_quote: A given sedative drug can appear at most once per prontuario; quantity is in ml.
RULE-SEDACAO-027.justification: Unique-sedative-per-prontuario DB constraint and its associated ml-quantity field are legacy Tasy-era data-model plumbing for the bespoke manual Sedativo entry model; under ADR-001's platform change, v2 consumes medication data via FHIR MedicationAdministration from the AMH Gold layer rather than owning a proprietary per-drug uniqueness constraint, so this rule is retired with the platform change.

RULE-SEPSE-001.source_quote: Requires simultaneous major AND minor thresholds.
RULE-SEPSE-001.justification: This is the earliest sepsis screening generation, whose AND-aggregation directly contradicts the later v3 OR-aggregation (RULE-SEPSE-002); the mandatory UNVERIFIABLE escalation band requires clinical ratification of which generation to encode in v2 sepsis screening before either can be adopted.

RULE-SEPSE-002.justification: v3 flips the v1 AND-aggregation to OR and moves criterio_11 from minor to major group, a P1-flagged internal contradiction between two generations of the same alert; the target v2 sepsis screening logic cannot be chosen without explicit clinical ratification of intended aggregation.

RULE-SEPSE-003.source_quote: branch is unreachable because criterio_11 (RULE-SEPSE-037) is hard-coded False
RULE-SEPSE-003.justification: This homecare-pathway classifier duplicates the v1/v3 major/minor screening concept but has a dead menores==4 branch and strict comparisons unlike v1/manual >=; it is superseded by a single unified v2 sepsis screening implementation once the generation choice is ratified via RAT-SEPSE-01/02, rather than porting four divergent pathway-specific classifiers.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: unified major/minor two-axis sepsis screening in clinical/domains/sepsis.md (semantics pending RAT-SEPSE-01/02)
supersedes_note.lost: homecare-specific dead menores==4 branch and strict >/== comparison style

RULE-SEPSE-004.source_quote: Distinct from the other three pathways (which use a single count).
RULE-SEPSE-004.justification: This manual-pathway alert reproduces the same maiores-AND-menores aggregation as v1 (RULE-SEPSE-001) and is UNVERIFIABLE against any external screening standard, so like v1 it cannot be adopted or corrected until the generation-choice ratification (RAT-SEPSE-01) fixes the intended aggregation for v2.

RULE-SEPSE-005.source_quote: Ordinal severity mapping of consciousness levels used to detect changes between successive readings.
RULE-SEPSE-005.justification: This ordinal consciousness ranking is a custom institutional severity ordering with no validated published scale match (only loosely resembling AVPU/GCS), and feeds the direction-of-change logic in RULE-SEPSE-033 which is itself DISCREPANCY-flagged; the mapping's clinical validity must be ratified before it can anchor v2 neuro-sedation or sepsis alerting.

RULE-SEPSE-006.source_quote: balanco_hidrico_24h ADDS ganhos + perdas (does not subtract)
RULE-SEPSE-006.justification: The 24h fluid-balance snapshot sums ganhos+perdas instead of net-subtracting them, a sign-convention ambiguity that is UNVERIFIABLE without knowing the internal perdas-sign data convention, and the method is not even wired into production output, so clinical intent must be ratified before porting to v2 fluid-balance logic.

RULE-SEPSE-007.justification: The implementation checks PRESENCE of noradrenaline where the docstring intends ABSENCE, inverting the sepsis-organ-dysfunction gating logic for the fever criterion; this P1 DISCREPANCY on core inclusion/exclusion logic requires clinical ratification of the intended vasopressor gate before correction.
evidence: Bone RC et al. ACCP/SCCM Consensus, Chest 1992

RULE-SEPSE-008.source_quote: No noradrenaline (6h) AND no invasive mechanical ventilation AND (FR>20 OR SatO2<94).
RULE-SEPSE-008.justification: Verified against SIRS respiratory rate >20/min and the SSC 2021 supplemental-oxygen SpO2 >=94% target with no discrepancy flagged and no escalation, so the tachypnea/hypoxemia-without-vasopressor criterion carries forward unchanged into the v2 sepsis domain as a minor/major screening input.
evidence: Bone RC et al. ACCP/SCCM Consensus, Chest 1992
Evans L et al. SSC 2021, Crit Care Med 2021;49(11):e1063-e1143

RULE-SEPSE-009.source_quote: DISCREPANCY - missing negation on noradrenaline vs docstring.
RULE-SEPSE-009.justification: Like criterio_1 (RULE-SEPSE-007), this respiratory-failure-prescription criterion checks PRESENCE instead of documented ABSENCE of noradrenaline, a P1 gating inversion on a SOFA-respiratory-component proxy that must be clinically ratified before the vasopressor exclusion can be corrected in v2.
evidence: Singer M et al. Sepsis-3, JAMA 2016

RULE-SEPSE-010.source_quote: Noradrenaline OR vasopressin started in last 6h AND absent beyond ~24h (26h window).
RULE-SEPSE-010.justification: Verified as operationalizing new-onset septic-shock detection consistent with Sepsis-3 vasopressor-requirement criteria, with no discrepancy or escalation flagged, so the newly-started-vasopressor criterion carries forward as-is into v2 hemodynamics/sepsis correlation logic.
evidence: Singer M et al. Sepsis-3, JAMA 2016

RULE-SEPSE-011.source_quote: No noradrenaline (12h) AND (PAS<90 OR PAD<60 OR PAM<65) in last 6h.
RULE-SEPSE-011.justification: Verified against Sepsis-3/SSC MAP>=65 mmHg and SBP<90 mmHg hypotension anchors with no discrepancy, and the cluster brief flags it as the clean reference rule to anchor v2 hypotension logic, so it carries forward unchanged.
evidence: Singer M et al. Sepsis-3, JAMA 2016

RULE-SEPSE-012.source_quote: No noradrenaline (6h) AND platelets < 100000 in last 24h.
RULE-SEPSE-012.justification: Verified against the SOFA coagulation subscore threshold of platelets <100x10^3/uL with no discrepancy or escalation, so the thrombocytopenia-without-vasopressor criterion carries forward into the v2 sepsis domain unchanged, pending a fix to the underlying CharField DB-comparison risk noted in edge_cases.
evidence: Vincent JL et al. SOFA score, Intensive Care Med 1996;22:707-710

RULE-SEPSE-013.justification: Catalog status OK is overridden by verdict DISCREPANCY because the legacy >=3 mmol/L lactate cutoff is stricter than the SSC 2021/Sepsis-3 elevated-lactate anchor of >2 mmol/L, missing the 2-3 mmol/L hypoperfusion band; v2 must adopt the guideline-correct >2 mmol/L threshold, not the legacy >=3 cutoff.
evidence: Evans L et al. SSC 2021, Crit Care Med 2021;49:e1063

RULE-SEPSE-014.source_quote: weight-parse strips the decimal separator inflating peso ~10x
RULE-SEPSE-014.justification: This oliguria criterion is the single clinical_impact=high rule in the cluster: a weight-parsing bug inflates body weight ~10x (understating mL/kg/h) and the DRC-em-TRS exclusion gate is both non-functional and logically inverted, making the whole predicate effectively always False; the P0 escalation mandates clinical ratification before any AKI-urine-output logic can be adopted for v2.
evidence: KDIGO Clinical Practice Guideline for AKI 2012, Kidney Int Suppl 2012;2:1-138

RULE-SEPSE-015.source_quote: DRC exclusion always-true due to fromkeys bug
RULE-SEPSE-015.justification: The dialysis/CKD exclusion gate is vacuously always-true due to a fromkeys() lookup bug, so the AKI criterion fires purely on creatinine even for patients who should be excluded per its own documented intent; this P1 DISCREPANCY requires ratification of the correct exclusion mechanism before adoption.

RULE-SEPSE-016.source_quote: GCS-change condition computes (last - penultimate) >= 2 which detects an INCREASE
RULE-SEPSE-016.justification: The GCS-delta computation detects an INCREASE of >=2 rather than the documented decrease/worsening, and the dementia exclusion is vacuously true via the same fromkeys bug family as RULE-SEPSE-015; per ledger CON-0193 this is a correction candidate, but the mandatory P1 escalation band requires clinical ratification before the direction fix and exclusion logic can be adopted.
evidence: Vincent JL et al. SOFA score, Intensive Care Med 1996;22:707-710

RULE-SEPSE-017.source_quote: branch from the docstring is not implemented; only the jaundice
RULE-SEPSE-017.justification: The hyperbilirubinemia major criterion never implements the documented bilirubin>2mg/dL branch, relying solely on a truthy jaundice flag that under-detects hepatic dysfunction relative to the SOFA liver subscore; this P1 incompleteness in a MAJOR-weighted criterion requires ratification of whether to add the missing bilirubin check.
evidence: Vincent JL et al. SOFA score, Intensive Care Med 1996;22:707-710

RULE-SEPSE-018.source_quote: No noradrenaline (6h) AND temperature < 36C in last 6h.
RULE-SEPSE-018.justification: Verified against the ACCP/SCCM SIRS temperature criterion (<36C) with no discrepancy or escalation, so the hypothermia minor criterion carries forward unchanged into the v2 sepsis domain screening logic.
evidence: Bone RC et al. ACCP/SCCM Consensus, Chest 1992;101:1644-55

RULE-SEPSE-019.source_quote: filters fr (frequencia respiratoria) instead of fc (frequencia cardiaca)
RULE-SEPSE-019.justification: This tachycardia criterion filters the wrong column (respiratory rate fr instead of heart rate fc), so it almost never fires in practice despite the docstring intending HR>100 bpm; this P1 wrong-field bug on a scored minor criterion requires ratification before the column fix and threshold (90 vs 100 bpm) can be finalized.
evidence: Bone RC et al. ACCP/SCCM Consensus, Chest 1992;101:1644-55

RULE-SEPSE-020.source_quote: P/F uses raw diurna_po2/diurna_fio2 (fio2 as fraction vs percent not normalized).
RULE-SEPSE-020.justification: Catalog status OK is overridden by verdict DISCREPANCY: although the PaCO2<32 and P/F<300 cutoffs match SIRS/Berlin references, the P/F ratio computation does not normalize FiO2 between fraction and percent, a P1-flagged computation defect requiring ratification of the correct unit handling before adoption.

RULE-SEPSE-021.source_quote: PCR>100 is AND-combined with the leukocyte/band clause
RULE-SEPSE-021.justification: The CRP>100 institutional add-on is mandatorily AND-combined with the WBC/band SIRS criteria rather than offered as an alternative per the docstring, making this minor criterion far stricter than intended; this P1 boolean-logic inversion requires ratification of whether CRP should remain a co-condition or become an independent OR branch.
evidence: Bone RC et al. ACCP/SCCM Consensus, Chest 1992

RULE-SEPSE-022.source_quote: No noradrenaline (6h) AND TEC>3s in last 24h AND not TEC>3s before 24h (new onset).
RULE-SEPSE-022.justification: Verified against the ANDROMEDA-SHOCK CRT>3s abnormal-perfusion threshold with no discrepancy or escalation, so the new-onset prolonged capillary refill criterion carries forward unchanged into v2 sepsis/hemodynamics screening.
evidence: Hernandez G et al. ANDROMEDA-SHOCK, JAMA 2019

RULE-SEPSE-023.source_quote: No invasive vent AND GCS>=13 AND nasoenteral tube prescription in last 12h.
RULE-SEPSE-023.justification: This enteral-tube-with-adequate-GCS composite has no primary clinical reference establishing it as a sepsis-screening marker; it is an internal institutional risk indicator, so the UNVERIFIABLE band mandates ratification of clinical intent before it can be carried into v2 as a minor criterion.

RULE-SEPSE-024.source_quote: No noradrenaline (6h) AND CVC or CDL present in an evolution older than 7 days.
RULE-SEPSE-024.justification: CDC/HICPAC guidance explicitly discourages fixed catheter-replacement intervals, so the >7-day CVC/CDL dwell-time cutoff used here is an unanchored institutional parameter; the UNVERIFIABLE band mandates clinical ratification of whether any fixed dwell-time threshold should be retained in v2.
evidence: O'Grady NP et al. CDC/HICPAC Guidelines 2011

RULE-SEPSE-025.source_quote: Femoral site codes hardcoded VFD/VFE (veia femoral direita/esquerda).
RULE-SEPSE-025.justification: The >5-day femoral-line dwell cutoff and hardcoded VFD/VFE site codes have no authoritative published anchor even though femoral-access infection risk itself is guideline-supported; the UNVERIFIABLE band mandates ratification of the specific dwell-time parameter before v2 adoption.
evidence: Marik PE et al. Crit Care Med 2012 meta-analysis

RULE-SEPSE-026.source_quote: Uses generic enf_cirurgia_recente flag (not abdominal-specific despite docstring).
RULE-SEPSE-026.justification: The generic recent-surgery flag is not scoped to abdominal surgery as the docstring intends, and no scored SIRS/qSOFA/Sepsis-3 criterion assigns weight to recent surgery as a discrete screening item; the UNVERIFIABLE band mandates ratification of both the surgical-site scope and its inclusion as a scored criterion.
evidence: Evans L et al. SSC 2021, Crit Care Med 2021;49(11):e1063-e1143

RULE-SEPSE-027.source_quote: fires when the most recent vital-sign temperature exceeds 38 C
RULE-SEPSE-027.justification: Verified against the ACCP/SCCM SIRS fever threshold of >38C with no discrepancy or escalation, so this homecare-pathway major fever criterion carries forward into the v2 sepsis domain, though the always-true 24h window guard (tempo_criacao seconds bug) should be fixed in implementation.
evidence: Bone RC et al. ACCP/SCCM Consensus, Chest 1992;101(6):1644-55

RULE-SEPSE-028.source_quote: fires if the patient is NOT on mechanical ventilation and respiratory rate > 20
RULE-SEPSE-028.justification: Verified against the ACCP/SCCM SIRS respiratory-rate criterion (>20/min) with the SpO2<96% institutional refinement noted but not flagged as discrepant, and no escalation, so this homecare tachypnea/desaturation criterion carries forward into v2 unchanged.
evidence: Bone RC et al. ACCP/SCCM Consensus, Chest 1992

RULE-SEPSE-029.source_quote: fires if current ventilation is mechanical while the previous vital-sign reading was room air
RULE-SEPSE-029.justification: No authoritative published sepsis scale defines this exact ventilatory-escalation transition (mechanical-after-room-air OR current O2-support) as a discrete criterion; the UNVERIFIABLE band mandates clinical ratification of the institutional escalation logic before it can be carried into v2 respiratory/sepsis screening.

RULE-SEPSE-030.justification: Catalog status OK is overridden by verdict DISCREPANCY: legacy fires the capillary-refill major criterion only at >5s, whereas ANDROMEDA-SHOCK defines the perfusion-abnormality threshold at >3s, so v2 must adopt the guideline-correct >3s cutoff rather than porting the legacy >5s threshold.
evidence: Hernandez G et al. ANDROMEDA-SHOCK, JAMA 2019;321(7):654-664

RULE-SEPSE-031.source_quote: fires when systolic BP (PAS) < 90 mmHg, or diastolic BP (PAD) < 60 mmHg
RULE-SEPSE-031.justification: Verified against SSC/Sepsis-2 sepsis-induced hypotension criteria (SBP<90 mmHg) with no discrepancy or escalation, so this homecare hypotension major criterion carries forward into v2 unchanged.
evidence: Dellinger RP et al. SSC 2012, Crit Care Med

RULE-SEPSE-032.source_quote: fires if the last fluid-output was catheter diuresis (diurese_sonda) with volume <= 100
RULE-SEPSE-032.justification: Catalog status OK is overridden by verdict DISCREPANCY: the oliguria branch uses a fixed absolute 100 mL cutoff and a buggy timedelta.seconds recency check instead of the weight- and time-normalized KDIGO/SSC definition (<0.5 mL/kg/h for >=2h); this P1 multi-dimensional deviation requires ratification of the corrected formula.
evidence: KDIGO Clinical Practice Guideline for AKI 2012

RULE-SEPSE-033.source_quote: returns True when the PREVIOUS severity rank is greater than the current rank
RULE-SEPSE-033.justification: This consciousness-variation criterion fires on clinical IMPROVEMENT (previous rank worse than current) rather than deterioration, an inverted-direction bug flagged in ledger CON-0193 as an ADOPT-CORRECTED candidate; the mandatory P1 escalation band requires ratification before the direction can be corrected for v2 neuro-sedation/sepsis screening.
evidence: Singer M et al. Sepsis-3, JAMA 2016

RULE-SEPSE-034.source_quote: temperature < 36 C on the latest reading within 24h
RULE-SEPSE-034.justification: Minor criterion 8 (hypothermia <36C within 24h) reproduces the ACCP/SCCM SIRS hypothermia threshold exactly with high confidence and no boundary defects, so it carries forward unchanged into the v2 sepsis domain as a minor screening criterion.
evidence: Bone RC et al. ACCP/SCCM Consensus, Chest 1992;101:1644-1655 (SIRS temp<36C)

RULE-SEPSE-035.source_quote: heart rate > 110 bpm on the latest reading within a 12-hour window
RULE-SEPSE-035.justification: Legacy HR>110 bpm cutoff for minor criterion 9 undershoots the SIRS tachycardia threshold of HR>90 bpm, silently missing HR 91-110 bpm patients (reduced sensitivity); v2 must adopt the >90 bpm cutoff per escalation ESC-P2-065 while keeping the correct frequencia_cardiaca column mapping.
evidence: Bone RC et al. ACCP/SCCM Consensus, Chest 1992;101:1644-1655 (SIRS HR>90)

RULE-SEPSE-036.source_quote: a peripheral venous access in use has been inserted > 7 days ago
RULE-SEPSE-036.justification: Escalation band UNVERIFIABLE mandates ratification; the >7-day peripheral/dialysis-catheter dwell cutoff is an institutional CRBSI/HAI risk heuristic with no authoritative sepsis-scoring guideline behind it, so the clinical committee must decide whether it stays a minor sepsis criterion in v2.
evidence: CDC/HICPAC 2011 Guidelines for the Prevention of Intravascular Catheter-Related Infections

RULE-SEPSE-037.source_quote: hard-coded to always return False (unimplemented placeholder)
RULE-SEPSE-037.justification: Escalation band P1 mandates ratification; criterio_11 is a dead placeholder that caps the 'menores' minor-criteria group at 3 true values, making the documented menores==4 alert branch permanently unreachable, so the committee must decide whether to design a real 4th minor criterion for v2 or retire the 4-of-4 gate.

RULE-SEPSE-038.justification: Major criterion C1 reproduces the 2001 SCCM/ESICM/ACCP/ATS/SIS sepsis fever threshold (core temperature >38.3C) exactly, verified with strict-boundary tests (37 false, 38.5 true), so it carries forward unchanged into the v2 sepsis domain as a major screening criterion.
evidence: Levy MM et al. Crit Care Med 2003;31(4):1250-1256

RULE-SEPSE-039.source_quote: (FR>20 OR SatO2<96) AND no mechanical ventilation
RULE-SEPSE-039.justification: Major criterion C2 correctly combines the SIRS tachypnea threshold (RR>20/min) with an institutional SpO2<96% oxygenation screen and correctly excludes mechanically-ventilated patients (spontaneous distress only); verified with passing test vectors, so it carries forward unchanged.
evidence: Levy MM et al. Crit Care Med 2003;31:1250-1256 (SIRS RR>20)

RULE-SEPSE-040.source_quote: mechanical ventilation started less than 1 day ago
RULE-SEPSE-040.justification: Escalation band UNVERIFIABLE mandates ratification; new mechanical ventilation is a recognized Sepsis-3 organ-dysfunction marker, but the specific strict <24h-since-VM-start scoring window is an institutional Trilha-Sepse design choice with no published source, so the committee must confirm the window before v2 adoption.
evidence: Singer M et al. JAMA 2016;315(8):801-810 (Sepsis-3 organ dysfunction)

RULE-SEPSE-041.source_quote: noradrenaline infusion started within the last 24h
RULE-SEPSE-041.justification: Escalation band UNVERIFIABLE mandates ratification; vasopressor requirement is a valid Sepsis-3 septic-shock marker, but the specific inclusive >=24h-since-start scoring rule for major criterion C4 is proprietary to the Trilha-Sepse screening tool and needs clinical confirmation before v2 adoption.
evidence: Singer M et al. JAMA 2016;315(8):801-810 (Sepsis-3 vasopressor shock definition)

RULE-SEPSE-042.justification: Legacy major criterion C5 fires only above 5 seconds while ANDROMEDA-SHOCK/SSC-2021 define abnormal capillary refill as >3 seconds, silently missing CRT 3.1-5.0s hypoperfusion; v2 must adopt the published >3s cutoff per escalation ESC-P2-087.
evidence: Hernandez G et al. JAMA 2019;321(7):654-664 (ANDROMEDA-SHOCK, CRT>3s)

RULE-SEPSE-043.source_quote: _REGRAS text says "PAS < 90 OU PAD < 60"; code compares PAD < 90
RULE-SEPSE-043.justification: Escalation band P0 (highest severity in this shard) mandates ratification; the code diverges from its own documented rule text (comparing PAD<90 instead of the specified PAD<60) with clinical_impact high, so the committee must resolve the canonical hypotension logic for major criterion C6 before any v2 build.
evidence: Dellinger RP/Levy MM Sepsis-3/SSC hypotension criteria (SBP<90, MAP<70)

RULE-SEPSE-044.source_quote: 24h urine output <= 1200 ml OR (creatinine > 2 AND not on hemodialysis)
RULE-SEPSE-044.justification: Escalation band P1 mandates ratification; the fixed 1200 mL/24h oliguria cutoff (vs the KDIGO weight-based ~0.5 mL/kg/h standard) and the debito==0 falsy exclusion of true anuria both require clinical-committee resolution before major criterion C7 becomes a v2 sepsis/AKI criterion.
evidence: Vincent JL et al. SOFA renal component, Intensive Care Med 1996;22:707-710

RULE-SEPSE-045.source_quote: Glasgow decreased by >= 2 points vs the most-recent 24h value OR delirium present
RULE-SEPSE-045.justification: Major criterion C8 aligns with the Sepsis-3 organ-dysfunction framework (acute mental-status change; GCS-based SOFA/qSOFA) and has verified boundary tests (delta -2 true, -1 false), so it carries forward unchanged into the v2 sepsis domain.

RULE-SEPSE-046.justification: Major criterion C9 matches the SOFA hepatic organ-dysfunction threshold (bilirubin >2 mg/dL) and ACCP/SCCM severe-sepsis hyperbilirubinemia criterion exactly, with correct strict-boundary test vectors (2 false, 3 true), so it carries forward unchanged.
evidence: Vincent JL et al. SOFA score, Intensive Care Med 1996;22:707-710

RULE-SEPSE-047.justification: Minor criterion C10 reproduces the ACCP/SCCM SIRS hypothermia threshold (core temp <36C) exactly using a 24h lookback-chain value distinct from the current-reading variant already adopted as RULE-SEPSE-034, so both minor-hypothermia variants of the manual pathway carry forward separately into v2.

RULE-SEPSE-048.source_quote: heart rate > 100 bpm using 24h value (falling back to 12h)
RULE-SEPSE-048.justification: Minor criterion C11's HR>100 bpm threshold undershoots the SIRS tachycardia cutoff of HR>90 bpm, missing HR 91-100 bpm; v2 must adopt >90 bpm and replace the convoluted 24h/12h conditional-expression precedence with an explicit OR of the two lookback windows, per escalation ESC-P2-088.
evidence: Bone RC et al. Chest 1992;101:1644-1655 (SIRS HR>90)

RULE-SEPSE-049.source_quote: (PaCO2<32 AND no VM) OR P/F ratio < 300
RULE-SEPSE-049.justification: Minor criterion C12 correctly reproduces the SIRS PaCO2<32 mmHg criterion and the Berlin/SSC P/F<300 mild-ARDS oxygenation-failure threshold, with verified strict-boundary tests (ratio 300 false, 299 true), so it carries forward unchanged.

RULE-SEPSE-050.justification: Minor criterion C13's lactate cutoff of >=2.5 mmol/L is stricter than the Surviving Sepsis Campaign hyperlactatemia threshold of >2 mmol/L, missing the 2.0-2.49 mmol/L hypoperfusion band; v2 must adopt >2 mmol/L per escalation ESC-P2-089 and the canonical mmol/L unit registry.
evidence: Evans L et al. Crit Care Med 2021;49:e1063-e1143 (SSC 2021, lactate >2 mmol/L)

RULE-SEPSE-051.justification: Minor criterion C14 reproduces the SIRS WBC upper-bound threshold (>12,000/mm3) exactly; the active build implements only the leukocytosis branch (the historical <4000/mm3 leukopenia branch is commented out per RULE-SEPSE-066), so v2 should adopt the active >12000 form and separately weigh restoring leukopenia.
evidence: Levy MM et al. Crit Care Med 2003;31:1250-1256

RULE-SEPSE-052.justification: Minor criterion C15 reproduces the SOFA coagulation subscore and organ-dysfunction thrombocytopenia threshold (<100,000/uL) exactly, with verified strict-boundary tests (100000 false, 99999 true), so it carries forward unchanged.
evidence: Vincent JL et al. SOFA score, Intensive Care Med 1996;22:707-710
Levy MM et al. Crit Care Med 2003;31:1250-1256

RULE-SEPSE-053.source_quote: low oral diet acceptance AND Glasgow >= 13
RULE-SEPSE-053.justification: Escalation band UNVERIFIABLE mandates ratification; minor criterion C16 (reduced oral intake with preserved consciousness) is an institutional homecare early-warning observation absent from SIRS/Sepsis-3/SOFA/qSOFA, so the clinical committee must decide whether it earns a place among v2 minor sepsis criteria.

RULE-SEPSE-054.justification: Minor criterion C17 fires only at GCS<=13, missing the isolated GCS=14 case that both qSOFA and the SOFA neurological subscore treat as abnormal altered mentation (any GCS<15); v2 must widen the threshold to GCS<15 per escalation ESC-P2-090.
evidence: Singer M et al. JAMA 2016;315:801-810 (qSOFA altered mentation, GCS<15)
Vincent JL et al. Intensive Care Med 1996;22:707-710 (SOFA neuro GCS 13-14)

RULE-SEPSE-055.source_quote: presence of CVC/CDL for more than 7 days
RULE-SEPSE-055.justification: Escalation band UNVERIFIABLE mandates ratification; minor criterion C18's central-line dwell >7 days is a recognized CLABSI infection-source risk factor with no published sepsis-scoring reference, and the duration is a clinician-judged boolean rather than computed, so the committee must confirm its role in v2.
evidence: O'Grady NP et al. CDC/HICPAC 2011 catheter-related infection guideline

RULE-SEPSE-056.source_quote: CVC/CDL in femoral site for more than 5 days
RULE-SEPSE-056.justification: Escalation band UNVERIFIABLE mandates ratification; minor criterion C19's femoral-site CVC dwell >5 days is directionally supported by CLABSI risk literature but has no validated cutoff or published sepsis-screening source, so the committee must confirm whether/how this boolean flag survives into v2.
evidence: CDC/HICPAC 2011 catheter-related infection guideline (femoral site risk)

RULE-SEPSE-057.justification: Escalation band UNVERIFIABLE mandates ratification; minor criterion C20 (recent abdominal surgery) is a plausible intra-abdominal infection source per SSC 2021 source-control guidance, but the institutional '<20 dias' recency window has no published cutoff, so the committee must confirm the window before v2 adoption.
evidence: Evans L et al. Crit Care Med 2021;49:e1063-e1143 (SSC 2021 source control)

RULE-SEPSE-058.source_quote: Alert-label catalog (core/facade/trilha_sepse_v3.py, payload_sepse_automatica_v3) vs the v3 model
RULE-SEPSE-058.justification: Escalation band P1 mandates ratification; the v3 alert-label catalog disagrees with its own underlying model predicates on criterion numbering and at least six distinct thresholds (e.g. plaquetopenia <150000 label vs model <100000), so the committee must pick the reference-correct threshold set before any v2 sepsis alert catalog is built.

RULE-SEPSE-059.source_quote: Sepsis pathway variant B (payload_sepse_automatica). 27 flagged-criterion alert labels plus one shared
RULE-SEPSE-059.justification: Variant B's 27-flag-plus-static-recommendation mechanism carries sound, SSC-2021-aligned care-pathway intent (30 mL/kg bolus, blood cultures x2, gram-negative double coverage) but the mechanism itself (flat boolean flags, lactate written in mg/dl instead of mmol/L, concatenated static text) must become structured v2 alert definitions with normalized units, per escalation ESC-P2-082.
evidence: Evans L et al. Crit Care Med 2021;49:e1063-e1143 (SSC 2021)

RULE-SEPSE-060.justification: Escalation band P1 mandates ratification; variant A's live wiring is unclear (catalog status AMBIGUOUS, facade/sepse.py appears to alias variant B instead) and it prescribes a distinct fixed-dose regimen (Meropenem 1g, 1500ml fixed bolus) conflicting with variant B's weight-based 30 mL/kg dosing, so the committee must confirm whether this pathway was ever live before any of its content enters v2.
evidence: Evans L et al. Crit Care Med 2021;49:e1063-e1143 (weight-based 30 mL/kg crystalloid)

RULE-SEPSE-061.source_quote: Clinical decision-support text presented for the hour-1 "realizacao_expansao_volemica" item
RULE-SEPSE-061.justification: Dosing guidance (30 mL/kg Ringer Lactato, 20 mL/kg reduced dose for cardiac/renal dysfunction, early norepinephrine, 6h central-access target) matches SSC 2021 exactly; the sole defect is the lactate hypoperfusion trigger written as 30 mg/dl (~3.3 mmol/L) instead of the SSC >2 mmol/L marker, which v2 must correct per escalation ESC-P2-068.
evidence: Evans L et al. Crit Care Med 2021;49:e1063-e1143

RULE-SEPSE-062.source_quote: Clinical decision-support text for the "exames" (labs reassessment) item
RULE-SEPSE-062.justification: The restrictive-transfusion threshold (Hb>7, do not transfuse above), avoidance of routine bicarbonate in lactic acidosis, and dobutamine-on-hypoperfusion guidance all match SSC 2021 exactly; the hemoglobin unit is written mg/dl in the legacy text (physiologically g/dL) and should be normalized at the units-registry layer, not in clinical content, so this carries forward via ADOPT.
evidence: Evans L et al. Crit Care Med 2021;49:e1063-e1143

RULE-SEPSE-063.source_quote: Clinical decision-support text for the "status_hemodinamico" item
RULE-SEPSE-063.justification: Intubation-with-RASS-2-sedation-target guidance for poor-perfusion/tachydyspnea/confusion, plus ultrasound-guided 250 mL fluid-challenge assessment, both match standard ICU shock management and the published RASS scale definition exactly, so this decision text carries forward unchanged.
evidence: Sessler CN et al. Am J Respir Crit Care Med 2002;166:1338-1344 (RASS)

RULE-SEPSE-064.source_quote: Clinical decision-support text for the "dispositivos_invasivos" item
RULE-SEPSE-064.justification: Static clinical-guidance text (early peripheral noradrenaline, 6h central-access target with invasive BP/diuresis monitoring, CVC replacement after 7 days, post-line chest X-ray) is verify:false/NOT_APPLICABLE but content-consistent with standard ICU sepsis practice, so it carries forward as v2 domain documentation via ADOPT.
evidence: Evans L et al. Crit Care Med 2021;49:e1063-e1143

RULE-SEPSE-065.source_quote: Two NE thresholds: 0.3 (add vasopressin/hydrocortisone) and 1.0 (switch to epinephrine)
RULE-SEPSE-065.justification: Vasopressin+hydrocortisone escalation at NE>0.3 mcg/kg/min, epinephrine preference above NE>1 mcg/kg/min or shock index >0.7, and hydrocortisone 50mg q6h dosing all match SSC 2021 vasopressor-escalation guidance and the published Allgower/Rady shock-index normal range, so this carries forward unchanged.
evidence: Evans L et al. Crit Care Med 2021;49:e1063-e1143
Rady MY et al. shock index, Am J Emerg Med 1994

RULE-SEPSE-066.source_quote: Documents criteria that exist in code but are disabled, and the prior 27-criterion version
RULE-SEPSE-066.justification: This rule only documents disabled/commented-out legacy code paths (the 27-criteria _ANTIGAS_REGRAS, commented leukocyte and CVC criteria) that never executed in production; its substantive content is already independently captured in the active C1-C20 dispositions above (e.g. leukocytosis in RULE-SEPSE-051), so it is retired as legacy-code bookkeeping rather than carried forward as its own v2 artifact.

RULE-SEPSE-067.source_quote: time-windowed infection/device exposures and vasoactive/steroid use
RULE-SEPSE-067.justification: Boolean screening flags (antibiotic-24h, device-dwell 10d/7d-femoral, adjunct-therapy) are verified, device-associated-infection surveillance inputs that feed the v2 sepsis/CLABSI-CAUTI screening engine unchanged.

RULE-SEPSE-068.source_quote: stored as a plain number with no bounds
RULE-SEPSE-068.justification: AMBIGUOUS-band escalation on a live sepsis-criterion input: the ureia field name implies a >50mg/dL-in-24h boolean threshold but the stored type is an unbounded raw number, so a clinician must ratify whether v2 encodes urea as a threshold flag or a continuous lab value before it feeds any sepsis screening logic.

RULE-SEPSE-069.justification: Verified against the Surviving Sepsis Campaign Hour-1 Bundle: the overdue-flag windows (1h for the first-hour bundle, 3h for reassessment) are the correct operational timers for tracking hour-1 bundle compliance in v2.
evidence: SSC-2018 (Levy MM, Evans LE, Rhodes A. Surviving Sepsis Campaign Bundle: 2018 update. Crit Care Med 2018;46:997)

RULE-SEPSE-070.justification: The 2h reveal-delay for reassessment bundle items complements the verified RULE-SEPSE-069 overdue windows and is a sound, low-risk display-timing rule worth carrying into the v2 hour-1 bundle timer design unchanged.

RULE-SEPSE-071.source_quote: last concluded one is >3 days old (or none)
RULE-SEPSE-071.justification: Sound intent (prevent duplicate open sepsis protocols; enforce a 3-day cooldown after a concluded protocol before allowing a new one) but the mechanism is legacy Django ORM filters with a cross-module serializer dependency; v2 should re-implement the same dedup/cooldown semantics via the alert-suppression mechanism.

RULE-SEPSE-072.source_quote: generates the 7 bundle items, and posts an audit observation
RULE-SEPSE-072.justification: The accept-and-generate-bundle intent is sound, but the mechanism (legacy TrilhaInterativaSepse Django model, color-string alert state 'LARANJA', synchronous item generation) is legacy-app plumbing tied to the retired Tasy-era stack per the cluster note that workflow rules 069-098 are retire-candidates for a rebuilt state machine.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: v2 alert-lifecycle mechanism (severity taxonomy normal|watch|urgent|critical per _work/alerts/<domain>.yaml) plus an audit-trail event on protocol acceptance
supersedes_note.lost: the legacy color-string alert state 'LARANJA' and the synchronous Django TrilhaInterativaSepse accept workflow; the 7-item hour-1 bundle generation concept itself is preserved via RULE-SEPSE-076

RULE-SEPSE-073.justification: P3 escalation ESC-P3-125: the rejection branch never sets horario_registro_aceitacao yet the observation message dereferences it, a live crash risk; this known-broken legacy behavior must never be ported, so the rejection workflow is superseded by a redesigned v2 protocol-decline path with a corrected audit message.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: v2 protocol-decline workflow that stamps a decline timestamp before composing any audit message (see RULE-SEPSE-094 mandatory-reason concept)
supersedes_note.lost: the specific crash-prone message-building code path; the NEUTRO/assistido state reset on rejection is preserved conceptually

RULE-SEPSE-074.justification: The accepted-to-closed transition guard (was-accepted, now-not-accepted) is legacy Django serializer state -machine plumbing on the same rebuilt-candidate list as RULE-SEPSE-072/073; the closure semantics (mark closed, reset parent alert, audit observation) are preserved but the mechanism itself is replaced in v2.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: v2 protocol-lifecycle service handling accept/reject/close as first-class alert-lifecycle transitions instead of ad-hoc serializer branching
supersedes_note.lost: the passthrough-on-other-updates implicit default branch; explicit v2 transitions should be enumerated rather than inferred from field-diffing

RULE-SEPSE-075.source_quote: stamps horario_checagem=now and posts an item-specific documentation observation
RULE-SEPSE-075.justification: The bundle item check/uncheck-with-auto-completion logic (last unchecked item flips the pathway to concluded, forcing aceito=false and clearing the parent alert) captures a genuine bundle-compliance-timing KPI worth preserving, but the synchronous Django state mutation needs redesign for the v2 alert engine.

RULE-SEPSE-076.source_quote: request labs, start/escalate antimicrobials, perform volume expansion
RULE-SEPSE-076.justification: The exact 7-item hour-1/reassessment bundle structure mirrors the Surviving Sepsis Campaign hour-1 bundle and is a clinical-content asset (item set and pacote grouping) to carry forward verbatim into the v2 sepsis domain bundle definition, independent of the legacy generation mechanism.
evidence: SSC-2018 (Levy MM, Evans LE, Rhodes A. Surviving Sepsis Campaign Bundle: 2018 update. Crit Care Med 2018;46:997)

RULE-SEPSE-077.source_quote: automatically set to the authenticated request user, overriding any client-supplied value
RULE-SEPSE-077.justification: Server-side auto-attribution of the checking clinician's identity (never trusting a client-supplied checker id) is a reusable security/audit-integrity pattern worth keeping for any v2 checklist-style clinical action, distinct from the legacy Django view mechanism that implements it here.

RULE-SEPSE-078.source_quote: belonging to the 'first hour' bundle that are not yet checked
RULE-SEPSE-078.justification: The auto-check dispatch guard (only fire for unchecked first-hour items, per-minute cadence for up to 1h) encodes a sound automation intent for verifying hour-1 bundle compliance against CPOE/ADEP data, but the hardcoded 'sistema' user and 1-minute polling task need redesign as an event-driven v2 mechanism.

RULE-SEPSE-079.justification: P3 escalation ESC-P3-126: the exam-solicitation auto-check is dead code because the dispatch string ('solicitacao_exame') never matches the bundle-item enum ('solicitacao_exames') from RULE-SEPSE-076/096; v2 must adapt this rule by aligning the field name so the intended CPOE-driven auto-check actually fires.

RULE-SEPSE-080.source_quote: CPOE updated within the 24h preceding the interactive pathway's creation time
RULE-SEPSE-080.justification: The 24h CPOE-lookback auto-check for antimicrobial escalation is a sound hour-1-bundle automation concept (distinct 24h window vs the 4h volume-expansion window in RULE-SEPSE-081), but the direct Django ORM query against a legacy CPOE table must be redesigned against the v2 AMH Gold data source via Athena.
evidence: SSC-2018 (Levy MM, Evans LE, Rhodes A. Surviving Sepsis Campaign Bundle: 2018 update. Crit Care Med 2018;46:997)

RULE-SEPSE-081.source_quote: updated within the 4h preceding pathway creation shows lactated-Ringer
RULE-SEPSE-081.justification: The 4h ADEP-lookback auto-check for volume expansion (lactated Ringer OR 0.9% saline, strictly >0 mL) is a sound automation for the first-hour fluid-resuscitation bundle item, but the direct legacy ADEP-table query and first()-based professional attribution need redesign onto the v2 data platform.
evidence: SSC-2018 (Levy MM, Evans LE, Rhodes A. Surviving Sepsis Campaign Bundle: 2018 update. Crit Care Med 2018;46:997)

RULE-SEPSE-082.source_quote: automatically creates a linked Sepse (sepsis-screening) record for the same encounter
RULE-SEPSE-082.justification: Triggering a sepsis-screening evaluation on every vital-signs submission is the correct clinical intent (no vitals should go unscreened), but the linkage to 'the most recent Formulario regardless of type/status' is an unfiltered legacy edge case that v2 should redesign as an explicit, status-aware screening-trigger event.

RULE-SEPSE-083.source_quote: returns TrilhaSepseV3Model rows whose leito matches the 'ocupacoes__pk' URL kwarg
RULE-SEPSE-083.justification: This is a legacy Django REST viewset queryset filter tied to the retired Trilhas API URL scheme; the v2 platform will expose bed-scoped sepsis-pathway queries through its own API design, so this exact mechanism carries no forward clinical content.

RULE-SEPSE-084.source_quote: as the first element of ocupacao.trilhas whose tipo equals
RULE-SEPSE-084.justification: Pure legacy frontend UI-state selection logic (pick the first trilha with tipo==='sepse' from an array) tied to the trilhas-frontend Next.js route structure; retired with the platform change since v2 screens are built fresh per the ADR-001 stack change.

RULE-SEPSE-085.source_quote: only when BOTH its concluida (concluded) flag AND finalizado (finalized) flag are true
RULE-SEPSE-085.justification: Legacy frontend tab-state transition mechanics (move an instance from 'current' to 'historical' lists and refetch) specific to the retired trilhas-frontend page component; the underlying concluida-AND-finalizado precedence knowledge is preserved separately via RULE-SEPSE-090's ADAPT disposition.

RULE-SEPSE-086.source_quote: is fetched with an explicit filter {aceito: false}
RULE-SEPSE-086.justification: AMBIGUOUS-band escalation ESC-AMBIGUOUS-305: the meaning of filtering 'historical' pathway instances by aceito=false is unconfirmed by any comment or type definition; this is legacy frontend query plumbing, not load-bearing clinical logic, so it is retired rather than ratified given the low clinical stakes.

RULE-SEPSE-087.source_quote: navigates to the parent sector page with query parameters
RULE-SEPSE-087.justification: Pure legacy frontend back-navigation/URL-construction mechanics for a retired Next.js route; carries no clinical semantics and will be rebuilt as part of the new v2 screen navigation design.

RULE-SEPSE-088.source_quote: unless there is either a current active interactive pathway instance
RULE-SEPSE-088.justification: Legacy frontend empty-state rendering condition ('Não há protocolos de sepse para este paciente') tied to the retired page component's local state shape; v2 screens will define their own empty-state UX from scratch.

RULE-SEPSE-089.source_quote: field is truthy/non-empty; otherwise only historical tabs
RULE-SEPSE-089.justification: Legacy frontend tab-visibility guard on a specific TabPane component in the retired trilhas-frontend sepsis page; a purely presentational rule with no clinical content to carry into the redesigned v2 UI.

RULE-SEPSE-090.justification: The display precedence (concluida checked before finalizado, i.e. full completion outranks early closure/refusal for status labeling) encodes real state-machine business meaning worth preserving for the v2 bundle-compliance status display, distinct from the retired React component rendering it here.

RULE-SEPSE-091.source_quote: expandable (collapsible) only when it has a description (descricao_item)
RULE-SEPSE-091.justification: Pure legacy frontend rendering mechanics (show/hide via item.exibir, collapsible-panel heuristics) for the retired ItemProtocoloSepse component; the underlying exibir/2h-reveal concept is preserved via RULE-SEPSE-070.

RULE-SEPSE-092.source_quote: interactive only when an onCheck handler is supplied (otherwise read-only)
RULE-SEPSE-092.justification: Legacy frontend checkbox-toggle wiring (disabled/read-only when no onCheck callback, label swap Checado/Checar) specific to the retired React component tree; the actual check-off business logic lives in RULE-SEPSE-075/077, which are dispositioned separately.

RULE-SEPSE-093.source_quote: the exact distinction between the two flags is not defined
RULE-SEPSE-093.justification: AMBIGUOUS-band escalation ESC-AMBIGUOUS-306: whether 'finalizado' means any session end and 'concluida' means successful completion is only the extractor's best guess with no confirming backend logic in partition; this distinction directly affects hour-1-bundle compliance reporting and needs clinical/product ratification.

RULE-SEPSE-094.justification: Requiring a documented reason to decline activation of a life-critical sepsis protocol, gated by a dedicated 'can_recusar_protocolo_sepse' permission, is a sound clinical-governance pattern worth preserving in the v2 protocol-decline workflow even though the Django permission/type-shape mechanism itself is legacy.

RULE-SEPSE-095.source_quote: visually flagged with a red clock-alert icon
RULE-SEPSE-095.justification: P3 escalation ESC-P3-110: the backend exposes the overdue flag as atraso_item_interativa (RULE-SEPSE-069) while the frontend reads a never-populated atraso_primeira_hora, so the red delay icon never renders; v2 must adapt this by using one consistent overdue-flag field name across the hour-1-bundle timer alert.

RULE-SEPSE-096.justification: The pacote/nome_item PT-BR enum (Primeira hora/Reavaliacao packages; item labels like 'Solicitacao de exames laboratoriais e culturas para bacterias e fungos em ate 1 hora') is a controlled clinical vocabulary asset to adopt verbatim into the v2 clinical-forms bundle-item catalog.

RULE-SEPSE-097.source_quote: gating who may refuse (recusar) the sepsis protocol
RULE-SEPSE-097.justification: A dedicated, deny-by-default permission specifically for refusing a sepsis protocol is a worthwhile RBAC pattern for high-stakes clinical actions, to be re-implemented under the v2 IAM/authz model rather than the legacy Django Meta.permissions declaration used here.

RULE-SEPSE-098.source_quote: actor on a sepsis-pathway checklist item includes a
RULE-SEPSE-098.justification: AMBIGUOUS-band escalation ESC-AMBIGUOUS-338: sepsis checklist check-off actors uniquely carry a CPF field absent from equivalent checked-by shapes elsewhere in the codebase; whether v2 must require CPF-verified signatures for sepsis-bundle compliance is an LGPD/medical-legal policy decision, not one agents can infer.

RULE-SEPSE-099.source_quote: so descricao_criterio_8 always defaults to None, silently dropping the stored/displayed description
RULE-SEPSE-099.justification: ADDENDUM-band item (Phase-6 security/coverage finding) — policy mandates RATIFY; prior analysis retained: ADDENDUM escalation ESC-ADDENDUM-349: the 20-criteria manual-pathway PT-BR clinical description text (e.g. 'Temperatura > 38,3oC', the Glasgow/delirium criterio_8 text) is a valuable vocabulary asset, but the live criterio8-vs-criterio_8 key typo silently drops criterio_8's description; v2 must adopt the corrected form using the consistent criterio_N key pattern so no criterion description is silently lost.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-001.source_quote: Frontend antd number-input min/max bounds for systolic/diastolic BP and heart rate
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-001.justification: This antd input mirrors backend validators RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-018/019/027 exactly and belongs to the legacy movimentacao (nurse handoff) form, a Tasy-era UI mechanic retired under ADR-001's AMH Gold/FHIR read model; the clinical BP/HR bounds themselves survive via the backend validator dispositions carried into v2.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-002.source_quote: Frontend min/max bounds for arterial blood-gas and lab values on the movimentacao form
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-002.justification: These seven lab-input bounds duplicate backend validators RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-014/016/022/024/025/026/028 exactly; the movimentacao antd form is legacy UI plumbing retired with the platform change, while the plausibility ranges themselves carry forward via the backend validator dispositions into the sepsis domain.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-003.source_quote: Frontend min/max bounds for 24h urine output and body temperature
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-003.justification: Urine-output and temperature bounds on this legacy movimentacao form duplicate backend validators RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-021/023 exactly with no additional clinical content; the antd form itself is a Tasy-era UI mechanic that does not survive the ADR-001 platform change, so it is retired rather than carried forward.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-004.source_quote: Capillary refill time (TEC) is captured three different ways across the frontend
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-004.justification: Three incompatible encodings of capillary refill (numeric 3-20s, boolean >5s, checkbox >5s) coexist across forms, and the numeric floor of 3s excludes physiologically-normal refill against the ANDROMEDA-SHOCK <=2-3s cutoff; the mandatory P1 escalation band requires clinical ratification of a single unified representation before v2 hemodynamics can adopt it.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-005.source_quote: heart rate, respiratory rate, temperature and SpO2 are unbounded number inputs
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-005.justification: The intent to plausibility-bound every vital-sign field is sound, but the physician form's mechanism under-validates HR/FR/temp/SpO2 relative to the movimentacao form and backend validators, creating an accept-then-reject UX; v2 must enforce identical bounds on every vital-sign entry surface per constraint SINAIS-VITAIS-C-04.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-006.source_quote: uses the default SinaisVitais.objects manager (not objects_without_deleted)
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-006.justification: The listing intent is sound but the viewset queries the default manager instead of objects_without_deleted, leaking soft-deleted vital-sign rows into the list, the same bug pattern as balanco-hidrico entrada/saida; v2 must use the non-deleted manager for any vitals listing per constraint SINAIS-VITAIS-C-05, an audit/data-integrity lesson worth preserving in the security architecture.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-007.source_quote: marks it deleted by the current user and logs an AcaoHomecare audit entry
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-007.justification: The specific AcaoHomecare/Tasy audit call is legacy plumbing, but the underlying pattern of recording who deleted a clinical record and when is a deny-by-default audit-trail lesson worth keeping, distinct from the entrada/saida destroy handlers which also adjust a fluid-balance field that vitals deliberately does not touch.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-008.source_quote: Parent balanco id injected from the route kwarg
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-008.justification: This is Django viewset payload-wiring specific to the legacy balanco/sinais-vitais nested-route pattern, with no clinical content; ADR-001 constraint ADR001-C-01 removes Intensicare's own write-path ingestion in favor of AMH Gold/Athena reads, so this parent-id injection mechanic has no v2 equivalent to adapt into.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-009.source_quote: Generic percentage field validator; value must be between 0 and 100
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-009.justification: PorcentagemValidator is a generic 0-100 inclusive percentage-field bound with no zero exemption, unattached to any single vital sign; it is a reusable form-validation building block for any percentage-typed clinical field, unlike the sign-specific validators elsewhere in this cluster.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-010.source_quote: FiO2 (%) must be between 21 and 100 inclusive
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-010.justification: FiO2Validator's 21-100 range with a zero-exemption sentinel for not-measured is a well-formed, backend-confirmed bound (test_limites_parametros.py:30-36) feeding the respiratory domain's ventilator/oxygenation inputs, and the frontend movimentacao form matches it exactly.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-011.source_quote: Glasgow Coma Scale total must be between 3 and 15 inclusive
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-011.justification: The 3-15 GCS range with a zero-exemption not-measured sentinel is externally VERIFIED against the published Glasgow Coma Scale (Teasdale & Jennett 1974) and StatPearls, feeding the neuro-sedation domain's consciousness assessment inputs unchanged.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-012.source_quote: PEEP (positive end-expiratory pressure) must be between 0 and 40
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-012.justification: PeepValidator's 0-40 inclusive range with no zero exemption (0 cmH2O is a valid no-PEEP state) is confirmed by test_limites_parametros.py:38-40 and matches the frontend movimentacao PEEP input, feeding the respiratory domain's ventilator-mechanics inputs.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-013.source_quote: TEC (tempo de enchimento capilar / capillary refill time) must be between 3 and 20
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-013.justification: The intent to bound capillary-refill entry is sound, but the 3-20s permissible range excludes physiologically-normal refill (<=2-3s per ANDROMEDA-SHOCK) and is entangled with the three-way encoding dispute in RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-004; the numeric floor must be corrected to the clinical cutoff once RAT-SINAIS-VITAIS-01 fixes the unified representation.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-014.source_quote: PO2 must be between 0 and 500 inclusive
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-014.justification: Po2Validator's 0-500 inclusive range with no zero exemption is confirmed by test_limites_parametros.py:46-48 and matches the frontend movimentacao input; PaO2 feeds the respiratory domain's oxygenation and P/F-ratio inputs unchanged.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-015.source_quote: FR (respiratory rate) must be between 0 and 50 inclusive
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-015.justification: FRValidator's 0-50 inclusive range with no zero exemption (0 breaths/min represents apnea) is confirmed by test_limites_parametros.py:50-52; the movimentacao form matches it while the physician form's divergent unbounded FR is tracked separately under RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-005, so this backend bound is the correct-form target for the respiratory domain.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-016.source_quote: Arterial lactate must be between 0 and 20 inclusive
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-016.justification: LactatoArterialValidator's 0-20 inclusive plausibility range, confirmed by test_limites_parametros.py:54-56, is the collection-surface bound for the sepsis domain's serum-lactate severity input; the scoring formula itself lives outside this cluster in the sepse cluster.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-017.source_quote: Tidal volume must be between 0 and 1500 inclusive
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-017.justification: VolumeCorrenteValidator's 0-1500 inclusive range, confirmed by test_limites_parametros.py:58-60, is the plausibility bound feeding the respiratory domain's ventilator tidal-volume input, matching the frontend movimentacao input exactly.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-018.source_quote: PAS (systolic blood pressure) must be between 50 and 250 inclusive
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-018.justification: PASValidator's 50-250 inclusive range with a zero-exemption not-measured sentinel is confirmed by test_limites_parametros.py:62-64 and matches both frontend forms; it is the systolic-BP plausibility bound for the hemodynamics domain.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-019.source_quote: PAD (diastolic blood pressure) must be between 0 and 150 inclusive
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-019.justification: PADValidator's 0-150 inclusive range with no zero exemption (internally inconsistent with the paired PASValidator's exemption, recorded verbatim not corrected) is confirmed by test_limites_parametros.py:66-68 and is the diastolic-BP plausibility bound for the hemodynamics domain.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-020.source_quote: The validator class is defined but its application on the model field is COMMENTED OUT
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-020.justification: PAMValidator's 0-200 bound is dead code: the model field and its import are commented out so MAP is never stored or validated today; constraint SINAIS-VITAIS-C-01 explicitly forbids silently porting this disabled state, so whether v2 hemodynamics validates MAP at all must be ratified before any ADOPT/ADAPT decision.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-021.source_quote: 24-hour urine output must be between 0 and 10000 inclusive
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-021.justification: DebitoUrinario24hValidator's 0-10000 inclusive range with no zero exemption (0 mL/24h anuria is representable), confirmed by test_limites_parametros.py:74-76, is the plausibility bound for the AKI domain's hourly/24h urine-output input.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-022.source_quote: Bilirubin must be between 0 and 30 inclusive
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-022.justification: BilirrubinasValidator's 0-30 inclusive plausibility range, confirmed by test_limites_parametros.py:78-80, is the collection-surface bound for the sepsis domain's SOFA-hepatic input; the SOFA scoring formula itself lives outside this cluster.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-023.source_quote: Temperature must be between 20 and 43 (Celsius) inclusive
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-023.justification: TemperaturaValidator's 20-43C range with a zero-exemption not-measured sentinel, confirmed by test_limites_parametros.py:82-84 and matching the movimentacao form, is the plausibility bound for the early-warning-scores domain's core temperature vital-sign input feeding MEWS/NEWS2.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-024.source_quote: PaCO2 must be between 0 and 150 inclusive
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-024.justification: PaCO2Validator's 0-150 inclusive range, confirmed by test_limites_parametros.py:86-88 and matching the movimentacao form, is the plausibility bound feeding the respiratory domain's blood-gas input.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-025.source_quote: Creatinine must be between 0 and 20 inclusive
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-025.justification: CreatininaValidator's 0-20 inclusive plausibility range, confirmed by test_limites_parametros.py:90-92, is the collection-surface bound feeding the AKI domain's serial serum-creatinine input, the core AKI marker per the vision brief.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-026.source_quote: Leukocyte count must be between 0 and 40000 inclusive
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-026.justification: LeucocitosValidator's 0-40000 inclusive plausibility range, confirmed by test_limites_parametros.py:94-96, is the collection-surface bound for the sepsis domain's SIRS-leukocyte input; scoring logic lives outside this cluster.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-027.source_quote: Heart rate must be between 0 and 200 inclusive
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-027.justification: FrequenciaCardiacaValidator's 0-200 inclusive range with no zero exemption (0 bpm asystole/arrest is representable), confirmed by test_limites_parametros.py:106-108 and matching the movimentacao form, is the plausibility bound for the early-warning-scores domain's core heart-rate vital-sign input.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-028.source_quote: Platelet count must be between 0 and 700000 inclusive
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-028.justification: PlaquetasValidator's 0-700000 inclusive plausibility range, confirmed by test_limites_parametros.py:110-112, is the collection-surface bound feeding the sepsis domain's SOFA-coagulation platelet input.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-029.source_quote: Dobutamine dosing value must be between 0 and 30 inclusive
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-029.justification: The dose unit for this 0-30 bound is undocumented in code (ml/h per one capture, mcg/kg/min per another), so it cannot be mapped to the published dobutamine range of 2-20 mcg/kg/min; the mandatory UNVERIFIABLE escalation and constraint SINAIS-VITAIS-C-03 require an explicit unit decision before any hemodynamics dosing bound can be adopted.
evidence: Dobutamine IV infusion label (Pfizer/USP); Drugs.com dosage monograph

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-030.source_quote: Noradrenaline dosing value/quantity must be between 0 and 200 inclusive
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-030.justification: The dose unit for this 0-200 bound is undocumented (ml per capture, unstated in the validator itself), so it cannot be reconciled against the published norepinephrine range of 0.05-0.4 mcg/kg/min; the mandatory UNVERIFIABLE escalation and constraint SINAIS-VITAIS-C-03 require an explicit unit decision before v2 hemodynamics can adopt a vasopressor dosing bound.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-031.source_quote: Sedative dosing value/quantity must be between 0 and 30 inclusive
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-031.justification: 'Sedativo' aggregates an entire drug class (midazolam, propofol, dexmedetomidine, fentanyl) under one unit-less 0-30 bound with no single external dosing anchor, and the backend test name itself disagrees with the enforced bound (0-200 in the name vs 0-30 enforced); constraint SINAIS-VITAIS-C-03 requires an explicit per-drug unit decision before this dosing field can be ported to neuro-sedation.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-032.source_quote: Inspiratory pressure (PINS) must be between 0 and 30 inclusive
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-032.justification: PressaoInspiratoriaValidator's 0-30 inclusive range with no zero exemption, matching the frontend movimentacao PINS input, is the plausibility bound feeding the respiratory domain's ventilator inspiratory-pressure input.

RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-033.source_quote: SpO2/SatO2 validator requires min_value=21 to max_value=100 inclusive
RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-033.justification: SatO2Validator is both internally inconsistent (no zero exemption unlike the structurally identical FiO2Validator) and disabled on the model field, so SpO2 is effectively unvalidated today even though continuous SpO2 is a required respiratory-domain input per the vision brief; the AMBIGUOUS escalation band and constraint SINAIS-VITAIS-C-01 require ratifying whether and how v2 validates SpO2 before adoption.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-001.source_quote: computes bed occupancy percentage as (occupied beds
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-001.justification: Bed occupancy % is a hospital-ops metric tied to the legacy Estabelecimento/Setor/Leito hierarchy which ADR-001 replaces with AMH Gold's tenant/patient model; not part of any v2 clinical domain or SLO metric, so this formula is retired with the platform change rather than ported.
