RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-002.source_quote: computes bed occupancy percentage as (occupied active beds
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-002.justification: Sector-level occupancy % duplicates the same hospital-ops formula as RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-001 at a different hierarchy level; both are legacy bed-inventory bookkeeping outside IntensiCare v2's clinical-alerting scope and are retired together with the Setor entity model.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-003.source_quote: an inactive-but-occupied bed inflates occupancy above what the denominator accounts for
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-003.justification: This ativo/ocupado scoping bug (ESC-P2-072, P2) lets an inactive-but-occupied bed push occupancy above 100%, violating WHO BOR; rather than correcting it, the entire establishment bed-occupancy computation is retired since ADR-001 moves IntensiCare off owning bed-inventory entities.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-004.justification: Sector-level ativo/ocupado asymmetry (ESC-P2-073, P2) mirrors RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-003 and can push occupancy above 100%; retired alongside the establishment-level formula rather than corrected, since bed-inventory tracking is out of IntensiCare v2's AMH-Gold-consumer scope.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-005.source_quote: aggregates MacroIndicadores rows for the establishment code
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-005.justification: Escalation ESC-UNVERIFIABLE-208 mandates ratification: this proprietary macro-indicator aggregation (vidas_salvas/obitos/tempo_permanencia/admissao summed, tx_mortalidade/tx_ocupacao averaged) has no published clinical standard, so an internal reviewer must decide whether/how it migrates into v2.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-006.source_quote: A bare `except:` clause silently swallows ANY exception
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-006.justification: Escalation ESC-UNVERIFIABLE-209 mandates ratification: the sector macro-indicator fetch silently swallows all exceptions via a bare except and diverges from the establishment-level aggregate/Round implementation, so a reviewer must pick the canonical behavior before any v2 port.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-007.source_quote: sums the Firestore unread-count for every sector belonging to the establishment
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-007.justification: Escalation ESC-UNVERIFIABLE-215 mandates ratification: whether this unread-message counter should sum only the user's own sectors or the whole establishment is AMBIGUOUS scoping intent that code alone cannot resolve, so a reviewer must confirm before deciding disposition.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-008.source_quote: looks up the requesting user's unread-message counter for this specific sector
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-008.justification: Escalation ESC-UNVERIFIABLE-216 mandates ratification even though behavior is internally consistent: it shares a duplicated (not shared) Firestore-lookup helper with the ambiguous establishment-level counter (RULE-007), so its messaging/tenancy migration path must be decided jointly.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-009.source_quote: concatenates the parent establishment's name and the sector's own
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-009.justification: Escalation ESC-UNVERIFIABLE-218 mandates ratification for this pure display-string concatenation despite OK status, because it has a hard dependency on a non-null estabelecimento FK; a reviewer must decide whether this presentation rule carries into v2's UI layer at all.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-010.source_quote: rounded DOWN to the nearest multiple of 5
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-010.justification: Escalation ESC-UNVERIFIABLE-219 mandates ratification: this cache-bucketing 'last updated' floor-to-5-minutes value is proprietary UI throttling logic with no clinical anchor, and a reviewer must decide if such a display artifact belongs in v2's real-time dashboard design at all.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-011.source_quote: produces a VERMELHO/AMARELO/NEUTRO count dict by combining two sources
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-011.justification: Escalation ESC-UNVERIFIABLE-217 mandates ratification: this per-sector VERMELHO/AMARELO/NEUTRO alert tally merges manual Movimentacao counts with automatica-pathway aggregates via proprietary business logic with no published clinical scale backing it, requiring reviewer sign-off.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-012.source_quote: returns the total count of active (ativo=True) beds in the sector
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-012.justification: Escalation ESC-UNVERIFIABLE-214 mandates ratification: although internally consistent (no ativo/ocupado mismatch unlike RULE-004), this active-bed inventory count is proprietary bed-bookkeeping with no clinical calculator behind it, so a reviewer must decide its v2 disposition.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-013.source_quote: produces an M/F/N/O gender-count dict combining active (atual=True) Movimentacao
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-013.justification: Escalation ESC-UNVERIFIABLE-220 mandates ratification: this gender tally has a fragile implicit field-ordering dependency on get_alertas running first and uses a non-FHIR-conformant M/F/N/O enum, so a reviewer must decide whether/how it migrates before any port.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-014.source_quote: returns the text of instance.observacoes.first() (or empty
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-014.justification: Escalation ESC-UNVERIFIABLE-221 mandates ratification: extraction status is already AMBIGUOUS because 'latest message' selection has no explicit ordering and depends entirely on an out-of-scope model default, so a reviewer must confirm intended behavior before any v2 chat-preview design.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-015.justification: Escalation ESC-UNVERIFIABLE-210 mandates ratification: this current-calendar-month intervention count uses timezone.now() without astimezone (implicit UTC month boundaries) and has swapped inline comments, so a reviewer must confirm intended semantics before any v2 analogue is designed.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-016.source_quote: selects which pathway engine and alert logic
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-016.justification: The manual/automatica/homecare company-type enum gates a legacy multi-pathway engine-selection model (movimentacao trilhas vs automatic bed aggregates) that has no counterpart in v2's uniform FHIR-driven detector pipeline (vision.md §4.1), so this tenancy-level enum is retired, not ported.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-017.source_quote: treats the NEXT segment as the empresa pk
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-017.justification: Path-segment tenant resolution with a bare except-pass is superseded by ADR-001's mandated Lake Formation ABAC plus IAM Identity Center SSO (Implicação 7), which performs tenant/permission resolution declaratively instead of fragile URL-parsing middleware with silently swallowed errors.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: IAM Identity Center SSO + Lake Formation ABAC (ADR-001 Implicação 7)
supersedes_note.lost: custom URL-segment parsing convention and the bare except:pass silent-failure behavior are dropped; tenant resolution becomes declarative, not code-path-order-dependent

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-018.source_quote: otherwise the request is rejected with HTTP 400
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-018.justification: The empresa-vs-estabelecimento cross-tenant HTTP 400 rejection pattern is a sound deny-on-mismatch intent, but its bespoke middleware mechanism is superseded by ADR-001's Lake Formation ABAC, which enforces tenant-boundary checks centrally rather than per-middleware order-dependent code.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: Lake Formation ABAC tenant-boundary enforcement (ADR-001 Implicação 7)
supersedes_note.lost: the specific 'Este estabelecimento não pertence à esta empresa' PT-BR error-message convention and its dependency on prior middleware ordering

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-019.source_quote: validates the resolved setor's estabelecimento against
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-019.justification: SetorMiddleware's dual empresa+estabelecimento cross-check extends the same deny-on-mismatch intent one level deeper; superseded by ADR-001's Lake Formation ABAC which can express the full empresa->estabelecimento->setor hierarchy as declarative access policy instead of chained middleware.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: Lake Formation ABAC hierarchical tenant policy (ADR-001 Implicação 7)
supersedes_note.lost: the specific dual-cross-check-only-if-already-resolved behavior and its dependency on MIDDLEWARE list ordering

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-020.justification: Escalation ESC-AMBIGUOUS-340 flags this bed-resolution middleware's missing cross-tenant check as unclear intent; since the entire path-based middleware chain is superseded by Lake Formation ABAC (RULE-017/018/019), this incomplete, less-mature variant is retired outright rather than fixed.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-021.source_quote: applies BOTH an empresa filter and an estabelecimento filter
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-021.justification: This queryset-level dual empresa/estabelecimento filtering is the consumer of the middleware-attached tenant context; once tenant resolution moves to Lake Formation ABAC (RULE-017-019), row-level filtering is expressed as ABAC policy rather than cumulative Django queryset filters.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: Lake Formation ABAC row-level tenant filtering (ADR-001 Implicação 7)
supersedes_note.lost: the AND-not-elif cumulative-filter nuance and its duplicated implementation across SetorViewSet/SetorUsuarioViewSet

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-022.source_quote: filters to the empresa__pk URL kwarg when present
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-022.justification: This establishment queryset's optional nested-route scoping (with no request.user filter, a flagged multi-tenant gap) is superseded by Lake Formation ABAC, which would enforce tenant scoping uniformly regardless of whether the route happens to be nested.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: Lake Formation ABAC (ADR-001 Implicação 7)
supersedes_note.lost: none functionally lost; the previously-flagged multi-tenant gap (no request.user scoping) is closed by uniform ABAC enforcement rather than perpetuated

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-023.source_quote: briefly unmounting/remounting it via a 100ms delay
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-023.justification: This React cascading estabelecimento-then-setor picker with an un-debounced 100ms setTimeout is legacy frontend UI mechanics for the retired Estabelecimento/Setor hierarchy; IntensiCare v2's screen designs will define their own selection UX against the new patient/unit data model.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-024.justification: The intent of exempting GET reads from a manage-level permission while gating mutations is a sound method-scoped-authorization pattern worth adapting into v2's access-control design, even though the concrete Trilhas permission_trilhas/exceto_metodo mechanism itself does not exist in the new stack.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-025.source_quote: floating shortcut buttons to the Feed page and the Relatório
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-025.justification: Homecare-only dashboard shortcut buttons are legacy frontend UI mechanics keyed on the retired empresa.tipo enum (RULE-016); v2's screen designs have no homecare/manual/automatica company-type distinction, so this conditional-visibility rule is retired rather than ported.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-026.source_quote: companies of other types cannot create establishments through this UI
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-026.justification: This 'add estabelecimento' button visibility gate tied to empresa.tipo==='manual' is legacy frontend UI mechanics for the retired multi-tenant hierarchy (RULE-016); v2 has no manual/automatica/homecare company-type gating, so it is retired without replacement.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-027.source_quote: add leito" (bed) floating action button is only rendered when the current company
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-027.justification: Legacy React FAB visibility gated on empresaData.tipo is v1 UI plumbing tied to the old frontend stack; v2 will re-derive create-affordances from the new screen framework's permission/tipo model rather than porting this component logic verbatim.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: v2 clinical-forms screen conditional-rendering rules driven by tipo enum + ABAC permission, defined fresh in design/screens/clinical-forms.md
supersedes_note.lost: exact legacy FAB placement/wording; the manual-tipo-gates-creation intent is preserved

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-028.source_quote: companies with tipo === "manual" may both create new setores
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-028.justification: Client-side-only tipo-based create/edit gating for setores (no SSR permission gate per rule notes) is legacy frontend plumbing with a known authz weakness; v2 must enforce this via server-side ABAC, not port the client-only gate.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: server-enforced ABAC policy (Lake Formation, per ADR-001 Implicacao 7) gating setor create/edit by tipo, backed by SSR/API checks not just client rendering
supersedes_note.lost: the specific manual-tipo create/edit UI toggle mechanics; clinical intent (manual orgs manage setores directly) is preserved

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-029.source_quote: any other tipo delegates to Leito-level aggregate helpers
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-029.justification: Establishment-level manual-vs-automatic branch for gender/alert/assisted totals is legacy Django serializer plumbing duplicated across scopes (TENANCY-SYSTEMIC-02); v2 replaces this with a single AMH Gold-backed aggregation query, not per-scope copy-pasted branches.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: unified aggregation service reading AMH Gold observation/alert tables, single implementation not duplicated per hierarchy level
supersedes_note.lost: the tipo-branch duplication and the commented-out ocupado filter quirk (edge_cases); the underlying gender/alert/assisted count semantics are preserved

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-030.source_quote: any other tipo (homecare) uses the same leito filter but Leito.get_total_assistidos_homecare
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-030.justification: Three-way tipo branch (manual Python loop vs automatica vs homecare helper) for counting assisted patients is legacy Django plumbing duplicated with the sector-scope version (RULE-036); v2 consolidates into one AMH Gold-backed 'assistido' computation.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: unified assisted-patient count derived from AMH Gold encounter/pathway state, independent of legacy tipo Python-loop-vs-helper split
supersedes_note.lost: the manual-branch Python-level loop performance characteristic and the leftover-TODO ambiguity noted for homecare; assisted-patient semantics are preserved

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-031.source_quote: exposes login_camera and senha_camera fields only for the 'retrieve' and 'partial_update' actions
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-031.justification: Action-scoped field exposure hiding camera credentials from list/create responses is a sound least-privilege pattern worth keeping, but the DRF action_fields mechanism itself is legacy; v2 should implement equivalent per-endpoint field-level ABAC/serialization scoping natively.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-032.source_quote: prevents deletion if any of the establishment's sectors has a bed with an active
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-032.justification: Blocking establishment deletion while any bed has an active occupation is a correct safety invariant worth keeping (and correctly names the entity, unlike its sector-level sibling RULE-037); v2 re-implements the guard against AMH Gold encounter state rather than the legacy Django ORM prefetch chain.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-033.source_quote: lists only sectors the user belongs to
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-033.justification: Chats-action membership filtering via Django queryset (usuarios=request.user) is legacy tenancy-scoping plumbing tied to the old ORM model; v2 inherits equivalent scoping from Lake Formation ABAC per ADR-001 rather than porting this per-endpoint filter.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: Lake Formation ABAC row-level access policy scoping chat/sector visibility to user membership (ADR-001 Implicacao 7)
supersedes_note.lost: the specific distinct(pk)+order_by(nome) query shape; membership-scoping intent is preserved

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-034.source_quote: the 'ocupado=True' filter is present in source but commented out for the non-manual branch
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-034.justification: Sector-level gender-count tipo branch duplicates RULE-029's pattern one level down and carries the same commented-out ocupado filter inconsistency (TENANCY-SYSTEMIC-04); v2 replaces both with one aggregation implementation, correcting the filter drift rather than porting it.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: unified gender-aggregation service reading AMH Gold, applying occupancy filter consistently across all tipo branches
supersedes_note.lost: the commented-out ocupado=True inconsistency (a bug, not a feature) and the manual/automatica code duplication

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-035.source_quote: Same commented-out ocupado=True filter as RULE-setor-BE-05-008
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-035.justification: Sector-level VERMELHO/AMARELO/NEUTRO alert-count tipo branch shares the same duplication and commented-out occupancy-filter defect as RULE-034/029 (TENANCY-SYSTEMIC-04); v2's alert engine computes these counts once, consistently, from AMH Gold alert state.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: unified alert-severity count aggregation in the v2 alert engine, occupancy filter applied consistently
supersedes_note.lost: the legacy commented-out ocupado filter defect and per-scope duplicated implementation

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-036.source_quote: iterates Movimentacao records in Python calling dados_prontuario.get_assistido() per row
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-036.justification: Sector-scope assisted-patient tipo branch duplicates RULE-030's establishment-scope logic verbatim (per notes: 'Structurally identical'); consolidating into one v2 implementation avoids the Python-loop performance risk called out in edge_cases.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: unified assisted-patient count computation shared across establishment/sector scope, backed by AMH Gold, avoiding per-row Python iteration
supersedes_note.lost: the Python-level loop implementation; assisted-patient semantics preserved

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-037.source_quote: the error message text refers to 'leito' (bed) rather than 'setor' (sector)
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-037.justification: Escalation band P3 flags this deletion-guard error-message defect; the underlying delete-while-occupied safety invariant is worth keeping (ADAPT-worthy) but the exact corrected user-facing PT-BR message needs product sign-off before v2 copy is finalized, so the wording fix is ratified rather than silently corrected.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-038.source_quote: 'manual' sectors are explicitly unsupported and raise a ValidationError
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-038.justification: UNVERIFIABLE escalation band mandates ratification; this is internal proprietary cross-pathway alert-count aggregation with a latent None-arithmetic TypeError risk (edge_cases) and an uncorroborated pk==nr_atendimento assumption outside partition scope (TENANCY-SYSTEMIC-07/10), not a clinical formula despite the category tag.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-039.source_quote: revokes 'p' from the previous owner when they no longer own any company
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-039.justification: Auto-granting/revoking the 'Proprietario' access code on company-ownership save is a sound deny-by-default lifecycle pattern worth keeping, but the Django SetUpModel original_object snapshot mechanism is legacy; v2 re-implements the same lifecycle rule against IAM Identity Center group membership per ADR-001.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-040.source_quote: converts an incoming logo_b64 string into an actual file
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-040.justification: Base64-to-file conversion for company logo upload is legacy Django serializer plumbing tied to the old file-storage backend; v2's asset upload flow uses the new platform's storage/CDN mechanism, not this transform_b64_to_file helper.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-041.source_quote: an explicit TODO ('Filtrar pelo usuário que fez a requisição, baseado em vinculo')
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-041.justification: UNVERIFIABLE escalation band mandates ratification; this is pure access-scoping business logic (mislabeled 'clinical-scoring') with an author-acknowledged incompleteness (TODO for finer-grained vinculo-based filtering) that needs a product/security decision before v2 encodes equivalent ABAC scope rules.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-042.source_quote: sectors with zero currently-occupied beds are still included in the list
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-042.justification: UNVERIFIABLE escalation band mandates ratification; establishment-level instance of the same access-scoping 'indicadores' pattern as RULE-038/041, with its own commented-out occupancy filter ambiguity, needs the same product decision before v2 defines the ABAC-scoped sector-status query.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-043.source_quote: auto-refreshes on an interval equal to the company's configured tempo_atualizacao
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-043.justification: Client-side setInterval polling gated by a legacy AutoReloadContext toggle is v1 frontend plumbing; v2's real-time dashboard is expected to use push-based updates (e.g. websocket/event stream) per the platform's event-driven design, not fixed-interval polling.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: event-driven dashboard refresh (websocket/SSE push on new indicator data) replacing fixed-interval client polling
supersedes_note.lost: the tempo_atualizacao-driven interval mechanism itself; the underlying configurable-cadence intent may inform a server-side batching window

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-044.source_quote: Functionally identical to RULE-empresa-FE-08-002 but implemented independently on a different page
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-044.justification: Establishment-dashboard duplicate of RULE-043's polling mechanism; both are legacy client-side interval polling to be replaced by the same v2 event-driven refresh mechanism, consolidating the duplicated implementation noted in the rule's own notes.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: the same event-driven dashboard refresh mechanism as RULE-043, eliminating the duplicated per-page polling implementation
supersedes_note.lost: the separate establishment-dashboard polling code path

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-045.source_quote: one and only one of empresa, estabelecimento, or setor may be set
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-045.justification: The exactly-one-scope XOR invariant for GrupoAcesso is a sound, verified (per notes: 'Verified truth table') validation rule worth keeping; v2 re-implements the same exclusivity constraint against the new ABAC scope model rather than the legacy three_xor Python helper.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-046.source_quote: scope their server-side results to the target grupo id
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-046.justification: Client-side autocomplete query-parameter scoping for grupo/sector member search is legacy React component plumbing (getSetores/getAllUsuariosByGroup); v2's equivalent admin UI issues scoped queries against the new ABAC-aware access-management API.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: v2 access-management screen querying the new ABAC-scoped member-search endpoint
supersedes_note.lost: exact legacy getSetores/getAllUsuariosByGroup call shape; the grupo/estabelecimento-scoped candidate-filtering intent is preserved

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-047.source_quote: Company whitelabel/brand identifier, unique across all companies
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-047.justification: The controlled whitelabel enum (americashealth|ficticia|homecare|outras) with DB-level uniqueness is a simple, verified vocabulary/constraint asset with no legacy-plumbing entanglement; v2's tenant-config model can adopt the same enum and uniqueness invariant directly.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-048.justification: Field-level constraints are OK/VERIFIED-shaped facts, but status echo is OK while the hex-color validation is admittedly incomplete (edge_cases: 'no # or hex-format check'); the reference-correct v2 form adds actual hex-format validation, not just length-6, before carrying the constraint forward.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-049.source_quote: silently discarded from the payload entirely with no error and no effect
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-049.justification: Escalation band P3 flags this silent-data-loss defect (non-string logo values dropped without client feedback); DISCREPANCY status forbids plain ADOPT, and whether v2 should surface a 400 error or coerce the value is a product decision, so it is ratified rather than silently fixed.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-050.justification: Rejecting requests whose URL path contains the literal string 'undefined' is a cheap, sound defensive input-hygiene guard against a common frontend bug class, worth keeping; v2 re-implements it as generic API-gateway/middleware input validation rather than the legacy Django UndefinedMiddleware.

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-051.source_quote: restricted to records whose sector's establishment belongs to the requesting user's company
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-051.justification: Company-scoped filtering of the homecare action/audit log via request.empresa Django queryset filter is legacy tenant-resolution plumbing dependent on the middleware chain being superseded; v2 enforces the same multi-tenant isolation via Lake Formation ABAC row-level policy per ADR-001.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: Lake Formation ABAC row-level access policy scoping homecare audit-log rows to the requesting tenant (ADR-001 Implicacao 7)
supersedes_note.lost: the specific request.empresa Django filter dependency on upstream middleware; the audit-log tenant-isolation guarantee is preserved

RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-052.source_quote: restrict rows to the caller's active tenant context
RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-052.justification: Indicator/inconsistent-signature viewsets filtering by request.setor.codigo/request.estabelecimento.codigo depend on the legacy middleware-injected tenant context (out of partition, per edge_cases); v2 replaces this per-viewset filter pattern with Lake Formation ABAC row-level scoping per ADR-001.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: Lake Formation ABAC row-level access policy scoping indicator/inconsistent-signature rows to the caller's tenant (ADR-001 Implicacao 7)
supersedes_note.lost: the request.setor/estabelecimento.codigo Django ORM filter dependency; the AND-scoped tenant isolation guarantee is preserved

RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-001.source_quote: leaving the v3 variants (TrilhaSedacaoV3, TrilhaEstabilidadeV3, TrilhaSepseV3, TrilhaProfilaxiaV3) canonical
RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-001.justification: The v2/v3 duplicate composition list (v3 canonical, v2 counterparts commented-out but still present) is Tasy-bound Django plumbing under the retired ETL stack; it is superseded by the vision-mandated domain-based Alert Engine (7 detectors + Correlation Engine) that routes patients into sepsis/hemodynamics/neuro-sedation domains directly from AMH Gold rather than bed-type pathway-model lists.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: v2 Alert Engine domain-based routing (7 detector boxes + Correlation Engine, vision.md §4.1) reading AMH Gold instead of Tasy-bound trilha model composition
supersedes_note.lost: bed-type-keyed static Python model-list composition; the disabled v2 pathway classes left dangling in trilha_automatica.models

RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-002.source_quote: Homecare beds evaluate exactly two pathway models — PioraClinica and Sepse
RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-002.justification: The homecare-specific two-pathway (PioraClinica + Sepse) composition and its static NEUTRO stub cards are Django bed-type routing tied to the retired Tasy-linked trilha models; what actually replaces this mechanism in v2 is the Alert Engine's own threshold/enablement resolution (tenant -> unit -> bed), architecture/alert-engine.md#threshold-config-resolution — not a vision-mandated 'care-context' concept, since vision.md §4.1 is AMH Data Platform integration and mandates no care-context/bed-type/homecare domain-enablement. The underlying intent (lighter-weight deterioration + sepsis surveillance scoped to non-ICU-bed patients) is a plausible ADAPT-worthy idea for a future care-context-scoping layer on top of that resolution mechanism, not itself a vision mandate.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: the Alert Engine's threshold/enablement resolution (tenant -> unit -> bed), architecture/alert-engine.md#threshold-config-resolution
supersedes_note.lost: static NEUTRO display-only stub cards for PrescricaoContinua/BalancoHidrico/Formulario/Ventilacao/Eficiencia

RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-003.source_quote: relies on the Trilha model's default queryset ordering (not defined in this partition)
RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-003.justification: The 'automatica' branch's reliance on implicit default queryset ordering (vs the explicit -criado_em ordering used by 'homecare') makes the selected record implementation-dependent and is flagged AMBIGUOUS by the audit; the mandatory AMBIGUOUS escalation band requires ratification of whether deterministic explicit ordering (per ledger CLU-TRILHAS-ENGINE-C04) is the intended v2 semantics before any pathway-selection logic can be adopted.

RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-004.source_quote: if the pathway is marked assistido (attended), use the ASSISTIDO style; otherwise use the pathway's alert level
RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-004.justification: This is legacy React/TSX tab-styling mechanics (statusTrilha color map, borderColor/backgroundShade wiring) tied to the trilhas-frontend component tree, not a clinical threshold computation; the v2 platform replaces it with the CONTRACTS.md alert schema's own normal|watch|urgent|critical severity scale and a new frontend design system, so no legacy styling code is carried forward.

RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-005.source_quote: offered only for pathways named exactly "Sepse" or "Profilaxia"
RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-005.justification: Hard-coding the two interactive-protocol pathways by exact string name is legacy trilhas-frontend gating tied to the retired trilha model; the underlying capability (an accept/refuse clinician workflow gated on protocol-history/creation permission) is superseded by the v2 alert response/acknowledgment workflow defined generically in the alert entry schema (response.required + ack_sla) rather than a name-matched UI card.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: generic alert response/acknowledgment fields (response, ack_sla) on every v2 alert entry, CONTRACTS.md alert schema
supersedes_note.lost: hard-coded exact-name gating restricted to only Sepse/Profilaxia; can_criar_novo_protocolo/has_historico_protocolo flag combination

RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-006.source_quote: rendered inside a pathway tab only when the occupancy's bed type is "automatica"
RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-006.justification: This is a pure legacy UI mount-gate on the leito.tipo === "automatica" string, part of the trilhas-frontend component tree being replaced wholesale; the v2 Alert Engine has no bed-type-keyed UI concept, so the gate itself has no successor artifact beyond the general response workflow already captured under RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-005's SUPERSEDE target.

RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-007.source_quote: only the user who assisted it may un-assist it. Any eligible user may check (assist); only the owner may uncheck
RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-007.justification: The ownership-gated un-assist rule (any permitted user may acknowledge, but only the original acknowledger may reverse it) is a sound audit/authorization pattern worth preserving even though the concrete mechanism (postAssistido side-effect + assistido_por.id comparison in a React checkbox) is legacy trilhas-frontend wiring; it should be re-implemented as a deny-by-default ownership check on alert acknowledgment reversal in the v2 security model.

RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-008.source_quote: a red warning "Existem itens em atraso." is displayed in the interactive protocol card header
RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-008.justification: Surfacing a boolean overdue-item warning at the pathway aggregate level is a sound clinical-operations signal (echoing the sepsis 1-hour-bundle overdue concept, RULE-sepse-FE-03-002) worth keeping, but its mechanism — a server-computed has_item_em_atraso flag consumed by a bespoke React card header — is legacy and should be reimplemented as a bundle-timer-overdue alert on the sepsis domain's response.ack_sla tracking rather than a display-only flag.

RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-009.justification: The nine trilha-to-Tasy-model bindings and their quantidade_criterios contract are wiring for the retired Tasy-direct ETL engine (novo_etl_schema); ADR-001 mandates that Intensicare read clinical data only from the AMH Gold layer via Athena and never implement its own Tasy-facing ingestion/ETL, so this per-pathway ETL contract has no place in the v2 architecture.

RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-010.source_quote: the facade __init__ binds each trilha's "_automatica" alert payload to a SPECIFIC variant
RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-010.justification: This facade module purely wires which of several legacy Python payload variants (module dict vs get_ function, v1 vs v3) backs each trilha's automatic alert, entirely internal to the retired trilha_automatica package; it has no clinical content of its own and no successor is needed once the Tasy-ETL-driven trilha engine (RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-009) is retired.

RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-011.source_quote: spawns exactly four manual care pathways — TrilhaEstabilidade, TrilhaSedacao, TrilhaSepse, TrilhaVentilacao
RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-011.justification: The clinical intent — every admission (movimentacao) should immediately enable structured surveillance across stability/sedation/sepsis/ventilation — maps directly onto four of the v2 Alert Engine's core Fase-2 domains (hemodynamics, neuro-sedation, sepsis, respiratory), but the mechanism (Django CriarTrilhas use case creating four hard-coded manual trilha rows per prontuario) is legacy and must be replaced by admission-triggered domain enablement in the new Correlation Engine.

RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-012.source_quote: re-fetches via the paciente relation with .first() and NO filter on nr_atendimento
RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-012.justification: Whether v3 pathway records are meant to be scoped per-encounter (nr_atendimento) or per-patient-lifetime cannot be determined from this partition and directly changes whether the missing nr_atendimento filter in the bed re-linking loop is a stale-record bug or intended behavior; the mandatory AMBIGUOUS escalation band and ledger constraint CLU-TRILHAS-ENGINE-C03 both require ratification before any encounter-scoping design can be adopted for v2.

RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-013.source_quote: splitting it at a FIXED 6-character boundary, capitalizing each part
RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-013.justification: Although flagged AMBIGUOUS (the hard-coded 6-char 'trilha' prefix assumption is fragile against future slug variants), this display-string humanization utility encodes no clinical content whatsoever — per the AMBIGUOUS-band policy of ratify-if-clinically-worth-keeping-else-retire, this pure frontend string-formatting hack is retired rather than ratified, since v2 pathway/domain names will use an explicit human-readable label field instead of positional substring splitting.

RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-014.source_quote: Accept button is disabled once a protocol instance already exists
RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-014.justification: The accept-protocol POST/navigate workflow, including its recorded routing bug (a stray literal '}' appended to the protocol URL), is legacy trilhas-frontend wiring for the same interactive-protocol concept gated in RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-005; it is superseded by the v2 alert acknowledgment workflow (response.required + ack_sla) rather than being ported with its known bug.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: generic v2 alert acknowledgment workflow (response.required, ack_sla), CONTRACTS.md alert schema
supersedes_note.lost: trilha_interativa!==null disable/label-switch mechanics and the buggy goToProtocolos routing template literal

RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-015.source_quote: aceito is submitted as the STRING "false" on refuse, whereas accept
RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-015.justification: The refuse workflow's required-justification requirement is clinically sound, but its aceito field is submitted as the string "false" while the sibling accept workflow (RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-014) submits boolean true — an unresolved API-contract type inconsistency flagged AMBIGUOUS and called out in ledger constraint CLU-TRILHAS-ENGINE-C06, requiring ratification of a single boolean-typed contract before the v2 protocol-response API can be designed.

RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-016.source_quote: lists its recommendations followed by its interventions
RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-016.justification: The structured content pattern — per-alert-criterion recommendations followed by interventions, with a fallback 'no recommendations' message — is a sound clinical-communication design worth preserving even though the concrete mechanism (bespoke React list concatenation in TabRecomendacoes.tsx) is legacy trilhas-frontend and must be reimplemented against the v2 alert/recommendation content model.

RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-017.source_quote: the actual dose values are embedded in an opaque image asset
RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-017.justification: This is categorized drug-dosing and thus mandatorily verify:true, but the numeric doses live only inside an opaque image asset and are not machine-readable in this partition, producing the mandatory UNVERIFIABLE escalation band; it cannot be adopted, adapted, or retired without ratifying an authoritative machine-readable dosing source to replace the legacy image-modal mechanism.

RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-018.justification: The 12-slug controlled vocabulary (sedacao/estabilizacao/ventilacao/sepse/antimicrobiano/nutricao/equilibrio/profilaxias/piora_clinica/zero_glosa/auditoria/eficiencia) is a genuine clinical-domain asset worth adopting verbatim, but its DISCREPANCY status (two drifted label copies — 'Sepse' vs 'SEPSE', 'Piora Clínica' missing its accent in the second copy) means it must be corrected to a single canonical source of truth using the accented, title-cased labels (AssistidoChoices.tipo form) rather than ported as-is, per ledger constraint CLU-TRILHAS-ENGINE-C05.

RULE-VENTILACAO-001.justification: PBW formula (male 50 + 0.91*(h-152.4); female 45.5 + 0.91*(h-152.4)) and 4/5/6 mL/kg lung-protective targets match ARDSNet/ARMA exactly, so this calculator is carried forward verbatim into the respiratory domain's tidal-volume calculator.

RULE-VENTILACAO-002.source_quote: Number of days a patient has been on mechanical ventilation
RULE-VENTILACAO-002.justification: This internal days-on-VM duration helper feeds four downstream criteria (C4/C5/C6/C8) but carries an UNVERIFIABLE verdict (no published clinical anchor for a raw duration count) plus documented .days-truncation and abs()-on-future-timestamps quirks, so the mandatory UNVERIFIABLE escalation band requires ratification of whether the truncation/sign-handling behavior should be corrected before v2 adopts it as the shared duration primitive.

RULE-VENTILACAO-003.source_quote: Flags inspiratory pressure > 16 OR tidal volume > 500 ml
RULE-VENTILACAO-003.justification: C1's absolute VT>500mL threshold contradicts the correct weight-indexed PBW calculator (RULE-VENTILACAO-001) and the ARDSNet <=6 mL/kg-PBW standard, and it is a hard alert-forcing criterion feeding the pathway's VERMELHO override, so the mandatory P1 escalation band requires clinical ratification of the corrected weight-indexed threshold before v2 respiratory alerting can adopt it.

RULE-VENTILACAO-004.source_quote: the peep>0 requirement is dead (inside `False and ...`)
RULE-VENTILACAO-004.justification: C2's guard silently drops the PEEP>0 requirement via a malformed boolean expression and treats fio2 as a percentage while sibling criterion C8 (RULE-VENTILACAO-011) treats fio2 as a fraction, a P1 unit-inconsistency affecting a moderate-hypoxemia PEEP/FiO2 mismatch alert, so ratification must fix both the guard and the unit convention before v2 adopts this table.

RULE-VENTILACAO-005.source_quote: Flags a PEEP not matching the (different, severe) FiO2->PEEP table
RULE-VENTILACAO-005.justification: C3's severe-hypoxemia PEEP/FiO2 mismatch check shares the same fio2/100-as-percentage unit assumption as C2 (RULE-VENTILACAO-004) which clashes with C8's fio2-as-fraction convention, a P1 unit-inconsistency across the same clinical model that must be ratified into one canonical FiO2 unit before v2 adopts the severe-hypoxemia table.
evidence: ARDSNet Higher-PEEP/Lower-FiO2 titration table (ALVEOLI, ARMA NEJM 2000)

RULE-VENTILACAO-006.source_quote: Defined in both criterio_2 and criterio_3 but never read (dead code)
RULE-VENTILACAO-006.justification: peep_old_table is a superseded, never-read single-expected-PEEP-per-FiO2 variant sitting dead inside the criterio_2/criterio_3 methods; its successor (peep_new_table, captured under RULE-VENTILACAO-004/005) is already queued for P1 ratification, so this inert prior-version artifact is retired rather than ported.

RULE-VENTILACAO-007.source_quote: long VM with good oxygenation on controlled mode without sedation
RULE-VENTILACAO-007.justification: C4's RASS>=-2 / Glasgow>8 weaning-readiness disjuncts are clinically sound and published-anchored, but the controlled-mode conjunct compares the dispositivo field (tot/tqt/dni) against modalidade values (PCV/VCV), making that branch unreachable outside tests; v2 keeps the same three-way readiness semantics but corrects the mechanism to compare against the modalidade field.

RULE-VENTILACAO-008.source_quote: Flags more than 10 days of mechanical ventilation via orotracheal tube
RULE-VENTILACAO-008.justification: C5's >10-day orotracheal-intubation trigger for tracheostomy workup matches contemporary tracheostomy-timing evidence and is carried forward unchanged into the respiratory domain's prolonged-intubation criterion.
evidence: Contemporary tracheostomy-timing evidence, post-TracMan reassessment, JALH 2025

RULE-VENTILACAO-009.source_quote: Flags TOT ventilation > 10 days AND COVID-19 diagnosis
RULE-VENTILACAO-009.justification: C6's COVID-tracheostomy timing (>10 days) directly contradicts both the sibling facade protocol's >14-day threshold (RULE-VENTILACAO-017) and the AAO-HNS/international COVID tracheostomy guidance recommending deferral to >=14 days, a mandatory P1 cross-pathway contradiction that must be ratified to a single value before v2 respiratory logic can adopt a COVID-specific tracheostomy trigger.

RULE-VENTILACAO-010.source_quote: Flags P/F<150 AND noradrenaline dose<25 AND admission length>7 days
RULE-VENTILACAO-010.justification: C7's combination of severe hypoxemia (P/F<150), low-dose vasopressor support, and prolonged admission is a verified PROSEVA-anchored severe-ARDS-with-shock pattern spanning both respiratory and hemodynamic status, so it is adopted into the correlation engine's respiratory-hemodynamic cross-domain check named in the vision.
evidence: PROSEVA trial, Guerin et al., NEJM 2013;368:2159

RULE-VENTILACAO-011.source_quote: fio2 < 0.4 treats FiO2 as a fraction here
RULE-VENTILACAO-011.justification: C8's extubation-readiness bundle is a hard alert-forcing criterion whose fio2<0.4 fraction convention directly clashes with C2/C3's fio2/100 percentage convention on the same model, a mandatory P0 escalation because a unit mismatch in a hard-alert extubation-readiness rule can silently mis-fire or mis-suppress a safety-critical alert; ratification must fix the canonical FiO2 unit before v2 adopts this bundle.
evidence: Boles J-M et al., Eur Respir J 2007;29:1033-1056

RULE-VENTILACAO-012.source_quote: Strict lactato>2.5 (note estabilidade/sepse use >=2.5; ventilacao uses >2.5)
RULE-VENTILACAO-012.justification: C9's shock-without-ventilation composite (noradrenaline present + elevated lactate + no VM) is a hard alert-forcing criterion whose lactate>2.5 mmol/L cutoff deviates from the Sepsis-3 septic-shock definition of lactate>2 mmol/L; v2's correlation-engine check adopts the corrected >2 mmol/L Sepsis-3 boundary while keeping the same three-part composite logic.

RULE-VENTILACAO-013.source_quote: COPD lowers the SpO2 target to >92
RULE-VENTILACAO-013.justification: C10's oxygenation-adequacy check, including the COPD-specific lowered SpO2 target, matches the BTS 2017 oxygen guideline's 94-98% general / 88-92% hypercapnic-risk targets and is carried forward unchanged into the respiratory domain's oxygenation-target logic.
evidence: O'Driscoll BR et al., BTS Guideline, Thorax 2017;72(Suppl 1):ii1-ii90

RULE-VENTILACAO-014.source_quote: VERMELHO if >=3 criteria OR any of C1/C8/C9 is set
RULE-VENTILACAO-014.justification: The trilha_manual count-plus-hard-override aggregation (>=3 true criteria escalate, OR any of the three alert-forcing criteria C1/C8/C9 force escalation regardless of count) is the most complete of the three competing legacy alert-aggregation variants and its clinical intent (severity by count with critical-criterion override) is preserved, but the three-state VERMELHO/AMARELO/NEUTRO mechanism is replaced by v2's normal/watch/urgent/critical severity scale per the alert entry schema.

RULE-VENTILACAO-015.justification: This trilha_automatica v1 alert is a separate active pathway variant using only a subset of criteria (C2/C5/C7/C10) with a count(True)-only boolean match against IntegerField criteria that can silently under-match integer flags; it is superseded by the single unified v2 respiratory alert definition that consolidates all three legacy ventilation pathways rather than porting a second, narrower aggregation convention.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: unified v2 respiratory-domain alert consolidating trilha_manual (RULE-VENTILACAO-014), trilha_automatica v1 (this rule), and the facade protocol (RULE-VENTILACAO-017) into one alert definition per the CONTRACTS alert schema
supersedes_note.lost: the criterio_2/5/7/10-only subset and its count(True) boolean-only matching against IntegerField criteria

RULE-VENTILACAO-016.source_quote: Legacy ventilation alert defined but NOT called by save(); retained on class
RULE-VENTILACAO-016.justification: This is confirmed dead code (not invoked by trilha_automatica's save() path, which calls calcular_alerta_v2/RULE-VENTILACAO-015 instead) and its logic is a redundant near-duplicate of the trilha_manual alert already carried forward via RULE-VENTILACAO-014's ADAPT, so despite the AMBIGUOUS escalation band it is not worth a separate ratification and is retired as unreachable legacy code.

RULE-VENTILACAO-017.source_quote: lung-protective ventilation, PEEP/FiO2 table titration, spontaneous-breathing readiness, tracheostomy timing, PRONA indication
RULE-VENTILACAO-017.justification: This composite weaning/PEEP-titration/PRONA/tracheostomy protocol facade is VERIFIED against six independent published anchors (ARDSNet, PROSEVA, ERS/ATS weaning consensus, BTS 2017, tracheostomy-timing literature, high-risk-extubation VNI) and is adopted wholesale as the respiratory domain's weaning and lung-protective-ventilation protocol, superseding the need to separately port each C1-C10 criterion's narrower mechanism.
evidence: PROSEVA, Guerin C et al. NEJM 2013;368:2159-68

RULE-VENTILACAO-018.justification: This movimentacao-form input-plausibility bound agrees with the physician form (RULE-VENTILACAO-019) but its PEEP 0-40/PINS 0-30 ceiling diverges from the physiotherapist and homecare validators' PEEP 5-18/PINS 5-40 (RULE-VENTILACAO-020/022/023); v2 adopts a single reconciled bound (PEEP 0-24 cmH2O matching the ARDSNet PEEP/FiO2 table ceiling used in RULE-VENTILACAO-004/005, PINS/plateau 0-30 cmH2O matching the Amato driving-pressure/plateau limit already cited in RULE-VENTILACAO-003) instead of four divergent per-form ranges.
evidence: Amato MB et al., NEJM 2015;372:747 (driving/plateau pressure <=30 cmH2O)

RULE-VENTILACAO-019.justification: The physician form's conditional reveal of invasive-VM parameters vs supplemental-O2 flow is sound UX logic sharing the same VM-parameter ranges as RULE-VENTILACAO-018 (agreeing) and the same 1-15 L/min O2-flow bound as RULE-VENTILACAO-021 (agreeing), but its tipo_ventilacao value/label pairing (ventilacao_ar_ambiente value labeled "Espontanea") is semantic drift; v2 keeps the PT-BR labels verbatim while reconciling the shared parameter bounds per RULE-VENTILACAO-018's target.

RULE-VENTILACAO-020.source_quote: PEEP bounded 5-18 here vs 0-40 on movimentacao/physician forms
RULE-VENTILACAO-020.justification: The physiotherapy form's tipo_ventilacao decision tree and its rich free-text weaning-predictor fields (Tobin index, IWI, cuff leak) are sound clinical documentation, but its PEEP 5-18/PINS 5-40 bounds diverge from the movimentacao/physician forms' PEEP 0-40/PINS 0-30 (RULE-VENTILACAO-018/019); v2 reconciles all four forms onto the single PEEP 0-24/PINS 0-30 bound defined at RULE-VENTILACAO-018's target instead of preserving the P3-flagged divergence.

RULE-VENTILACAO-021.source_quote: Supplemental oxygen flow is constrained to 1..15 L/min
RULE-VENTILACAO-021.justification: The homecare supplemental-O2 flow bound of 1-15 L/min agrees exactly with the physician form's fluxo_o2 field (RULE-VENTILACAO-019), so this uncontested input-plausibility bound is carried forward unchanged into v2's clinical forms.

RULE-VENTILACAO-022.source_quote: Positive end-expiratory pressure is constrained to 5..18
RULE-VENTILACAO-022.justification: The homecare backend's PEEP 5-18 cmH2O bound agrees with the physiotherapist frontend form (RULE-VENTILACAO-020) but diverges from the movimentacao/physician forms' PEEP 0-40 (RULE-VENTILACAO-018/019); it is reconciled into the same single PEEP 0-24 cmH2O bound defined at RULE-VENTILACAO-018's target rather than kept as a third divergent range.

RULE-VENTILACAO-023.justification: The homecare backend's inspiratory-pressure 5-40 cmH2O bound agrees with the physiotherapist frontend form (RULE-VENTILACAO-020) but diverges from the movimentacao/physician forms' PINS 0-30 (RULE-VENTILACAO-018/019); it is reconciled into the Amato-anchored PINS/plateau 0-30 cmH2O bound defined at RULE-VENTILACAO-018's target rather than kept as a third divergent range that permits an unsafe 40 cmH2O ceiling.

RULE-VENTILACAO-024.source_quote: Lists contain duplicates and misspellings matched VERBATIM
RULE-VENTILACAO-024.justification: The free-text ventilation-category matcher's exact-string membership preserves misspellings and misroutes verbatim (e.g. 'vni/ mascara facial' and 've pela tqt por mascara' misclassified as spontaneous breathing), a P3-flagged data-quality defect; v2 supersedes this free-text fuzzy matching entirely with the structured tipo_ventilacao enum already defined in RULE-VENTILACAO-019/026, losing backward-compatibility with historical free-text records which instead require a one-time migration rather than live matching logic.

supersedes_note.replaced_by: structured tipo_ventilacao enum entry at data-capture time (RULE-VENTILACAO-019/026), design/screens/clinical-forms.md#ventilation-type-selector
supersedes_note.lost: live free-text reclassification of legacy misspelled/misrouted values (e.g. 'espontnaea', 'vni/ mascara facial'); historical records need a one-time backfill instead

RULE-VENTILACAO-025.source_quote: Enumerations for ventilation support type, airway device, and ventilation modality
RULE-VENTILACAO-025.justification: The VentilacaoMecanica ventilacao/dispositivo/modalidade enumerations are a sound controlled vocabulary preserved verbatim, but the vm_invasiva value is paired with the nasal-cannula label 'Cateter Nasal de O2' and C4 (RULE-VENTILACAO-007) compares dispositivo against uppercase PCV/VCV values that only exist in modalidade, making that branch unreachable; v2 keeps the PT-BR enum values/labels but corrects the label mismatch and harmonizes the C4 comparison against the modalidade field.

RULE-VENTILACAO-026.justification: The nursing ventilation/secretion assessment enums (secrecao_traqueal, tosse, ausculta, aspecto_do_curativo flogistic-signs) are an uncontested PT-BR controlled vocabulary asset with no reported discrepancy, so they are carried forward verbatim into v2's respiratory clinical-forms documentation.
